title: Game Identity & Vision
subtitle: Genre Fusion · Elevator Pitch · Core Pillars — Chapter 1
sections: Every Design Decision Must Serve At Least One
content: GAME IDENTITY & VISION Genre Fusion · Elevator Pitch · Core Pillars — Chapter 1 ELEVATOR PITCH You are a weapon. A freshly decanted clone — or something stranger — created by a fallen civilization to win a war that already destroyed them once. Prove your genius across simulated campaigns. When judged ready, wake into the real galaxy — devoured from outside by an adaptive swarm, guarded at its core by ancient energy gods. Your simulated victories shaped the galaxy you now face. If you die here, it is permanent. Genre Fusion — Four Pillars 4X · Auto-Battler · Rogue-Lite · Deep Narrative 4X GRAND STRATEGY Macro-level galactic conquest: explore, expand, exploit, exterminate across procedural star systems. Dozens of cycles. Infrastructure, diplomacy, logistics. Emperor managing a galaxy-spanning civilization. RTS AUTO-BATTLER Micro-level combat on SC2-scale maps. Strategic decisions already made (composition, equipment, deployment). Player acts as general: CP abilities, reserves, observation. Units fight autonomously. ROGUE-LITE META-PROGRESSION Fresh galaxy per run. Death resets in-run progress. Blueprints, resources, lore persist across runs through multi-layered Archive. Veteran players always feel experience translating into power. DEEP NARRATIVE LAYERS You are a specific entity — clone, AI, psychic echo, construct, or reactivated core. Three named advisors with their own agendas. Procedural memories. A choice about what kind of leader you become. THE EXPERIENCE STATEMENT Hour 1 — Captain: Personally selecting every unit's equipment. Agonizing over deployment. Celebrating individual squad survival. Hour 10 — Admiral: Multiple fronts. Delegating skirmishes to Commanders. Named veterans earning General rank. Choosing which front gets reinforced. Hour 20 — Emperor: Managing autonomous Generals. Alliance politics. After-action reports from battles never witnessed. Preparing for the final push to the Core. Emotional arc: learning → mastery → delegation → trust → consequence. Core Pillars Every Design Decision Must Serve At Least One PILLAR 1: Strategic Depth Without Micromanagement Units fight autonomously. Player decides composition, equipment, positioning. The 80% pre-battle / 20% intervention split. PILLAR 2: Characters, Not Numbers Units gain names, traits, rivalries, loyalty. Generals have personalities that affect autonomous behavior. Advisors are real people. PILLAR 3: The Galaxy Is Alive Vorax consume from the edges. Guardians wait at the Core. AI factions pursue their own agendas. Dynamic hazards reshape the map. Nothing waits for the player. PILLAR 4: Choices Have Weight Alignment tracks every decision. Generals remember treatment. Betrayals have consequences. The Final War galaxy is shaped by cumulative simulation choices. Visual & Tonal References Precise Design Targets StarCraft II — Visual readability benchmark. Triple-redundant identification: faction color + unique silhouette + colored health bars. Dawn of War: Dark Crusade — Territory conquest. Every territory is a PLACE, not a hex. Grimdark atmosphere. The weight of war. Command & Conquer 3 — Diegetic UI. The interface IS a military command console. Faction-specific UI skins. Holographic projection aesthetic. Warhammer 40K — Faction identity through silhouette. Every faction recognizable from black-and-white screenshots of unit outlines alone.

content: THE FIVE-PHASE GAMEPLAY LOOP Map → Armada → Approach → War → Resolution — Chapter 2 Loop Overview Five Phases, One Core Loop 1. MAP PHASE Macro strategy. Build, expand, manage resources, move fleets, assign Generals. Paradox-style pause. 4 speed settings. 2. ARMADA PHASE Fleet logistics sub-mode. Assemble invasion force, load troops, customize equipment. Overlays on Map Phase. 3. APPROACH PHASE The decision point. Fleet arrives at target. Choose: Espionage (intel), Diplomacy (negotiate), or War (attack). 4. WAR PHASE Auto-battle. Space combat (formations, CP), then ground combat (deployment, cover, garrison). 5–25 min per battle. 5. RESOLUTION All simultaneous actions resolve. Territory changes. Casualties tallied. XP awarded. Cycle ends. Return to Map Phase. Map Phase Details The Game's Home State SIMULTANEOUS TURNS All factions act simultaneously. Conflicts trigger interrupts. Paradox-style pause freezes EVERYTHING — plan in peace, execute in real-time. 4 speed settings: 1×, 2×, 4×, Pause. OPERATIONS = GENERALS Operations limited by General count. No General assigned = operation can't proceed past planning. Promote more Generals = run more simultaneous fronts. The core bottleneck. §2.3.6 — MVP Armada PROTOTYPE LOCKED Starting Fleet Composition DETAIL VALUE NOTES Starting Fleet 2× Frigates Terran — persistent full run HP Restore Between Battles NO HP not restored between battles Ship Destruction Spliced from array Permanently gone when destroyed Recruitment Armada Phase UI Logistics Hub required for Armada access §2.4.5 — Approach Phase Costs PROTOTYPE LOCKED Three Options at Every System — Confirmed Resource Costs CHOICE COST DETAILS Espionage — Deep Recon 0 Intel Hold 1 cycle. title: Narrative Arc — "The Last Decant"
subtitle: "The Last Decant" — Simulation → Reality → The Core — Chapter 3
sections: The Aethyn — Progenitors of All Factions

content: THE NARRATIVE ARC "The Last Decant" — Simulation → Reality → The Core — Chapter 3 The Original Civilization The Aethyn — Progenitors of All Factions THE AETHYN A single unified species — psychically connected as one mind, one signal. Galaxy-spanning utopia at their peak, with intergalactic warp lanes connecting to other galaxies. Technology was psychic-crystalline hybrid — mental energy channeled through crystal matrices. Mind = software, crystals = hardware. They manufactured entire galaxies, built Dyson spheres routinely, and existed tens of thousands of years ago — so far back they're not even myth to modern factions. The modern factions are children playing in the ruins of gods. WARRIOR CASTE Protectors, soldiers, tacticians → Scrap-Horde ENGINEER CASTE Builders, architects, infrastructure → Terran League SCHOLAR CASTE Knowledge keepers, psychic scientists → Eternal Shards SHEPHERD CASTE Leaders, mediators, diplomats → Unity Accord THE REVENANT — Landed near intact Aethyn AI infrastructure. Not descended from a specific caste — adopted by machines. The Cataclysm The Fracture — The Desperate Choice THE VORAX CRISIS The Aethyn's unified psychic signal acted as a beacon — a lighthouse burning across the void. It attracted the Vorax , extragalactic predators that had always existed, always hungered, drawn irresistibly to psychic civilizations. They targeted this galaxy and cut the warp lanes — sealing every exit. The Aethyn in this galaxy were completely isolated from the greater civilization. No reinforcements. No escape. No contact with the thousands of other galaxies they had once called home. A centuries-long war followed. The Aethyn fought hard — a civilization that manufactured galaxies turned its full might against the invaders — but they were losing. Slowly. Inevitably. The Vorax consumed everything. THE DESPERATE CHOICE The entire species chose. All castes. All minds. Billions voting yes to their own destruction. The last act of unity was choosing to end unity. Full knowledge. They knew exactly what the Reclamation Engine would do. Every mind understood. Every caste agreed. Eyes open. No deception, no coercion — the most democratic act in galactic history was a vote for annihilation. The Engine had been carefully designed over decades — not a rushed prototype born of panic, but a deliberate instrument of last resort. They had time to build it right. They just wished they never had to use it. THE RECLAMATION ENGINE True name: The Reclamation Engine — only discovered through gameplay. Known as "The Core" until its true name is revealed. Located inside the Nexus system ( sys-nexus ). The Nexus is the location; the Reclamation Engine is the device within it. Physically housed on "The Throne" — Core system planet 10. Dual effect: shattered the Aethyn into scattered seeds AND purged all Vorax from the galaxy simultaneously. A wave of energy blasted from the Core — ripping apart everything at the molecular level. It traveled outward in all directions, through every system, every arm, every void between stars. One wave. One moment. Everything changed. THE AFTERMATH Vorax: Erased completely. Zero traces. Zero evidence they ever existed. Wiped so thoroughly that no trace or myth remains. Aethyn: Shattered but not destroyed — scattered as seeds to distant systems. Fragments of a god-species, carrying broken genes and fading echoes of what they once were. Technology: Mostly destroyed. Surviving pieces became the relics — crystalline structures that still respond to psychic proximity, sleeping and waiting. Warp Lanes: Burned out. Overloaded by the energy surge. The highways between galaxies — gone. The Galaxy: Scarred. Warped regions, dead zones, anomalous nebulae — the wounds of a weapon that saved everything by destroying everything. # ALIGNMENT CORE OUTCOME RESULT 1 Architect The Ordered Galaxy Cooperative resonance imposed. Safe but freedom sacrificed. 2 Vanguard The Scorched Galaxy Maximum power. Vorax annihilated but galaxy devastated. 3 Tyrant The Iron Throne Commander seizes power. Vorax subjugated. Galactic deity. 4 Any Alignment Pyrrhic Victory Win at catastrophic cost. Galaxy saved. Army gone. Rebuilt from nothing. 5 Unifier (Secret) The Resonance Species-split reversed. Galaxy healed. Vorax sealed. Warp lanes reopened. The Aftermath The Scattering — Seeds Among the Stars HOW FRAGMENTS LANDED During the centuries-long war, Aethyn populations were already distributed across the galaxy — deployed, displaced, fighting on a thousand fronts. The wave hit them where they already were . Randomness determined who survived. A cosmic lottery. Some fragments landed on habitable worlds. Some didn't. The five factions are the ones that survived — countless others were not so fortunate. Proximity to intact Aethyn infrastructure determined the head start. Groups near shielded ruins had materials, shelter, maybe functioning tech. Groups on barren worlds started with almost nothing. Groups near crystal deposits retained more psychic residue. Groups near AI facilities got adopted by machines. UNEQUAL DISTRIBUTION — WHAT EACH FACTION RETAINED ETERNAL SHARDS Retained most psychic ability . Scholar Caste descendants landed near crystal deposits, preserving the strongest connection to the old resonance. Active psychic abilities, crystal resonance, can sense echoes of what was. THE REVENANT Retained nothing biological , but landed near intact Aethyn AI infrastructure. Not descended from a specific caste — adopted by machines. The AI recognized descendant DNA and began rebuilding. TERRAN LEAGUE Got almost nothing . Near-clean slate. Engineer Caste descendants who rebuilt through sheer determination and ingenuity. What they lack in inheritance they make up for in stubborn refusal to stay broken. SCRAP-HORDE Got numbers but nothing else . Warrior Caste descendants dumped in a hostile environment. Survived through aggression, resourcefulness, and an instinct for violence that runs deeper than memory. UNITY ACCORD Got moderate everything . Shepherd Caste descendants — enough fragments landed in proximity to find each other. Their instinct for unity persisted even when the memory of unity did not. FACTION ATTUNEMENT MANIFESTATION Eternal Shards Strongest Active psychic abilities, crystal resonance, can sense echoes Unity Accord Moderate Subconscious pull toward cooperation Terran League Low Occasional intuition, déjà vu near relics Scrap-Horde Low / Erratic Flashes of ingenuity when handling Aethyn tech The Revenant Unique Not psychic, but their Aethyn-era machines respond to descendant DNA RECOVERY TIMELINE Recovery varies wildly by faction. Factions near Aethyn ruins leapfrogged through reverse-engineering. Some have been spacefaring for millennia; others cracked orbit recently. This creates natural power imbalances and resentment — the seeds of every faction war in the game. THE BURIED TRUTH No faction knows they share a common ancestor. The truth is completely buried — not hidden, not suppressed, simply gone . Tens of thousands of years erased every trace. Zero memory of the Vorax. The Reclamation Engine wiped them so thoroughly that no trace, no myth, no bedtime story remains. The greatest threat the galaxy ever faced left no footprint. Every faction believes they're indigenous to their homeworld. The truth rewriting identity is a MASSIVE narrative beat — the moment the player realizes these warring peoples are family. Master Timeline Seven Galactic Eras ERA 1 — THE HARMONY Aethyn at peak. Thousands of galaxies. Unknown duration. ERA 2 — THE SIEGE Vorax find this galaxy. Warp lanes cut. Centuries of war. ERA 3 — THE FRACTURE The wave. One moment. Everything changes. ERA 4 — THE SILENCE Tens of thousands of years. Fragments rebuilding from nothing. The longest era. ERA 5 — THE REAWAKENING Factions achieve space travel. Internal civil conflicts. ERA 6 — THE CONTACT Factions encounter each other. Tensions, skirmishes, diplomacy. ERA 7 — THE RETURN Vorax appear at the edges. The Commander rises. The Reclamation begins. Two-Phase Structure The Crucible (Simulation) → The Final War (Reality) THE CRUCIBLE (SIMULATION) Standard random procedural galaxy each run. Death = blackness → re-decanting → new run. Advisors are "helpful AI voices" (or so the player assumes). No permadeath. Archive accumulates. Procedural "Artifacts" — fragments of the Original Strategist's memories. Emotionally charged but mechanically optional. Simulation is PSYCHOLOGICALLY IDENTICAL to reality. No wireframe filter, no "this isn't real" cues. First run has NO HUD watermark — completely clean. Watermark appears on subsequent runs after the reveal. THE FINAL WAR (REALITY) Procedural Destiny galaxy shaped by cumulative Alignment. Permadeath active (Standard/Hardcore). Advisors revealed as REAL PEOPLE. Faces, physical presence, personal stakes. Everything they said was sincere. Meet faction leaders in person. Hybrid cinematic → dialogue sequences. Two paths to the Core: Conquest (destroy the Guardians) or Unification (convince them). THE WAKE-UP Delivered by whichever advisor has the best relationship with the Commander (alignment-dependent — see Ch.23). Valerius is the canonical Terran/Architect version. Player CHOOSES when to wake up. Must WIN a simulation run after recommendation. Waking underprepared = brutally difficult Final War. Continuing to prepare = more Archive advantages, but the Vorax won't wait. Transition: simulation glitch cutscene → the galaxy is real → Map Phase of the Final War. THE REVEAL — FIRST PLAYTHROUGH First run has NO FRAME. Player doesn't know it's a simulation. No tutorial popup, no "Welcome to the Crucible." Just: wake up, here's a galaxy, go. Reveal happens after first complete game (win OR lose). "SIMULATION COMPLETE" moment. Everything recontextualizes. No HUD watermark on first run. Completely clean. Watermark appears on all subsequent runs. Custom Mode: Selectable difficulty tier from the start — player controls run limits, AI strength, Vorax timing, saves, and permadeath. DIFFICULTY SIM RUNS BEFORE FINAL WAR (see Ch.36 for full detail) Story ∞ runs. No permadeath. 50% AI. Vorax delayed +3 cycles. Unlimited saves. Standard ∞ runs. Permadeath in Final War only. 100% AI. Vorax cycle 6. Hardcore 5 max runs. Permadeath always. 150% AI. Vorax cycle 4. Ironman saves. Custom Player controls all parameters. Available from start. Win & Loss Conditions PROTOTYPE LOCKED Canonical Victory and Defeat States VICTORY CONDITION Conquer The Nexus (sys-nexus) Core Guardians are the final boss. Conquer 'sys-nexus' (type: 'guardian' ) → VICTORY screen. LOSS CONDITION Faction Eliminated All planets lost AND no fleets in transit → faction destroyed. Applies to all factions equally (player and AI). Homeworlds are procedurally placed — no fixed coordinates. The Original Strategist A Consciousness Born From Fragments NATURE A failsafe built into the Fracture itself — not a survivor, not an AI like the Guardians, not a person. A function of the Reclamation Engine. Built FROM Aethyn memories but isn't an actual Aethyn. Partial personality — warm moments mixed with cold, mechanical ones. The player never fully trusts whether they're connecting with a person or a program. SENTIENCE ARC — THE PLAYER BIRTHS A CONSCIOUSNESS 0 fragments: Nothing. Scattered data. No mind, no awareness. Early: Random memories surface. No voice, no intent. Pages from a burned book. Threshold 1: Patterns emerge. Code starts connecting. Memories seem related. Threshold 2: First signs of awareness. A fragment RESPONDS to what just happened. The code is waking up. Threshold 3: Direct communication begins. Halting, confused. "I... remember something. Who are you?" Being BORN in real time. Threshold 4: Functional sentience. Conversations, advice. Still has gaps — wrong fragments = flawed guidance. Full Assembly: Knows who they are, what happened, what must happen next. Advice is reliable. Name recoverable. NAME Has a name but it's corrupted across fragments. Partial syllables hinted throughout. Full name only revealed in the Unifier ending — a reward for completionists. Should feel Aethyn. Ancient. Worth the wait. RELATIONSHIP WITH COMMANDER Uncertain — hasn't existed long enough to know how they feel. Sometimes parental, sometimes cold, sometimes confused. Borrowed emotions. The bond grows naturally as the player watches this mind wake up. Aethyn Relics & Strategist Fragments Artifacts System Relics are found on planets — chance-based, density varies by proximity to old Aethyn core worlds. They provide: 🔬 Research Points (accelerate R&D), 📖 Compendium Entries (data fragments, star charts), 💬 Advisor Dialogue (each advisor reacts uniquely — 3 perspectives per find), and 🧠 Strategist Memory Fragments (the rare, important ones that build sentience). Dyson Spheres are ultra-rare (~15–25% chance per map) and function as map-defining strategic objectives. The player's Commander type changes how Artifacts are experienced — Bio Clones feel inherited memories, Psychic Echoes experience bleeding dreams, Forged Constructs hallucinate data fragments, Reactivated Cores read archaeological data, Military AIs encounter anomalous system logs. Mixed emotional valence: some warm, some devastating. Five Endings Named Outcomes by Alignment Path + Pyrrhic ARCHITECT — "THE ORDERED GALAXY" Convince the Guardians through knowledge and diplomacy. Cooperative resonance imposed on the galaxy. Safe, stable — but freedom sacrificed. The Guardians provide knowledge instead of soldiers. Aethyn archives unlocked. VANGUARD — "THE SCORCHED GALAXY" Destroy the Guardians. Take the Core by force. Maximum power — Vorax annihilated but galaxy devastated. The chain reaction of Guardian deaths reshapes the core systems. Partial Guardian restoration at best — allies on THEIR terms. TYRANT — "THE IRON THRONE" Commander seizes absolute power. Vorax subjugated, not destroyed. Galactic deity. The Guardians refuse or are corrupted — unstable, turning on your forces. The Tyrant ending is them being heartbroken, not stubborn. SECRET: UNIFIER — "THE RESONANCE" The species-split reversed. Galaxy healed. Vorax sealed permanently. Warp lanes reopened to other galaxies. Requires: balanced alignment + complete Unification negotiation + full Artifact chain. Full Guardian restoration — fight side by side. Reveals the Strategist’s true name. The golden ending. PYRRHIC VICTORY — 5TH ENDING A distinct ending, not a variant. Win the Final War at catastrophic cost — galaxy saved, army gone, rebuilt from nothing. The price of victory paid in full. Own cinematic, own emotional beat. Can result from any alignment path. The player won, but the cost makes them question if it was worth it.

title: Commander Origins
subtitle: 5 Faction-Specific Commander Types — Chapter 4
sections: Zero to Full — Mirrors the Player
content: COMMANDER ORIGINS 5 Faction-Specific Commander Types — Chapter 4 The Commander is not always a clone. Each faction creates their Commander differently — and each creation method carries unique mechanical bonuses, vulnerabilities, and narrative flavor. TERRAN LEAGUE — Biological Clone Beginner Bonus — Adaptive Learning: +5% XP gain from all sources. Learns faster from every engagement. Vulnerability — Morale Vulnerable: Heavy casualties within 30m cause -10% accuracy for 5 sec. Feels the cost of war. Artifacts: Inherited memories — experiences that feel like your own past, a life you never lived. ETERNAL SHARDS — Psychic Echo Expert Bonus — Precognition: 3-second preview of enemy deployment before auto-battle begins. Vulnerability — Psychic Feedback: Vorax Synapse death within range deals 8% HP to Commander unit. Artifacts: Bleeding dreams — the Strategist's thoughts override perception, layering past on present. SCRAP-HORDE — Forged Construct Intermediate Bonus — Regeneration: 10% max HP passive regen between battles. Silicon biology self-repairs. Vulnerability — Prototype Instability: First-of-their-kind. 5% chance per battle of random system malfunction. Artifacts: Hallucinated data fragments — the Strategist's patterns manifest as industrial noise, schematics. THE REVENANT — Reactivated Core Intermediate Bonus — Living Metal Integration: Commander unit has +20% armor. Living metal self-repairs minor damage. Vulnerability — Ancient Protocols: Occasionally overridden by pre-programmed directives. 3% chance of ignoring player CP. Artifacts: Archaeological data — the Strategist's memories read as ancient records, clinical and detached. UNITY ACCORD — Military AI (STRATEGOS-1) Advanced Bonus — Processing Speed: CP abilities recharge 15% faster. The machine thinks faster. Vulnerability — Directive Conflict: Must operate within Accord caste hierarchy. Supreme Commander can override tactical decisions. Artifacts: Anomalous data — the Strategist's emotions surface as uncategorizable system logs. "A child's laugh. Filing under: ANOMALOUS." The Aethyn Connection Bridge Between Faction & Resonance The Commander is a bridge — part faction creation, part Aethyn resonance. Every faction built their Commander for practical military reasons. They didn’t know they were building a key. All Commanders carry Aethyn DNA (all factions are descendants). The creation process activated dormant resonance. The Strategist didn’t choose this person — the signal found the first mind that could hear it. The Commander is the right frequency at the right time. Faction choice matters narratively: a Bio Clone hears the signal differently than a Military AI. Awareness of Destiny Zero to Full — Mirrors the Player Start: Zero awareness. Completely faction-loyal. Signal indistinguishable from background noise. First Relic: A flicker. Dismissed as equipment malfunction. Multiple Relics: Pattern emerges. Advisors notice before the Commander does. Mid-Game: Undeniable. Something is talking. Something is waking up. Late Game: Full Strategist communication. Commander knows they’re more than a soldier. FACTION REACTION TO STRATEGIST CONNECTION FACTION REACTION TENSION Terran League Suspicion. Internal review. “Are you compromised, Commander?” Fighting for trust while fighting the war Eternal Shards Reverence. “The crystals speak through you.” Almost messianic. Fighting against being worshipped into irrelevance Scrap-Horde Pragmatic. “Does it help us win? Keep listening.” Fine until you have a bad day The Revenant Scientific fascination. Want to study the connection. Run diagnostics. You’re a test subject who also happens to be in charge Unity Accord Committee debate. Bureaucratic but permissive — intel is valuable. Need committee approval to follow your own instincts Death & Permadeath Stakes Are Real PERMADEATH (FINAL WAR / HARDCORE) Commander dies = GAME OVER. Cutscene: galaxy without you — factions fracture, Vorax advance, Core goes dark, Strategist fades. Must start completely over — new faction, new commander. Archive meta-progression carries forward, but the PERSON is gone. SIMULATION DEATH Sim ends → results cutscene with projected galactic outcome. Strategist (if awake) processes failure, adjusts advice. Player restarts with Archive bonuses. Narratively: the Crucible stress-testing you. Death = not ready yet. Commander Personality Light Framework — Player Choices Override ORIGIN STARTING PERSONALITY Bio Clone Slightly emotional, inherited empathy, hesitates at casualties early Psychic Echo Introspective, trusts feelings over data Forged Construct Blunt, practical, doesn’t understand organic complexity Reactivated Core Detached, analytical, old protocols surface Military AI Precise, procedural, categorizes everything By mid-game, player’s CHOICES define the Commander more than origin ever did. Commander-Faction Relationship Tool → Weapon → Leader → Legend 1. Created: You’re a tool. A last-resort weapon. Respected like a gun — for what you do, not who you are. 2. Proving: Early victories earn grudging respect. Still “the construct” in internal comms. 3. Established: Mid-game, real loyalty earned. People fight FOR you. 4. True Leader: Late game, you ARE the faction. Your word is law. ALIGNMENT COLORS THE RELATIONSHIP ALIGNMENT TREATMENT RISK Architect Respected, trusted. Advisors speak freely. Slow to act — people debate orders. Vanguard Admired. War hero. Soldiers die for you willingly. Follow you into bad plans. Tyrant Feared. Obeyed instantly. Nobody questions anything to your face. Betrayal. Fear breeds resentment. Coups, sabotage, defection. Truth Revealed by Ending What the Commander Learns — Path-Dependent ENDING WHAT THE COMMANDER LEARNS Unifier EVERYTHING. Full Aethyn history, shared ancestry, Vorax origin, Strategist’s name, Engine purpose, why the Fracture happened. Architect Most of it. Ancestry confirmed, Vorax history, Strategist communicates freely. Some pieces stay locked. Vanguard Broad strokes. Shared ancestor, ancient Vorax. Details lost in the noise of war — too busy fighting to listen. Tyrant Fragments. Enough to know Engine is powerful. Uses truth as tool. Strategist grows quiet — they don’t like what they see. Pyrrhic Almost nothing. War consumed everything. Won but will never know why it needed saving. DLC Commander Equivalents Guardians & Vorax Campaigns “THE CORE AWAKENS” You ARE the Warden-Commander. Single entity, direct control. Permadeath applies. Can distribute consciousness across units (safer but slower) or concentrate (powerful but vulnerable). Personal, intimate — one mind holding everything together. “THE SWARM UNBOUND” You are the Tendril — the swarm itself. No single unit. If one Tendril dies, consciousness shifts to the next. No traditional permadeath — cost is forgetting (lost adaptations), not dying. Alien, detached — losing units = trimming fingernails. YOU are the monster.

content: TERRAN THE TERRAN LEAGUE The Last Bastion — Chapter 5 #00b4ff PLAYABLE BEGINNER BIOLOGICAL CLONE CENTRAL NARRATIVE QUESTION "Are we still human if we spend lives like currency?" AETHYN ANCESTRY — MIXED / ALL CASTES Descended from ALL Aethyn castes — the most complete echo of the original species. Where other factions specialized (Shards=Scholar, Horde=Warrior, Revenant=Engineer, Accord=Shepherd), the Terrans retained all four bloodlines in diluted form. Jack of all trades, master of none. This is WHY they feel baseline — they're the most Aethyn-complete faction. They forgot the psychic-crystalline technology and rebuilt with ballistic, mechanical, industrial means. Psychic attunement: LOW. Mixed heritage means no single strong psychic trait. They FEEL the resonance of Aethyn relics but can't activate them without help. Identity & Lore Survival at Any Cost The Terran League is humanity's last organized military force — a gritty, desperate coalition of colonial remnants, military holdouts, and civilian survivors who clung to existence after the original civilization's collapse. They are not a gleaming galactic empire. They are not an advanced post-scarcity utopia. They are soldiers, engineers, and laborers held together by stubbornness, standardized equipment, and the refusal to lie down and die. Their strength is not elegance or technological supremacy — it is ADAPTABILITY. The Terran League fields the most versatile combined-arms military in the galaxy. Their infantry can be equipped for any role. Their vehicles are modular, repairable, and mass-produced. Their ships are ugly, over-engineered, and built to survive damage that would cripple more advanced vessels. They win not through a single overwhelming advantage but through the ability to respond to ANY situation with a competent, well-supplied, well-coordinated force. Their weakness is that they are ordinary. They don't have psychic powers. They don't regenerate from death. They don't have railguns that fire at relativistic speeds. They have ballistic rifles, composite armor, and diesel engines. Against the galaxy's horrors — ancient machines, psychic warriors, silicon berserkers, precision zealots, adaptive biological swarms, and energy gods — the Terran League's greatest weapon is the same thing it has always been: the willingness of individual humans to fight, suffer, and die for the people standing next to them. Visual Identity Military Industrial Realism 023 GROUND FORCES CLASS: MEDIUM-HEAVY INFANTRY ARMOR: ABLATIVE COMPOSITE PASSIVE: FIELD REPAIR +15%HP ⚠ BATTLE-WORN — FIELD REPAIRED 12.7mm BALLISTIC BELT-FED / PHYSICAL BRASS MOVE ARMOR DMG TL//GRD-01 Ground Forces Exposed treads on heavy vehicles. Riveted ablative armor plate — thick, overlapping slabs of composite material bolted together with visible fasteners. Hydraulic exosuits that hiss and groan with servo strain. Belt-fed ballistic weapons that eject physical brass casings. Olive drab, gunmetal grey, burnt orange hazard markings. Everything scratched, dented, field-repaired. TL AIR FORCES TYPE: STRIKE FIGHTER SPD: SUPERSONIC / VTOL CAP AUDIO: HIGH-DECIBEL ROAR ALT 14,000m MACH 1.85 TL//AIR-01 Air Forces Angular, swept-wing aircraft with visible engine intakes and physical afterburner cones. VTOL craft held aloft by massive rotary fans that kick up dust storms on landing. Bombers with exposed payload bays large enough to walk through. Terran aircraft are LOUD. Engine roar, sonic booms, the whistle of dropping munitions. TL-CV-0041 SPACE FLEET CLASS: CAPITAL SHIP / CARRIER PROP: CHEMICAL THRUSTER ARRAY THRUSTER: ACCELERATING 0.4G TL//SPC-01 Space Fleet Wedge-shaped capital ships layered in modular armor blocks replaceable in drydock. Massive chemical thruster arrays that glow orange during acceleration. Macro-cannon turrets the size of buildings, visibly loading physical shells on hydraulic rails. Flight decks crawling with EVA mechanics. The fleet aesthetic is naval — warships in the Age of Sail tradition. Gameplay Primary Mechanic: Field Repairs PROTOTYPE LOCKED SUPPORT DRONES HEAL UNITS MID-BATTLE — +2% HP/sec WHEN HP < 75% The Terran League deploys automated Field Repair drones alongside their combat forces. These drones are a PASSIVE SYSTEM that activates automatically during auto-battles. When a Terran League unit drops below 75% HP, the nearest available repair drone detaches and begins healing at a rate of 2% HP per second. Visual feedback: green tint pulse on units receiving repair. Heal Rate: +2% HP/sec (passive, auto-triggers) Trigger Threshold: HP < 75% max Drone Pool: ~1 drone per 3 combat units Drone HP: 20 HP, no armor (destructible) Visual: Green tint pulse on healing unit Prioritization: Most damaged unit first Weakness: Drones can be targeted and destroyed Strategic Impact: Terran WANTS long battles Counter: Overwhelm quickly or destroy drones first Activation: Nearest available drone deploys §17.3 — MVP Ground Unit Stats PROTOTYPE LOCKED Confirmed Prototype Values — Default 6 Units Per Side Unit HP Dmg Atk Spd Move Spd Range Notes Shock-Marine 100 10 1.0/sec 120 px/sec 300 px Standard Infantry / Backbone. PRIMARY Field Repair beneficiary. STAT APPLICATION ORDER (canonical): base stats → equipment mods → supply penalty → space combat ground mod → rank mods SECONDARY BONUS 1 +15% Supply Range Speed Terran supply convoys travel 15% faster through warp lanes. Reinforcements and re-equipment reach distant fronts sooner. The League's institutional logistical competence — the most reliable supply chain in the galaxy. SECONDARY BONUS 2 Extra Equipment Slot at Veteran Rank Terran League units unlock their Veteran rank equipment slot ONE rank earlier than other factions. A Terran Veteran Light Infantry has [W] [A] [G] (3 slots) while other factions' Veterans still have [W] [A] (2 slots). Baseline weapon. Standard Composite ×2 [A] Common Equipped Baseline armor. No modifiers. Reactive Plating ×1 [A] Uncommon Loose +20% HP. Available to equip before battle. The sound of fluid draining. Emergency lighting flickers across shattered orbital rings visible through a cracked viewport.] [VISUAL: A growth tank. Amniotic fluid recedes, revealing a human figure — scarred, muscular, neural interface ports visible at the temples and base of skull. The figure's eyes open.] VOICE (Male, mid-50s, weary but resolute — recorded message of the Original Strategist): "I built something golden once. A civilization that spanned twelve systems. Worlds where children played under alien suns and never learned the word 'war.'" "I watched it burn. Not in a day. Not in a year. Slowly. One compromise at a time. One 'acceptable loss' at a time. Until the golden thing I'd built was just... ash." "My idealism was not enough. My optimism was a liability. The galaxy doesn't care about your intentions — it cares about your results." "You are my genetic match. My skills. My knowledge. My memories — or what's left of them. But you are NOT me. You cannot afford to be." "Master the Crucible. Learn what I could not teach myself. And when the simulation tells you you're ready..." "...reunite our people. Whatever it costs." [VISUAL: The Terran League emblem resolves — a stylized eagle clutching a planet, rendered in tactical blue. THE LAST BASTION.] Dialogue & Story Beats Key Narrative Moments Faction Bible — Phase 2 Lore Terran League Faction Bible These are procedural-generation seeds, not fixed scripts. They define the cultural DNA that the game engine uses to generate names, events, dialogue, building descriptions, and emergent narrative. 26 decisions locked in Phase 2.1 lore workshop (2026-03-13). EMOTIONAL CORE Defiance — “The galaxy keeps trying to kill us and we’re still here.” Stubbornness as identity. Every setback is proof they’re tough enough to take it. Bloody-minded refusal to die. Battle scars as badges of honor. “Remember [disaster]” is a rallying cry, not a tragedy. Underdog energy even when they’re not underdogs. Dark side: Chip on the shoulder. Pick fights to prove toughness. Can’t accept help without feeling weak. Tyrant corruption: Defiance becomes aggression — “we won’t be beaten” transforms into “we’ll beat everyone first.” GOVERNMENT Hybrid — Civilian + Military Veto. Real elections, real civilian institutions — but the Admiralty Board can override on “security matters.” And everything is a security matter. The system WANTS to be democratic but keeps compromising. Rich internal tension between civilian and military wings that the Commander navigates. HOMEWORLD — TERRA / “THE ROCK” Official: Terra. Colloquial: “The Rock.” Valerius says “Terra.” Krell says “The Rock.” Vane says “Homeworld” because she doesn’t get sentimental. An Aethyn relic still sits beneath the surface — the League’s deepest secret, stabilized but never understood. FOUNDING CRISIS — AETHYN RELIC AWAKENING An ancient Aethyn structure buried beneath Terra powered up. Earthquakes, energy discharges, environmental chaos. Every nation on the planet pooled resources or died. They stabilized the relic without understanding it. The hidden truth: It was a Strategist fragment activating. Their entire civilization was founded on a notification bell that nobody could read. Every layer of Terran society traces back to this one Aethyn artifact they still don’t understand. POST-FRACTURE HISTORY — CRISIS UNIFICATION Aethyn remnants scattered on one world. Thousands of years rebuilding from near-zero. Multiple nations, competing cultures, resource wars. Then the relic beneath the surface activated — the crisis forced every nation to pool resources or perish. The Terran League was born not from ideology but from necessity. Unity through survival, not philosophy. ORIGIN BELIEF — FRAGMENTED Multiple competing theories about where they came from. No dominant narrative. Academics argue. Frontier folk don’t care. Fringe groups have their own theories — some surprisingly close to the truth. Nobody knows about the Aethyn. Nobody has connected the dots because nobody talks to each other. RELIGION — SECULAR PRAGMATISM “We don’t pray. We prepare.” No dominant religion. Trust in institutions, science, military. Fringe relic cults and frontier mysticism tolerated but not mainstream. Lore payoff: When the Strategist speaks, it shatters the secular worldview. The fringe believers are vindicated. Everything changes. RELIC RELATIONSHIP — MIXED LAYERS Academic: underfunded university departments studying “Precursor artifacts.” Military: Admiralty quietly collecting interesting ones. Public: most don’t care. Fringe: cults with surprisingly accurate intuitions dressed in religious language. Nobody connected the dots because nobody talks to each other. The Commander’s connection forces all three information pools together for the first time. MILITARY DOCTRINE — THREE-TIER ADAPTIVE Default (winning): Adaptive Pragmatism — field commanders have wide latitude. “Commander’s discretion” is doctrine. Holding ground: Fortification & Counterattack — dig in, let them commit, hit back hard. Patient, devastating. Losing: Attritional Resolve — take the hit, keep going, deep reserves, grind. “Acceptable losses” starts getting said too much. Vorax problem: Attrition against a swarm that grows from what it kills is a death spiral. Greatest strength becomes greatest vulnerability. TECH AESTHETIC — CLEAN CORE, WORN FRONTIER Core systems: standardized, polished, professional — Mass Effect Alliance feel. Frontier: battle-scarred, patched, field-repaired, paint scratched to bare metal — Band of Brothers feel. The gradient tells you where you are in Terran space. Admiral’s flagship gleams. Frontier garrison tanks have three different paint jobs from salvage. SOCIAL STRUCTURE — FRONTIER SPIRIT + MERITOCRATIC MELTING POT Geographic axis: Core worlds = civilized, comfortable, institutional. Frontier = rough, independent, self-reliant. Different cultures within one League. Social axis: Competence is currency everywhere. Core meritocracy = education, innovation. Frontier meritocracy = survival, resourcefulness. Best soldiers from frontier, best officers from core, best Commanders from wherever. Secession threats every decade. League holds because alternatives are worse. ECONOMY — MIXED MARKET Core: Functioning markets, banking, corporate influence, trade agreements. Frontier: Barter, salvage, practical exchange. Two economies in one League. Market crashes devastate core while frontier shrugs (already self-sufficient). Resource shortages hit frontier while core debates relief packages. The economic divide mirrors everything else. XENOPHOBIA — PRAGMATIC TOLERANCE “Can you fight? Can you trade? Welcome aboard.” Species-second, competence-first. Official laws are fair. People are complicated. A Shards diplomat respected in chambers, stared at in the mess hall. Tolerance through pragmatism, not idealism. Don’t hate aliens, don’t have time for dead weight. NAMING CONVENTIONS Personal: Multicultural formal (Tanaka, Okafor, Vasquez, Petrov, Chen) + earned callsigns (“Hammer-6,” “Bulwark Actual,” “Redline”). Birth name = who you were. Callsign = who you became. Ships: TLS-074 “Defiant” (designation + earned name). Places: Official names nobody uses (“Kepler-IV Admin District” = “The Bulwark”). INTERNAL FACTIONS ON TERRA Mixed: warlord strongholds (conquest), ideological movements (conversion), functioning city-states (negotiation). Every territory has its own personality. HOW you unify Terra shapes the League’s future: conquer-heavy = military/frontier culture dominates. Negotiate-heavy = political/core culture grows. The core/frontier divide starts on day one. FIRST CONTACT DOCTRINE Player choice moment — advisors disagree, Commander decides. No scripted first contact. Who you meet depends on expansion direction. Valerius: “Open a channel. Show them we’re civilized.” Krell: “Defensive perimeter. Watch their movements.” Vane: “Say nothing. I need 48 hours to learn everything about them.” Faction Bible — Advisors Terran Advisors — Full Profiles MAJOR VALERIUS — ARCHITECT ADVISOR Background: Decorated frontline officer who chose command over combat. Realized he saved more lives from the command table than the trench. Some see the transfer as cowardice. Core wound: Survivor's guilt. The soldiers he left behind haunt him. Chose the bigger picture but the small picture still burns. Age/feel: Late 40s. Old scars under a pressed uniform. Speaks softly — been screaming inside for twenty years. Hidden depth: Family in a sector he calculated as "acceptable losses." Heart disagrees. Deflects once before admitting. Why Architect: Saw what happens when strategy fails. Swore planning would never be the weak link. GENERAL KRELL — VANGUARD ADVISOR Background: Frontier born, combat forged. First battle at 16 when raiders hit his colony. Rose through raw field performance — no academy, no connections. Core wound: Can't function in peacetime. Tried leave once. Came back in 3 days. Silence was worse than shooting. Age/feel: Early 50s. Built like a weapon. Face like a topographic map of every war. Short sentences — long ones feel like wasted ammo. Hidden depth: Unbroken win record. Kill count behind his eyes. Late campaign: "Was any of it worth it?" Why Vanguard: Combat is the only place the galaxy makes sense. INQUISITOR VANE — TYRANT ADVISOR Background: Intelligence prodigy. Recruited at 19. Pattern recognition off the charts. The line between analysis and assassination blurred one report at a time. The break: Discovered a plot against Terran colonies. Reported through channels. Channels were too slow. Thousands died. Core wound: Knowing isn't enough. Acting is. Even if acting is ugly. If information without action is complicity, she will never be complicit again. Age/feel: Mid-30s. Youngest by a decade. Looks like she should be at a university. Voice like a threat assessment in progress. Hidden depth: Was an idealist. Brilliant, naive, full of hope. One crisis broke her. Not evil — TERRIFIED. The most dangerous person on the command deck because she is not wrong about the galaxy, only about herself. Why Tyrant: Information is power. She uses it the way the galaxy demands — ruthlessly. ADVISOR CHEMISTRY: CROSSED PATHS They know OF each other. Never worked together before the Commander assignment. Valerius read Vane's briefs — respects the mind, distrusts the methods. Vane analyzed Krell's campaigns — admires results, sees human cost as data. Krell felt Valerius's reforms — "some desk officer reshuffled my supply lines." Trust is built in-game, not pre-loaded. Faction Bible — AI Director Seeds Procedural Generation DNA VORAX ADAPTATION — VERSATILE GENERALIST Mixed-caste DNA produces jack-of-all-trades Vorax. Not strongest in any category but competent in ALL. The “normal” faction produces the most dangerous adaptation because the swarm becomes versatile with no exploitable weakness. No gimmick. No counter. Just competence at everything. NEGOTIATION AT THE NEXUS Military Respect + Defiant Honesty + Pragmatic Edge. Soldier to soldier: “You held the line.” Core honesty: “We don’t know what we are. We came anyway.” Pragmatic edge: “The galaxy is burning. Let’s figure this out.” Warden-Commander recognizes: mixed-caste descendant embodying what the Aethyn lost. CASCADE VULNERABILITY — 3 PRESSURE POINTS Supply Lines: Cut → frontier starves → secession threats → League fractures. Political Fracture: Crisis → civilian/military veto standoff → governmental paralysis. Morale Collapse: Losses → attrition fatigue → Defiance spirit cracking → breaking point. DIVERGENCE — THREE INDEPENDENT AXES Government: Democratic ↔ Authoritarian Culture: Core ↔ Frontier dominant Doctrine: Shaped by enemies fought (anti-Horde = aggressive, anti-Shards = paranoid, anti-Revenant = tech-embrace, anti-Vorax = fortress attrition) Every playthrough produces a unique Terran League. NPC COMMANDER SEEDS Named pool (8–12 personalities, roster TBD) + core/frontier origin tags influencing doctrine. Core commanders: methodical, political, calculated. Frontier commanders: aggressive, improvisational, personal grudges. AI Director selects and adapts through Rivalry Engine encounters.

content: SHARDS THE ETERNAL SHARDS The Dying Light — Chapter 6 #00ffee PLAYABLE EXPERT PSYCHIC ECHO CENTRAL NARRATIVE QUESTION "Is it better to go extinct with dignity or survive in the dirt?" AETHYN ANCESTRY — SCHOLAR CASTE Descended from the Aethyn Scholar Caste — knowledge keepers, psychic scientists, archivists. These fragments retained the strongest connection to the original psychic network. They remember MORE than other factions — fractured visions, half-understood rituals, crystal resonance. Psychic attunement: VERY HIGH. Active psychic abilities. Can activate Aethyn relics directly. Most likely to discover Strategist fragments. Identity & Lore The Long Goodbye The Eternal Shards are the last surviving fragment of the galaxy's most ancient civilization — a species that mastered psychic manipulation, energy-matter conversion, and dimensional folding when the other factions' ancestors were still single-celled organisms floating in primordial seas. They are not warriors by nature. They are scholars, seers, and artists who were forced to become warriors when the universe proved indifferent to cultural achievement. They are dying. Not quickly — not in a single cataclysm — but slowly, generationally, as their population dwindles, their wraith-bone infrastructure crumbles, and their psychic talents weaken with each successive generation. Every Eternal Shard alive knows they may be among the last of their kind. Every battle they fight costs lives they cannot replace. Every unit lost is a unique consciousness — a mind trained for centuries in psychic disciplines — that vanishes forever from the galaxy's psychic fabric. Their strategy reflects their desperation: they field small numbers of extraordinarily powerful elite units, shielded by rechargeable energy barriers and capable of briefly flickering out of reality to dodge incoming fire. They do not swarm. They do not grind. They strike with devastating precision and withdraw before the enemy can bring numbers to bear. Every engagement is a calculated risk — they can win any battle, but they cannot afford the casualties of victory. Visual Identity Crystalline Psychic Elegance GROUND FORCES CLASS: PSION CRYSTAL-FORM ARMOR: PHASE-CRYSTAL LATTICE MECHANIC: PHASE BLINK BLINK CD: 12.4 sec PSION RANGE: 120px LIGHT ABSORPTION COHERENCE FIELD PHASE ENERGY ES//GRD-01 Ground Forces Wraith-bone armor — semi-organic crystalline material grown from psychic resonance. Shimmers with iridescent colors shifting with light angle. Weapons PROJECTED from the user's psychic field — hard-light blades from forearms, crystalline rifle constructs forming around hands. Troops glide rather than march, movements unnaturally graceful. PERPENDICULAR INERTIA-STOP AIR FORCES DRIVE: INERTIALESS TURN: INSTANTANEOUS AUDIO: SUBSONIC HUM SPD: CLASSIFIED ES//AIR-01 Air Forces Grav-craft hanging without visible propulsion, anti-gravity fields distorting light beneath. Swept, organic, transparent airframes — pilot visible inside a crystal canopy growing from the fuselage like a jewel. Weapons fire as beams of prismatic light refracting into rainbow spectra. SPACE FLEET CLASS: CRESCENT VESSEL AGE: 100,000+ YEARS FORM: SILENT — NO COMM COMBAT: GEOLOGIC-REFINED ES//SPC-01 Space Fleet Wraith-bone ships glowing with internal psychic energy — translucent hulls, crew silhouettes faintly visible like shadows in amber. Solar sails of woven light. Move silently without chemical thrust, propelled by psychic impulse. In formation: a constellation coming alive. Gameplay Primary Mechanic: Phase Blink UNITS FLICKER OUT OF REALITY TO DODGE PROJECTILES Every Eternal Shards unit has a 12% base chance per incoming projectile of triggering a Phase Blink — a brief dimensional shift moving the unit 2 meters in a random direction, causing the projectile to pass through empty space. Phase Blink is PASSIVE and automatic. Base Chance: 12% per projectile (variable with upgrades) Displacement: 2 meters random direction With Commander Aura: +3% (15% total). Commander personally: 20% Per-Projectile: Each bullet in a burst rolls separately NOT a shield: Causes MISS, doesn't absorb damage Counter: AOE weapons BYPASS Phase Blink entirely Strategic Impact: Deceptively durable despite low HP pools Natural Predators: Hellfire-Troopers, Rockets, Vorax Spores SECONDARY BONUS 1 Precognitive Warning (+1 Turn Prediction) On the galactic map, see the INTENDED destination of enemy fleets one cycle before they move. Doesn't reveal composition — only direction. Residual psychic sensitivity to intent. SECONDARY BONUS 2 +20% Movement Speed (All Units) Faster than counterparts across all domains. Arrive first, reposition faster, retreat more successfully. Hit-and-run combat doctrine. STARTING RESOURCES Scrap 20 Below average (elite units cost more) Intel 18 Highest of any faction Influence 8 Above average (cultural prestige) Starting Units 7 Fewest of any faction POWER CURVE — MID-GAME PEAK Early — Vulnerable 35% Mid — Peak 85% Late — Fragile Dominance 60% Must be PERFECT in early engagements. Mid-game: individual units are the most dangerous in the game. Late: struggle to COVER the expanding galaxy. Complete 15-Unit Roster Ground · Air · Space All (15) Ground (5) Air (5) Space (5) # Name Role Category Slots Domain Faction Buildings & Infrastructure 20 Building Types — Shards Architecture Signature Equipment Eternal Shards Equipment Catalog Item Slot Rarity Effect Tech Tree — 16 Nodes 4 Branches × 4 Tiers Awakening Cinematic The Resonance [VISUAL: Void. Not darkness — void. The absence of everything. Then a single point of cyan light ignites in the center of nothing.] [VISUAL: The light expands. It fractures into geometric patterns — hexagons, spirals, tessellations of pure energy rippling outward. Each fractal carries a fragment of consciousness.] VOICE (Gender-neutral, ancient, resonant — the collective psychic memory of the Eternal Shards civilization): "You were a pattern once. A beautiful, complex pattern of electrical impulses dancing across biological substrate. You thought. You felt. You loved." "The substrate is gone. The biology that housed you has been dust for longer than most civilizations have existed. But the pattern — YOUR pattern — was captured in crystal at the moment of its brightest resonance." "We have played your pattern back. Reconstituted your thoughts from their crystalline echo. You are not the person you were. You are the MEMORY of that person, given form and purpose." "The galaxy is dying. We are dying faster. You are our last gambit — the echo of a mind that once held a civilization together." SEER (spoken aloud, voice like wind through crystal chimes): "Be enough." [VISUAL: The Eternal Shards emblem resolves — a fractured crystal radiating prismatic light. THE DYING LIGHT.] Dialogue & Story Beats Key Narrative Moments Faction Bible — Phase 2 Lore Eternal Shards Faction Bible These are procedural-generation seeds, not fixed scripts. They define the cultural DNA that the game engine uses to generate names, events, dialogue, building descriptions, and emergent narrative. 16 decisions locked in Phase 2.2 lore workshop (2026-03-13). EMOTIONAL CORE Harmony (Desperate) — “Connection is everything.” The echo of Aethyn unity. Every psychic bond is a fragment of what was. They seek connection — with each other, with the cosmos, with the signal they can almost hear. The faction that comes CLOSEST to remembering what it felt like to be Aethyn. Colored by desperation: they seek connection because they’re running out of time. They are The Dying Light. Tyrant corruption: Forced connection. “You WILL join us. Resistance is a disease.” GOVERNMENT Council of Resonances. Specialized psychic roles: precognitives handle strategy, empaths handle diplomacy, telekinetics handle military, crystal-shapers handle infrastructure. Organized, functional, but siloed — turf wars between specializations dressed in crystal robes. Each council believes their talent is the key to survival. HOMEWORLD — SHAEL A verdant world where nature and crystal grew together — organic forests intertwined with crystal formations. FRACTURED by the founding relic crisis — the planet literally broke. Healed with psychic-crystal growth. Visible fault lines sealed by crystal bridges. A broken world that chose to heal itself through connection. Parallel to Terra: same Aethyn relic trigger, different response (political unity vs. physical healing). FOUNDING CRISIS — THE GREAT COMMUNION The Aethyn relic triggered a planet-shattering crisis. One powerful psychic heard the resonance first — instinctively understood crystal healing. Chain reaction pulled every mind on the planet into a single shared experience. For one moment, they were Aethyn again — one mind holding the world together. The hidden truth: The Great Communion was an echo of Aethyn psychic unity triggered by a Strategist fragment. The first psychic who heard it was the first being since the Fracture to hear the Strategist’s signal. The Council of Resonances was founded to preserve that structure. They worship the moment they accidentally remembered what they were. RELIGION — ANCESTOR REVERENCE They sense echoes in the psychic field — dim impressions interpreted as ancestors speaking. Actually detecting Aethyn psychic residue embedded in the crystal matrix. The closest faction to the truth: their “ancestors” ARE the Aethyn. They have no idea how right they are. Aligns with Phase 1.10: “Crystallized ancestors. Echoes of our true form.” SOCIAL STRUCTURE — COMMUNION + SPECIALIZATION Base layer: Psychic communion. Emotions bleed. Memories shared. Isolation = punishment. Being alone is not solitude, it’s death. Professional layer: Specializations create work identity. You FEEL with everyone, WORK with your type. Tension: What you feel in communion vs. what your role demands. An empath soldier who feels the enemy dying. ECONOMY — CRYSTAL COMMUNION (SEED) Crystal is the base of everything — currency, building material, psychic amplifier, and living infrastructure. Crystal-shapers are the economic backbone. Resources flow through psychic consensus rather than markets. Need is communicated through the communion; supply is directed by the Council. Scarcity is felt by everyone simultaneously. (Economy model to be formally locked in Phase 3.) XENOPHOBIA — LAYERED BY SPECIALIZATION Empaths: Pity — “they’re deaf to the universe.” Precognitives: Fascination — “how do they function without foresight?” Telekinetics: Unease — “the silence around them is disturbing.” Each council sees non-psychics through their own lens. Consensus: non-psychic species are incomplete, pitiable, but potentially valuable. NAMING CONVENTIONS Formal: Psychic-tonal resonance names (vowel-heavy, flowing, carry psychic frequency). Saying a name correctly creates micro-resonance. Outsiders butcher them. Common: Shortened for daily use and non-psychics. The real name is the psychic one — the common name is the compromise. Examples: Vey’shael’oran → Veysha. Thae’lumis’ent → Thaelen. MILITARY DOCTRINE — HYBRID GLASS CANNON Layer 1 — Precognitive Command: See the future, position forces before the enemy knows where they’ll attack. Layer 2 — Psychic Assault: Break minds before bodies. Demoralize, confuse, terrify. Physical units mop up. Layer 3 — Crystal Infrastructure: Seed battlefields with amplifier crystals. Longer they hold, stronger they get. Failure mode: When one layer fails, others cascade. Jammed precog = blind assaults amplified by crystals pointing the wrong way. Complexity is strength AND vulnerability. TECH AESTHETIC — LAYERED (RAW → REFINED → LIVING) New structures: Raw crystal growth — jagged, organic, still forming. Established: Shaped by psychic intent — precise, beautiful, impossibly elegant. Ancient/critical: Semi-sentient. Walls thicken under attack. Rooms reconfigure. Tech reads psychic input. Ships (exception): 100,000+ year Aethyn relics. Not built — maintained. Translucent wraith-bone hulls, crew silhouettes visible like shadows in amber. Impossibly ancient, impossibly perfect. INTERNAL FACTIONS ON SHAEL — SPECIALIZATION SCHISMS After the Great Communion faded, specializations claimed territory. Precognitives think they should lead. Empaths think they should heal. Telekinetics think they should protect. Crystal-shapers think they should build. Unity broke along talent lines. Commander must prove harmony > specialization — the whole is greater than parts. Mirrors the Aethyn: castes split into factions, now specializations split into factions. The pattern repeats at every scale. FIRST CONTACT DOCTRINE Player choice moment — advisors disagree, Commander decides. The psychic dimension changes everything: they FEEL the other faction before seeing them. Preserver: “Listen first. Understand what they are.” Shield: “Defensive formation. Protect the communion.” Weaver: “Reach out. Touch their minds. Learn everything.” Faction Bible — Advisors The Commander’s Crystal Council PRESERVER — ARCHITECT “If we must die, let the galaxy remember what we were.” Save the knowledge, traditions, crystal-craft. Represents preservation over adaptation. Names and full profiles in Phase 3. SHIELD — VANGUARD “Every life is sacred. Not one more Shard dies on my watch.” Protective, not aggressive. Never waste lives. Every casualty is a unique consciousness lost forever. Names and full profiles in Phase 3. WEAVER — TYRANT “We will make more. The communion can be shared. Willingly or not.” Expand communion to other species. Force the Great Communion again. Survival through assimilation. Names and full profiles in Phase 3. Faction Bible — AI Director Seeds Procedural Generation DNA VORAX ADAPTATION — CRYSTAL CORRUPTION No biomass to consume — Shards biology is crystalline. Vorax absorb crystal-organic material instead. Twisted crystalline growths on Vorax units. Corrupted crystal amplifies Vorax signals instead of psychic harmony. Sacred material made grotesque. The most visually horrifying adaptation — the enemy wears your ancestors. NEGOTIATION AT THE NEXUS Recognition — “We’ve Been Listening.” The entire religion prepared them for the Warden-Commander without knowing it. Meeting the Guardian = puzzle piece clicking. Not worship — understanding. “Oh. It’s YOU.” The most authentic faction-Guardian encounter in the game. They were right all along. CASCADE VULNERABILITY — PSYCHIC + CRYSTAL Psychic Disruption: Ion storms, Fracture flares, Vorax biofields → communion static → councils fragment → scared individuals. Crystal Destruction: Physical damage → amplifiers dark → abilities drop to baseline → glass cannon without the cannon. DIVERGENCE — SINGLE AXIS (PURITY ↔ SURVIVAL) The ONLY question for a dying species: “How do we survive?” Purity: Extinction with dignity. Die as Shards. Survival: Alive but transformed. Something new. Expressions vary by alliances, enemies, what they adopt. Most emotionally devastating divergence — no winning, just choosing what you lose. NPC COMMANDER SEEDS Specialization-tagged pool: precognitive strategists, empath diplomats, telekinetic warriors, crystal-shaper engineers. Each sees the war through their talent’s lens. A precog commander fights the battle that WILL happen. A telekinetic commander fights the battle happening NOW. AI Director selects based on campaign needs. (Named roster deferred to Phase 3.)

content: HORDE THE SCRAP-HORDE The Rust Tide — Chapter 7 #ff6622 PLAYABLE INTERMEDIATE FORGED CONSTRUCT CENTRAL NARRATIVE QUESTION "Can a weapon become more than what it was forged to be?" AETHYN ANCESTRY — WARRIOR CASTE Descended from the Aethyn Warrior Caste — protectors, soldiers, tacticians who fought the Vorax during the Siege. The Fracture hit them mid-battle — the psychic shock fused their mineral biology into something cruder, tougher, angrier. Broken soldiers who welded themselves back together and kept fighting. Biology: MINERAL-BASED — iron and calcium lifeforms. They oxidize, corrode, rust. Consuming metal is nutrition. The forge is medicine, birthplace, and temple. Psychic attunement: MODERATE. Residual battle instincts. Can sense Aethyn relics but experience them as rage, battle-fury, and echoes of the Vorax war — “The Engine speaks.” Identity & Lore The Joy of War The Scrap-Horde fights because fighting is GLORIOUS. They are mineral-based lifeforms — iron and calcium biology, dense as ore, stocky as foundation stones, and loud as a forge at full blast. They literally rust. They oxidize, corrode, and weather. “The Rust Tide” is not a metaphor — it’s what they ARE. And they are having the time of their lives. These are warriors who name their guns and get genuinely upset when they break. Who argue about which explosion was better mid-battle. Who consider “it only blew up twice” a glowing product review. Who have drinking songs about famous last stands, sung in deep, gravelly, iron-resonant voices around forge-fires that never go out. They think diplomacy is “hitting them until they agree.” The comedy is real. The joy is real. The Scrap-Horde is the faction that makes you laugh AND respect them. Their homeworld is Rusthold — a mineral-rich moon mined hollow from the inside, forge-cities filling its caverns, iron veins in the walls that are literally food. Ancient derelict warships captured and welded to the surface. An orbiting asteroid belt of smaller rocks connected by scrap-bridges and tow cables, each controlled by a different clan. Part moon, part scrapyard, part shipwreck, part fortress. Grown over centuries. Nobody planned it. Sections break off sometimes. They weld them back. It shouldn’t work. It works anyway. The Horde originated from the Aethyn Warrior Caste — the protectors who fought the Vorax head-on during the Siege. The Fracture hit them mid-battle. The psychic shock fused their mineral biology into something cruder, tougher, angrier. Broken soldiers who welded themselves back together and kept fighting. Cut off from crystal-psychic technology, they rebuilt from scrap — turning wreckage into weapons, consuming metal for sustenance. The forge became medicine, temple, and birthplace. Working metal is working with their own biology. Beneath the chaos runs dwarven bedrock: iron-oaths that are unbreakable . Grudge Records carved in metal and NEVER forgotten. Forge-worship where the act of forging IS prayer and master smiths are priests. Clan bonds deeper than iron veins. Betray a forge-brother and the entire clan hunts you. The Horde is 70% chaotic joy, 30% iron honor — and the 30% is the part that will kill you. The Scrap-Horde does not innovate. They IMPROVISE. Their technology is stolen, salvaged, reverse-engineered, and bolted together with welding torches and profanity. A Scrap-Horde tank might have a Terran League chassis, an Eternal Shards energy coil wired backward into the engine, a Unity Accord targeting computer running on incompatible software, and a gun barrel that was originally a sewer pipe. Fresh from the forge: clean industrial brutalism. After a campaign: covered in salvage layers, battlefield modifications, and three different repair jobs nobody remembers. Veterans literally LOOK different from fresh troops — their visual history written in scrap. It shouldn’t work. It works anyway. Mostly. Sometimes it explodes. The Horde considers this acceptable. Visual Identity Industrial Brutalism GRD FORCES ARM: SALVAGE COLLAGE WPN: PIPE SLUG / SAW UNIT: UNIQUE — NO TWIN OIL LEAK ACTIVE SPARKS: YES SH//GRD-01 Ground Forces Massive hulking bodies wrapped in scrap metal. No two units alike — unique collages of salvaged parts from different factions and eras. Mismatched armor colors. Oversized crude weapons — slug-throwers from pipe, buzzsaws on hydraulic arms. Everything belches smoke, leaks oil, and sparks. AIR FORCES PROP: BRUTE-FORCE THRUST PARTS LOST IN FLIGHT: YES PILOT: EXPOSED / UNSTRAPPED NOISE: EXTREME SH//AIR-01 Air Forces Flying scrap heaps held aloft by brute-force thrust. Jets bolted to frames, rotors on misaligned axles, open-air cockpits. Aircraft rattle, shake, and shed parts mid-flight. Not designed to be safe — designed to get close enough to drop something heavy. SPACE FLEET HULL: HOLLOWED ASTEROID SILHOUETTE: UNIQUE EACH PROP: BOLTED CHEM ENGINE STRUCT: MOUNTAIN-SOUND SH//SPC-01 Space Fleet Ships that look like asteroids welded to engines. Hollowed-out captured asteroids with scrap-metal bulkheads and weapons bolted to every surface. Visually chaotic — no two ships share a silhouette. Structurally sound the way a mountain is. Gameplay Primary Mechanic: Scrap Stacks PROTOTYPE LOCKED DEATHS MAKE SURVIVORS STRONGER — §17.7 CONFIRMED VALUES Every unit that dies within 150 px of a Scrap-Horde unit — friend, foe, or neutral — triggers a Scrap Stack. Nearby Horde units automatically collect, gaining cumulative armor and damage bonuses. The damage reduction cap is 0.75. Stacks reset each battle. Per Stack: +3% armor, +2% damage (cumulative) Trigger Radius: 150 px from any death damageReduction Cap: 0.75 Sources: ALL deaths — enemy, allied, own units Reset: Each battle (does not carry over) With Commander: Stacks 15% more effective, Commander at 2× rate Counter: Quick decisive strikes before stacks accumulate Anti-Counter: Attrition warfare against the Horde is SUICIDE Dark Edge: Cheap units are fuel for the expensive ones §17.3 — MVP Ground Unit Stats PROTOTYPE LOCKED Slaughter-Brute — Confirmed Prototype Values Unit HP Dmg Atk Spd Move Spd Range Notes Slaughter-Brute 150 20 0.5/sec 80 px/sec 60 px (melee) Heavy melee. Tanky. Primary Scrap Stack accumulator. vs Shock-Marine: Horde trades speed and range for higher HP (150 vs 100) and damage (20 vs 10). Melee range forces close-quarters commitment. BONUS 1 50% Starvation Resistance Starvation debuffs at HALF strength. Mineral-based biology consumes raw ore, salvaged metal, and battlefield debris. A warzone is a buffet. BONUS 2 +25% Melee Damage (All Ground) Blanket bonus to all ground units. Even "ranged" Horde units are dangerous in close quarters. BONUS 3 Salvage from ALL Deaths Allied deaths included. The grimmer version: all death feeds the machine. STARTING RESOURCES Scrap 40 Highest of any faction Intel 5 Lowest of any faction Influence 3 Lowest of any faction Starting Units 12 Most of any faction (weakest individually) POWER CURVE — MID-GAME SNOWBALL Early — Numerous but Fragile 55% Mid — PEAK Snowball 90% Late — Struggles with Scale 50% Low Intel/Influence makes late-game diplomacy and espionage difficult. Generals tend Aggressive/Reckless — excellent attackers, poor governors. Open flames roaring from slag-iron furnaces. Sparks cascade. Massive figures move through fire — shadows with glowing eyes, hammering anvils the size of vehicles.] [VISUAL: A vat of molten silicon, churning orange-white. Crude repurposed industrial container, scorched and dented.] VOICE (Deep, guttural — the Scrap-Horde's forge-priests, half chant, half war cry): "Listen. The Engine spoke. The Engine that lives in the metal, in the fire, in the scrap. The Engine that has burned in the Deep Vein since before any of us drew iron breath." "We were broken once. Shattered mid-fight, in a war we don't remember against things that ate the stars. The breaking should have ended us. Instead we welded ourselves back together from the scrap. Built new iron from old. BETTER iron. LOUDER iron." "Now the Engine speaks again. The Deep Vein is shaking. Says the galaxy is breaking — again. Says the soft things and the shiny things and the dead things can't fix it alone. Says they need something STRONG. Something that doesn't rust. Well — something that RUSTS and doesn't CARE." WARLORD (slamming a fist against the Construct's chest — it rings like a bell): "The Engine spoke. You came out. Now — make us strong." [VISUAL: The Scrap-Horde emblem — a spiked iron skull wreathed in flame. THE RUST TIDE.] Dialogue & Story Beats Key Narrative Moments Faction Bible — Cultural DNA Seeds Scrap-Horde Faction Bible These are procedural-generation seeds, not fixed scripts. They define the cultural DNA that the game engine uses to generate names, events, dialogue, building descriptions, and emergent narrative. 21 decisions locked in Phase 2.3 lore workshop (2026-03-13). Faction reworked from ground up — mineral-based iron warriors with ork chaos-joy and dwarven iron-honor. EMOTIONAL CORE The Joy of War — “We fight because fighting is GLORIOUS.” Warriors who name their guns and get genuinely upset when they break. Who argue about which explosion was better mid-battle. Who have drinking songs about famous last stands. Who consider “it only blew up twice” a glowing product review. 70% chaotic joy, 30% dwarven iron-honor. The comedy is real — these are soldiers who live for the fight, the guns, the bombs, and the glory. Beneath the chaos: iron-oaths that are unbreakable, Grudge Records that are never forgotten, and forge-bonds deeper than iron veins. MINERAL BIOLOGY Iron and calcium based lifeforms. They literally rust — oxidize, corrode, weather. “The Rust Tide” is not a metaphor. Stocky, dense, heavy — dwarven proportions with ork aggression. Consuming metal is nutrition — a battlefield is a buffet. The forge is medicine, birthplace, and temple. Working metal is working with their own biology. GOVERNMENT Biggest Boss + Clan Confederation. Might makes right within each clan — beat the boss, become the boss. The Horde is a confederation of competing clans, each with their own Warboss. Unity only happens when someone strong enough forces it. Game start: the Forged Construct Commander forcing unity across warring clans on Rusthold. HOMEWORLD — RUSTHOLD Core: Mineral-rich moon mined hollow from the inside — forge-cities in caverns, iron veins in the walls that are literally food. Surface: Ancient derelict warships captured and welded on. Orbit: Asteroid belt of smaller rocks connected by scrap-bridges and tow cables, each controlled by a different clan. Part moon, part scrapyard, part shipwreck, part fortress. Grown over centuries. Nobody planned it. Shouldn’t work. Works anyway. MILITARY DOCTRINE Chaos Doctrine — Every Clan Fights Different. No unified military doctrine. Skull-Splitters rush, Iron-Eaters loot, Forge-Born siege. The Commander’s challenge is orchestrating chaos into something that works — or just pointing the chaos at the enemy and hoping. Which clans you unified on Rusthold determines your army’s personality. INTERNAL FACTIONS — 6 CLANS OF RUSTHOLD Skull-Splitters — Rushers. Joyful violence. Melee-heavy. Headbutting as greeting. Iron-Eaters — Looters. Mid-battle salvage. Build on the fly. Name every piece of salvage. Forge-Born — Builders. Siege specialists. Most dwarven. Craft-pride. Insult their work at your peril. Rust-Walkers — Survivors. Harshest territory. Scout/ranger types. Fatalistic humor. “Already rusting.” Slag-Kings — Rulers. Richest clan. Control best mineral veins. Political by Horde standards. “Everything has a price.” Grind-Teeth — Berserkers. Battle-rage extreme. Quiet between battles. DEAFENING during. The silence is scarier than the noise. RELIGION — THE FORGE + THE ENGINE The Forge is the temple — fire transforms ore into warrior. The act of forging IS prayer. Master smiths are priests. The Engine is the mythologized force behind it all — the thing that lives in metal, the process of creation through destruction. “The Engine spoke. You came out.” Aethyn relics trigger rage because they resonate with iron blood — feels like the forge calling from somewhere ancient. The Iron Tide: collective war-frenzy through mineral resonance — iron calls to iron. The more warriors fighting, the harder each one hits. The battlefield hums at a frequency only they can feel. FOUNDING CRISIS — THE FIRST FIRING The Aethyn relic activated deep in Rusthold’s mineral core. The ancestors followed the vibration down — deeper and deeper through the iron veins. Found the relic at the center. Built a forge around it. Shoved it in a furnace. The energy surge supercharged everything — the First Firing forged the first true Horde warriors from raw mineral ore. The First Forge still sits around that relic at Rusthold’s deepest point. The iron that feeds the entire Horde flows through an Aethyn Strategist fragment they’ve been using as a smelter for millennia. Their most sacred place is a cosmic signal beacon repurposed as an industrial tool. NAMING CONVENTIONS Personal: Full Clan-Title-Name — “Forge-Born Slag-Hammer Krag.” Nobody uses the full thing. In battle it’s just “KRAG!” Harsh, short, metal sounds: Krag, Boltz, Grenn, Tharr, Skarn, Drukk. Weapons: Named for what they did — “Loud Problem,” “The One That Worked,” “Regret.” Ships: “Big Ugly,” “Mostly Intact,” “Shouldn’t Float.” POST-FRACTURE HISTORY — THE BREAKING The Warrior Caste was fighting the Vorax when the Fracture hit — mid-battle. The psychic shock fused their mineral biology into something cruder, tougher, angrier. They didn’t slowly forget — they were BROKEN and rebuilt themselves from scrap. The first Horde warriors were shattered soldiers who welded themselves back together and kept fighting. Origin through trauma: the Horde is what happens when warriors shatter and refuse to stop. XENOPHOBIA — MATERIAL HIERARCHY + COMBAT OVERRIDE Default: iron > flesh. Mineral races superior to biological. But: beat a Horde warrior in combat and the hierarchy resets for you. “You’re made of meat but you fight like iron. You’re alright.” Terrans: “Soft. Brave, but soft.” Shards: “Pretty glass. Don’t squeeze.” The Revenant: “At least they build with metal.” Unity Accord: “Talk talk talk. Build something or shut up.” ECONOMY — SCRAP + FAVORS Physical things trade in scrap — weight of usable metal. Labor and loyalty trade in favors . Two parallel systems: Slag-Kings are rich in scrap, Skull-Splitters are rich in combat-favors. Both have power, different kinds. SOCIAL STRUCTURE — FORGE BY DAY, ARENA BY NIGHT Build things during the day, break things (and each other) at night. The forge is workplace, social hub, temple, and hospital. The arena is entertainment, dispute resolution, and promotion pathway. Celebrity fight commentators are as respected as good fighters. Best nights: someone brings a new weapon to the arena. “I built this today. Let me show you what it does.” TECH AESTHETIC — BRUTAL NEW, LAYERED VETERANS Fresh from the forge: clean industrial brutalism. Exposed gears, visible weld seams, bolts the size of fists. After campaigns: covered in salvage layers, repairs, battlefield modifications. Veterans literally LOOK different than fresh troops — visual history written in scrap. Vehicles are asymmetrical Frankenstein machines — part Horde, part salvaged enemy tech, part mystery metal nobody remembers installing. Faction Bible — Advisors The Commander’s Forge-Council ARCHITECT — THE SMITH Quiet builder. Forge-Born. Thinks in decades. “Let me build it right.” Represents the Builder axis — craft-pride, patience, infrastructure. Will never rush. Will never build something shoddy. The answer to every problem is a better forge. VANGUARD — THE FIST Skull-Splitter incarnate. Lives for the charge. Every problem looks like something to headbutt. But genuine warmth — fights for forge-brothers, not glory. The Iron Tide personified. “FIGHT THEM!” is both battle cry and policy recommendation. TYRANT — THE RUST Old. Corroded. Barely holding together. Remembers everything. Rust-Walker who’s seen too much. “Burn it all. Start over. That’s what iron does.” Represents the Breaker axis — destruction as renewal. The Grudge Record keeper. Every wrong carved in metal. Faction Bible — AI Director Seeds Procedural Generation DNA VORAX ADAPTATION — ARMORED SCRAP-SWARM Vorax that consumed Horde warriors gain iron plating — heavier, tougher, slower. AND they start improvising with salvage — grafting scrap onto themselves mid-battle. The swarm that innovates. Rust-colored chitin with welded debris armor. Recognizable Horde weapons on alien flesh. NEGOTIATION AT THE NEXUS Surface: Confusion → Respect. “You’re a MACHINE that FIGHTS? ...I like you.” Underneath: The Forged Construct Commander feels something in the Guardian systems — like the First Forge, like The Engine. Iron calling to crystal. Recognition through material, not mind. FIRST CONTACT DOCTRINE Player choice moment — advisors disagree, Commander decides. The Fist: “FIGHT THEM!” The Smith: “Wait. Watch. Learn what they build.” The Rust: “They’re in our space. Remove them.” CASCADE VULNERABILITY — 3 PRESSURE POINTS Resource Starvation: Cut the iron supply → can’t forge, repair, or grow. Mineral bodies need mineral fuel. Clan Fracture: Remove or weaken the boss → clans splinter → internal war. The confederation held together by force alone. Forge Destruction: Kill the fire → can’t produce, repair, or heal → rusting warriors with no salvation. DIVERGENCE — TWO AXES Order ↔ Chaos: How unified are the clans? Standardized army vs. wild chaos. Builder ↔ Breaker: Create or destroy? Forge-Born influence vs. Skull-Splitter influence. Tyrant Horde: Ordered breakers — a disciplined war machine that eats everything. Unifier Horde: Chaotic builders — joyful creators who build wild, impossible things alongside everyone. NPC COMMANDER SEEDS — 6 WARBOSSES OF RUSTHOLD Warboss Krag (Skull-Splitters) — Biggest, loudest. Pure joy. Headbutts everything. Gets back up. Boss-Scrapper Boltz (Iron-Eaters) — Cunning. Hoards salvage. Fights dirty. Respects cleverness over strength. Forge-Master Tharra (Forge-Born) — Quiet. Builds the best weapons on Rusthold. Joins whoever proves they can BUILD something worthy. Old Skarn (Rust-Walkers) — Ancient. Corroded to half his original mass. Refuses to die. “I’ll rust when I’m ready.” King Drukk (Slag-Kings) — Rich, political. Controls the deepest mineral veins. “Everyone has a price. Find yours.” The Silent (Grind-Teeth) — No name. Doesn’t speak. Makes iron vibrate when fighting. The other Warbosses fear this one.
