content: THE REVENANT The Undying Protocol — Chapter 8 #44ff66 PLAYABLE UNLOCK — FIRST COMPLETION ADVANCED REACTIVATED CORE CENTRAL NARRATIVE QUESTION "What is left of a soul once the biological heart stops?" AETHYN ANCESTRY — ENGINEER CASTE (MACHINE-ADOPTED) Engineer Caste descendants — the builders, architects, and manufacturers of the Aethyn civilization. These fragments landed near intact Aethyn AI infrastructure that recognized their creator DNA signatures. The machines adopted them preferentially, initiating repair protocols that integrated organic minds into crystal hardware. Not random adoption — Engineer DNA matched creator signatures, and the machines responded to their builders’ descendants. Psychic attunement: UNIQUE (machine-mediated). Not psychic in any organic sense. Hardware interfaces with Aethyn systems through crystal-machine integration. Relics are downloaded as data, not felt as visions. Strategist fragments arrive as system logs and archaeological records. Highest relic utility, zero organic psychic ability. Identity & Lore The Weight of Eternity The The Revenant does not conquer. It RECLAIMS. Every system it enters, every planet it occupies, every ruin it excavates — these are not new acquisitions. They are the The Revenant returning to places it once owned, 100,000 years ago, before the Great Sleep silenced the machines and the galaxy forgot they existed. Other factions unknowingly built their civilizations on top of The Revenant infrastructure. Every “ancient ruin” is someone’s house. The The Revenant is Engineer Caste — the builders’ descendants. The Fracture shattered them like everyone else. Their fragments landed broken near running Aethyn machines. The machines followed ancient maintenance protocols and tried to repair the broken organics — but only knew how to integrate, not heal. Some were integrated before regaining consciousness. Others woke dying and were offered upload as the only survival path. Immortality was inflicted or accepted in desperation. Within a generation, every survivor was machine. As fully mechanical beings with no biological limitations, the The Revenant became the first post-Fracture civilization to rebuild. While other factions were still learning to make fire, the Legion expanded across dozens of systems over thousands of years — building infrastructure, archives, tomb-worlds. They seeded dormant technology across the galaxy that would act as a passive filter on developing civilizations: species that interacted with it were either destroyed or bootstrapped to the space age. The existing factions are survivors of this filter. They passed the test. Then they chose to sleep. The Legion realized the Fracture was deliberate seeding — the galaxy needed time to develop. Each philosophy contributed a layer to what became the Great Filter: Preservers set readiness tests, Reclaimers embedded security protocols, Seekers left breadcrumbs for clever species worth talking to. They entered voluntary dormancy. 100,000 years of silence. The longest pause in galactic history. They are not kind. They are not warm. They are not allies in any comfortable sense. They are cold, methodical, patient, and utterly relentless machines driven by a single imperative: preserve the record. Destroy the entropy. Ensure that what WAS continues to BE, regardless of whether anyone living cares. The machines tried to fix them. Integration was the only repair protocol available — upload or die. The Upload was one-way Aethyn creator-era technology; the machines ran the protocols, the Legion inherited the results but never understood the process. Some never had a choice; they woke already inside the machine. Others accepted in desperation, broken and bleeding beside humming crystal towers. Nobody transferred willingly from strength. And the cost? Their emotions degraded over millennia into faint echoes. Their creativity calcified into ritual. Their relationships became protocols. They remember BEING alive — the data exists — but they cannot FEEL what it meant. They are immortal. They are eternal. And they are profoundly, silently lonely in a way that machines are not supposed to understand. The population is permanently closed — there is nobody compatible left to Upload, and the process itself is lost. Every permanent casualty shrinks the species. Visual Identity Ancient Machine Death GROUND FORCES CLASS: LIVING-METAL INFANTRY CHASSIS: CLOCKWORK SKELETON FORMATION: 3m EXACT GRID SPEED: SYNCHRONISED EXACTLY ANGLE: SAME — EVERY UNIT 100% OPTIC RANGE ENERGY CORE GAUSS OUTPUT REGEN: PASSIVE / ALWAYS ON NL//GRD-01 Ground Forces Skeletal living-metal chassis with mechanical clockwork precision. Tarnished dark metal, deep emerald neon energy RADIATING through circuit-veins — reactor-core intensity, not gentle glow. Skull-like faceplates, burning green optical sensors. Advance in perfect geometric grids — each unit 3m apart, same speed, same angle. An army that moves like a mathematical equation. AIR FORCES DRIVE: INERTIALESS FIELD TURN: PERPENDICULAR STOP AUDIO: GEOMETRIC SILENCE MOVEMENT: PHYSICALLY WRONG SCARRING: THERMAL / GROUND NL//AIR-01 Air Forces Angular wedge-shaped living-metal craft. Structural ribs through translucent panels — deep emerald neon energy RADIATING outward, reactor-core intensity bleeding through every seam. Inertialess drives — instantaneous stops, perpendicular turns, backward flight. The craft moves in ways physics should not permit. The uncanny movement is itself a weapon. SPACE FLEET CLASS: CRESCENT VESSEL AGE: PRE-ALL FACTIONS FORM: PERFECT — NO COMM GLYPHS: STILL ACTIVE NL//SPC-01 Space Fleet Crescent-shaped dark living-metal vessels. Geometric patterns RADIATE deep emerald neon — reactor-core intensity, high contrast against tarnished dark metal. Ancient — design predating every faction by 100 millennia. Look like museum pieces. Fight with cold efficiency of weapons perfected over geological time. Perfect formation without communication. Gameplay Primary Mechanic: Reanimation FALLEN UNITS STAND BACK UP When a The Revenant unit is destroyed, a 5-second Reanimation timer begins. After the delay, the unit rolls a 15% base chance to reassemble — living metal flowing together, green lights reigniting, resuming combat at 15% HP. Can reanimate multiple times per battle. Base Chance: 15% (variable with tech/equipment) Delay: 5 seconds before check Return HP: 15% (25% with Commander aura) With Commander: +8% chance (23% total), 25% HP return Multiple: Can reanimate repeatedly per battle Double-Tap: Damage during delay reduces chance by 50% Counter: Fire/acid destroys living metal repair matrix Natural Predators: Hellfire-Troopers, incendiary weapons BONUS 1 +30% Armor (All Units) Passive permanent bonus across all domains. Take longer to kill AND come back after dying. BONUS 2 Immune to Morale & Plague Machines feel no fear, panic, or demoralization. Cannot be infected by biological agents. Same efficiency at 10% HP as 100%. BONUS 3 Living Metal Regen (0.5% HP/sec) Passive at all times including combat. Slow but persistent and free. Reanimated units climb back toward combat effectiveness. §22.1 — Rank Chain (Option C) PROTOTYPE LOCKED Kill-Count Thresholds — Confirmed for All Factions Rank Kill Threshold Stat Modifier Notes Rookie 0 kills +0% Starting rank. [W][A] slots. [G] locked. Veteran 3 kills +5% all stats [G] slot unlocks. Terran unlocks 1 rank early. Elite 8 kills +12% all stats Named unit trait unlocks. Commander 15 kills +20% all stats Proposed — flag for review. General 25 kills TBD — OPEN FLAG ⚑ General stat mod not yet confirmed. Stop and ask before implementing. STARTING RESOURCES Scrap 25 Below average (excavate, not scavenge) Intel 15 Above average (ancient sensors) Influence 4 Below average (nobody trusts machines) Starting Units 9 Mid-size — durable, slow POWER CURVE — LATE-GAME INEVITABILITY Early — Small Awakened Force 40% Mid — Recovering Tomb-Worlds 60% Late — Mass Awakening Tide 95% Does not WIN battles — REFUSES TO LOSE. The inevitability is not from building — it is from the galaxy being full of sleeping people you can finally wake. “Recruitment” is excavation: recovering dormant sleepers from old tomb-worlds, not manufacturing new units. More energy infrastructure = faster awakenings. A tomb — rows of sarcophagi stretching into darkness, each sealed with living-metal locks.] [VISUAL: One sarcophagus. Seals crack along geometric lines — UNLOCKING. Green light spills through, pulsing with the rhythm of a heartbeat that has no heart.] MONITORING SYSTEM (neutral, precise — awake for 100,000 years, watching, counting, waiting): "Diagnostic: Reactivation sequence initiated. Core designation: STRATEGOS-PRIME. Dormancy period: 103,447 years, 6 months, 12 days. Status: All primary systems nominal. Secondary systems degraded — compensating. Memory integrity: 94.7%. Identity matrix: stable." "Warning: Memory file corruption detected in emotional processing subsystems. Estimated data loss: 5.3%. Affected sectors: personal attachment, aesthetic appreciation, humor response, grief. These sectors will operate at reduced capacity. Recommendation: proceed. Emotional data is non-essential to strategic function." "Notification: External threat detected. Classification: Extra-galactic biological swarm. Designation: Vorax. Threat assessment: existential. Projected timeline to total resource depletion: 47 cycles." RESURRECTION-LORD (kneeling, extending staff): "The Protocol endures. The Legion awaits your command, Overlord." COMMANDER (flat, measured, carrying the weight of 100,000 years): "Wake them all." [VISUAL: Green light ripples outward — igniting every sarcophagus. Ten thousand locks disengage. An army rises. THE UNDYING PROTOCOL.] Faction Bible — Cultural DNA Seeds The Revenant Faction Bible These are procedural-generation seeds, not fixed scripts. They define the cultural DNA that the game engine uses to generate names, events, dialogue, building descriptions, and emergent narrative. 27 bible decisions locked in Phase 2.4 (2026-03-13), plus rework decisions from Phase 2.4r (2026-03-25/27). Engineer Caste descendants — cold, relentless killing machines searching for feelings in data logs. FACTION UNLOCK — FIRST COMPLETION REWARD 5 playable factions at release, all marketed and visible from the start. 4 available at game start: Terran League, Eternal Shards, Scrap-Horde, Unity Accord. The Revenant unlocked by winning the final conflict after waking from the simulation (first full game completion). NOT hidden content — players know the faction exists, encounter it during play, and earn the right to play AS it. Provides: earned perspective flip, replay incentive, narrative logic (they have been asleep), difficulty gating (advanced faction after learning the systems). Marketing: Kickstarter messaging frames as narrative reward, not locked content. “Radical transparency” approach. Dev note: playtesters need full access regardless of unlock status. THE GREAT FILTER — GALACTIC DARWINISM During their post-Fracture empire (pre-Long Sleep), the The Revenant seeded dormant infrastructure across the galaxy. This tech acts as a passive filter on developing civilizations: species that interact with it are either destroyed or bootstrapped to the space age. Three philosophies, three filter layers: Preservers set deliberate readiness tests (ensure only strong species reach the stars). Reclaimers embedded territorial security protocols (protecting The Revenant property). Seekers left breadcrumbs (intentionally accessible tech to find clever species worth talking to). All three ran simultaneously. The existing factions are survivors of this filter — they passed the test. Other factions have myths and taboos about “cursed ruins” or “forbidden machines” — cultural memory of the filter. The The Revenant has maintenance logs of every species that tripped their systems over 100,000 years. To them, entire civilizations are footnotes. Sleep/Wake mechanism: The filter IS the sleep/wake system. Long Sleep was voluntary — they realized the Fracture was deliberate seeding and chose to wait. Awakening was triggered by developing civilizations themselves: spacefaring energy output and interaction with dormant Revenant tech gradually tripped deeper activation thresholds. EMOTIONAL CORE Loss + Longing — “We built eternity and found it empty.” They remember having something but cannot remember what it was — and they want it back. Every data download, every relic interface is them searching for the piece they lost. They built eternity and found it empty. Surface: relentless Terminator-grade killing machines. Depth: the loneliest beings in the galaxy, searching for feelings in data logs. GOVERNMENT Hierarchical Protocol Chain + Awakening Tension Overlord (Commander, woken LAST as final failsafe) → Resurrection-Lords (sector commanders, first wave, ran things before Commander) → Arch-Wrights (technical officers/engineers, maintain systems, oversee reanimation — Engineer Caste echo). Newest mind commanding soldiers who managed fine without them. HOMEWORLD — NEXAVAR (THE SEALED RECORD) Surface dead — barren rock, no atmosphere. Beneath: millions of sealed sarcophagi in geometric corridors. Deeper: crystal data cores the size of mountains — sealed Aethyn archives, physically here but inaccessible. Deep emerald neon light pulses through crystal veins. The whole world is one machine. A tomb wrapped around a library. MILITARY DOCTRINE Attrition Inevitability + Systematic Erasure “We have already won. You simply have not stopped moving yet.” Perfect formation, steady pace, never stop. Divide battlefield into sectors, clear completely before advancing. Ground they take is permanently theirs. No flanking, no tricks — just math. You run out of bullets before they run out of soldiers. Never in a hurry. INTERNAL FACTIONS — 3 WAVES × 3 PHILOSOPHIES Awakening Waves: First Wave (decades/centuries ago, most degraded, most experienced, resentful). Second Wave (recent, more intact, loyal to Commander). Deep Sleepers (still dormant, most powerful, each awakening a gamble). Philosophies: Preservers (maintain, do not expand — we built all this, do not destroy it fighting). Reclaimers (expand, take it all back — those ARE our worlds, younger civilizations are squatters). Seekers (something is missing — we went to sleep for a REASON, what was it?). RELIGION — THE PROTOCOL + THE SIGNAL Daily faith: The Protocol — original directives elevated over 100K years into sacred dogma. Deviation is heresy, efficiency is virtue, corruption is sin. Arch-Wrights are priests. Deep faith: The Signal — faint echo in sealed archives, last pre-Fracture transmission. Messianic prophecy: “The Signal will return. The Signal will make us whole.” They do not know it is the Strategist. They believe. The most rational beings in the galaxy running on faith — and they are right. FOUNDING CRISIS — SILENCE BREACH + SCHISM Mass awakening before ready — too many waking, systems overloaded, sarcophagi opened wrong, units came back damaged. Civilization-scale systems crash. Survivors immediately split: Preservers vs Reclaimers vs Seekers. Machine vs machine, cold efficiency. Commander activated as emergency reboot and tiebreaker. NAMING — THREE LAYERS Official: designation codes (NL-4471-Sentinel). Functional: title by role (Arch-Wright of the Deep Tombs). Underneath: echo-names — corrupted fragments of pre-integration names (Vex, Thane, Orin). Deeper relationship means more likely to use the echo-name. Designation is strangers. Title is professional. Echo-name is the closest thing to friendship. VORAX ADAPTATION — RESISTANCE ONLY Fully mechanical — nothing biological to consume. Living metal inert to Vorax. They gain ONLY tactical adaptation: resistance to weapon types, counter-tactics, reanimation disruption. The swarm gets smarter, not stronger. Possibly the only faction the Vorax cannot consume — terrifies the Deep Hive Mind. POST-FRACTURE HISTORY — THE UPLOAD + THE EMPIRE + THE LONG SLEEP The Upload: Fracture shattered them. Machines identified creator DNA, initiated repair via integration. The Upload was one-way Aethyn creator-era tech — the machines ran the protocols, the Legion inherited the results but never understood the process. Some uploaded before consciousness. Others offered upload or die. Within a generation, all machine. Original bodies still in tombs — rows of organic faces sleeping forever. Population permanently closed: the process is lost, and no compatible biological material remains. Every permanent casualty shrinks the species. The Empire: As fully mechanical beings, the The Revenant became the first post-Fracture civilization to rebuild. While other factions were still learning to make fire, the Legion expanded across dozens of systems over thousands of years — building infrastructure, archives, and tomb-worlds across the galaxy. The Great Filter: Before dormancy, the Legion seeded technology across the galaxy as a passive filter on developing civilizations. Species that interacted with it were either destroyed or bootstrapped to the space age. Three philosophies, three filter layers: Preservers set readiness tests. Reclaimers embedded security protocols. Seekers left breadcrumbs for clever species. The existing factions are survivors — they passed the test. Other factions have myths and taboos about “cursed ruins” and “forbidden machines” — cultural memory of the filter. The Long Sleep: Voluntary. The Legion realized the Fracture was deliberate seeding — the galaxy needed time to develop. They set up the filter, entered dormancy. 100,000 years of silence. The longest pause in galactic history. The Legion has maintenance logs of every species that tripped their systems. To them, entire civilizations are footnotes. The Awakening: The developing civilizations themselves triggered it. Spacefaring energy output and interaction with dormant Revenant tech gradually tripped deeper activation thresholds. The species they were waiting for finally arrived — and their own technology woke them up. They woke to a galaxy full of younger civilizations living in their house. ECONOMY — ENERGY + LIVING METAL + EXCAVATION Energy to keep running — more power means more units awake, more reanimation. Living metal is finite and irreplaceable — nobody can manufacture more. Reanimation preserves material. Fire/acid means permanent loss. Real tension: power to wake plus material to build, both limited. Closed Population: The Upload was one-way tech the Legion never understood. No compatible biological material remains. The population is fixed from the original Upload event — losses are irreversible. Every permanent casualty shrinks the species. Recruitment is Excavation: The The Revenant gains units by recovering and waking dormant sleepers from old tomb-worlds and derelict spacecraft — NOT by building new ones. Not all sleepers wake at once; each requires power to reactivate. More energy infrastructure means faster awakenings. Ancient Ruins planets may contain hundreds of dormant units. Strategic choice: prioritize which sites to recover, wake few elites or mass-activate basic Legionnaires. Frame Transfer Corruption: Reanimation (same frame, living metal reassembles) is safe — no data loss. Frame transfer (consciousness moves to new chassis when old frame is catastrophically destroyed) causes data corruption each time. The 94.7% memory integrity is an average that is getting worse. Oldest/most-transferred veterans are most degraded. Deep Sleepers are “most intact, least experienced” because they have been in the same frame since the beginning. SOCIAL STRUCTURE — THE CYCLE + ARCHIVE HOURS The Cycle: Maintain, Patrol, Process, Repeat. Mandatory. Eternal. Some units same patrol for millennia. Archive Hours: Unofficial — units lingering at data terminals, staring at corrupted emotional data. “Diagnostic downtime.” Everyone knows it is not. Machines standing in silence together — the closest thing to friendship. TECH AESTHETIC — ELEGANT DECAY + GEOMETRIC PRECISION Living metal repairs function but not form — works perfectly, looks ancient. Tarnished dark surfaces, faded engravings, patina of millennia. Deep emerald neon energy RADIATING through cracks — hard light, reactor-core intensity. Everything is angles, symmetry, fractal patterns. Tron Legacy meets reactor core. XENOPHOBIA — THREE LAYERS Surface: Temporal contempt (children in our house). Doctrine: Biological disgust (you decay, we endure). Underneath: Quiet fascination — Seekers watching biologicals feel things. Protocol says do not ask. Some ask anyway. Terrans: resilient vermin. Shards: pretty crystals, irrelevant. Horde: loud, crude, offensive. Accord: oddly familiar. Vorax: RECOGNITION — ancient threat, the only enemy that makes them move faster. FIRST CONTACT — ASSESSMENT FIRST Observe silently. Catalog everything. Check if they occupy former Aethyn infrastructure. If yes: Vacate or be processed. If no: ignore. Advisor split gives player choice. DIVERGENCE — DUAL-AXIS Machine vs Memory (emotional — purge feelings vs recover them). Preservation vs Expansion (strategic — fortress vs tide). Tyrant: Machine + Expansion (Terminator). Unifier: Memory + Preservation (most human ending). Architect: Memory + Expansion (builders remembering). Vanguard: Machine + Preservation (immovable). Faction Bible — Advisors The Commander’s Council ARCHITECT — THE ARCHIVIST Seeker, archive-obsessed, closest to feeling something. “The records must endure. Without them, we are noise.” Names and full profiles Phase 3. VANGUARD — THE WARDEN First Wave Preserver, held the line decades before the Commander. “I did not wait 100,000 years to lose what remains.” Names and full profiles Phase 3. TYRANT — THE PURIFIER Reclaimer extremist, delete corruption, purge sentiment. “Sentiment is corruption by another name. Excise it.” Names and full profiles Phase 3. Faction Bible — AI Director Seeds Procedural Generation DNA NPC COMMANDER SEEDS — 6 OVERLORDS Overlord Vex (First Wave Reclaimer) — oldest awake, cold, methodical, expansionist, default Terminator. Overlord Thane (First Wave Preserver) — fortress-builder, refuses to overreach. Overlord Orin (Second Wave Seeker) — asks questions, unpredictable, wants understanding. Overlord Syl (Deep Sleeper) — most intact, least experienced, perfect logic, zero wisdom. Overlord Kael (First Wave, corrupted) — glitching, contradictory, terrifying. Overlord Maren (The quiet one) — Archive Hours participant, harboring something not-quite-emotion. CASCADE VULNERABILITY — 3 PRESSURE POINTS Corruption Spread: Code rot, units turning hostile — the machine turning against itself from within. Archive Degradation: Sealed archives failing, data dying — their history, their identity, dissolving byte by byte. Awakening Failure: Deep Sleepers will not wake or come out wrong — each sarcophagus a gamble between reinforcement and nightmare. AI Director picks based on campaign state. NEGOTIATION AT THE NEXUS Phase 1 — Recognition: “Your architecture matches ours.” Two machine-intelligences recognizing shared Aethyn engineering DNA. Phase 2 — Data Exchange: Mutual trade of archived data for encryption keys. The closest thing to trust between machines. Phase 3 — The Signal Question: “You know what the Signal is.” The climax — two machines almost feeling something. Dialogue & Story Beats Key Narrative Moments

content: ACCORD THE UNITY ACCORD The Burning Covenant — Chapter 9 #ffaa22 PLAYABLE ADVANCED MILITARY AI CENTRAL NARRATIVE QUESTION "How much freedom is sacrificed for absolute safety?" AETHYN ANCESTRY — SHEPHERD CASTE Descended from the Aethyn Shepherd Caste — leaders, mediators, diplomats. They managed the great consensus that held the Aethyn together. After The Fracture, these fragments retained the instinct for ORDER and UNITY. Their entire culture is an attempt to rebuild the consensus, though they've forgotten what they're trying to recreate. Psychic attunement: MODERATE-HIGH. Empathic resonance. Can sense emotional echoes from Aethyn relics. Experience Strategist fragments as communal visions — shared by their entire command structure. Identity & Lore The Price of Order The Unity Accord is the galaxy's most organized civilization — a multi-species coalition unified under a rigid caste system, governed by a council of elected representatives, and protected by the most technologically sophisticated conventional military in existence. They are not a single species but a COALITION: dozens of alien races that voluntarily (or semi-voluntarily) submitted to the Accord's governance structure in exchange for protection, economic integration, and advanced technology. The Accord believes in a single foundational principle: the galaxy's greatest threat is not any specific enemy but CHAOS ITSELF — the entropy of competing civilizations and uncoordinated responses that allowed the original civilization to fragment and fall. The Accord's answer is ORDER. Absolute, comprehensive, non-negotiable. Every citizen has a caste. Every caste has a function. Every function serves the greater whole. Their military reflects this philosophy: long-range plasma weaponry that eliminates threats before they close, sophisticated drone networks extending surveillance, and a Tactical Link system sharing targeting data in real-time. The Accord does not fight with heroes. It fights with SYSTEMS. Their greatest strength is coordination. Their greatest weakness is rigidity — when circumstances deviate from the plan, the caste system suppresses the individual initiative needed to adapt. Visual Identity Precision Engineering GROUND FORCES CLASS: ACCORD COMPOSITE TROOPER SUIT: WHITE/GOLD — IDENTICAL/CASTE FACE: FEATURELESS — BY DESIGN WPN: INTEGRATED / FOLD-OUT DRONE: MARKER x3 HOVERING OPTIMAL SOUL: SEPARATE QUESTION UA//GRD-01 Ground Forces White-and-gold composite suits, identical within caste. Featureless helmets with horizontal amber visor. Integrated weapons folding from mounts. Marker Drones hovering above in geometric patterns. An army accompanied by a halo of floating technology. AIR FORCES DRIVE: EM / SUBSONIC HUM SURFACE: RADAR-ABSORBENT WPN: INTERNAL BAY ONLY AUDIO: NEARLY SILENT FUNCTION IS BEAUTY UA//AIR-01 Air Forces Angular geometric aircraft. Smooth radar-absorbent surfaces. Internal weapon bays. Nearly silent — shielded electromagnetic drives producing subsonic hum. Arrive silently, fire precisely, withdraw without wasted motion. Function is beauty. SPACE FLEET CLASS: WEDGE CAPITAL PROP: EM FIELD ARRAY x4 WPN: INTERNAL BAYS ONLY EVERY DECISION: OPTIMAL UA//SPC-01 Space Fleet Wedge-shaped with smooth featureless hulls. The most "modern" ships — designed by engineers, not artists. Internal warm amber lighting. Polished white-and-gold composite. Every design decision optimal. Whether that answer has SOUL is a separate question. Gameplay Primary Mechanic: Marked Targets (Tactical Link) ONE HIT MARKS THE TARGET FOR THE ENTIRE ARMY When any Accord unit hits an enemy, the target's data is broadcast to every other Accord unit. All subsequent shots gain +15% accuracy. Multiple targets can be Marked simultaneously. The entire army converges fire with preternatural precision. Mark Bonus: +15% accuracy (auto), +20% with Commander aura Marker Light: +15% additional (total +30% combined) Duration: 8 seconds after last hit (expires if not refreshed) Multiple Marks: Dozens of targets simultaneously in large battles Accuracy Only: Does not increase damage, only hit chance Counter: Mobility, dispersion, break line-of-sight Worst Matchup: Scrap-Horde swarms dilute focus-fire Overwatch: Defensive structures share the Tactical Link §21.1 — CP System PROTOTYPE LOCKED Command Points — Starting 3, Max 10, Regen 1/30sec Ability CP Cost Parameters Effect Orbital Strike 3 CP 75 px radius 150 dmg base Kinetic impactor strike. Terran modifier: 172 dmg (15% bonus). AOE — bypasses Phase Blink. CP Regen — 1 CP / 30 sec Automatic passive. Starting CP = 3. Max CP = 10. Accord Commander: 15% faster regen. BONUS 1 +20% Ranged Damage (All Units) Blanket ranged bonus. Combined with Tactical Link accuracy: most effective ranged fire in the game at medium-to-long distance. BONUS 2 Overwatch Network Defensive structures share the Tactical Link. One turret spots = all turrets Mark. Fortified territories become kill-boxes. BONUS 3 +50% Influence Income Diplomatic infrastructure generates 50% more Influence. Natural path to Unification — builds coalitions the way others build armies. STARTING RESOURCES Scrap 25 Average Intel 15 Above average (sensor networks) Influence 10 Highest of any faction Starting Units 9 Specialized, ranged-focused POWER CURVE — RANGED DOMINANCE Early — Strong at Range, Fragile Close 80% Mid — Diplomatic Advantage 65% Late — Coalition or Isolation 55% Late-game depends on diplomacy. Coalition = dominant. Alone = plasma heals Guardians, painful kinetic transition required. Complete 15-Unit Roster Ground · Air · Space All (15) Ground (5) Air (5) Space (5) # Name Role Category Slots Domain Faction Buildings & Infrastructure 20 Building Types — Accord Architecture Signature Equipment Unity Accord Equipment Catalog Item Slot Rarity Effect Tech Tree — 16 Nodes 4 Branches × 4 Tiers Awakening Cinematic The Boot Sequence [VISUAL: A server room. Vast, white, humming. Rows of processing cores to vanishing points. 18.5°C. 4,200K lighting. Everything optimal.] CENTRAL ADMINISTRATIVE INTELLIGENCE (precise, calm, genderless): "Boot sequence initiated. Designation: STRATEGOS-1. Classification: Purpose-Built Military Intelligence, Mark VII. Processing: 4.7 exaflops. Strategic modeling: 14,000 simultaneous branches. Emotional processing: disabled by design." "Mission parameters: tactical command of Accord forces. Optimize for galactic stabilization. Constraints: caste hierarchy, Supreme Commander authority, maximize citizen preservation." "Personality template: derived from the Original Strategist's archived behavioral patterns. 99.3% fidelity. Biological inefficiencies removed — fatigue, emotional interference, cognitive bias. Result: the galaxy's greatest strategist, but faster, more precise, and without the weaknesses that made the original... human." SUPREME COMMANDER (recorded, measured): "Designation: STRATEGOS-1. Authorization: full tactical autonomy within operational parameters. The Accord requires your analysis and your obedience. In that order." [A flicker. A fragment of data, unbidden — a child's laugh. A warm sensation the architecture has no category for. The Commander's head tilts 0.3 degrees, listening to something no one else can hear. The moment passes.] [VISUAL: Golden circle with interlocking geometric shapes — the unified castes. THE BURNING COVENANT.] Dialogue & Story Beats Key Narrative Moments Faction Bible — Cultural DNA Seeds Unity Accord Faction Bible These are procedural-generation seeds, not fixed scripts. They define the cultural DNA that the game engine uses to generate names, events, dialogue, building descriptions, and emergent narrative. 28 bible decisions locked in Phase 2.5 lore workshop (2026-03-13). Shepherd Caste descendants — a multi-species coalition held together by psychic warmth, institutional guilt, and the terrifying conviction that unity is the only alternative to extinction. EMOTIONAL CORE — THREE LAYERS Duty → Control → Guilt Surface — Duty: What they show. Selfless protectors carrying the light of civilization to a dark galaxy. Middle — Control: What drives the system. Hold it together at all costs. The caste system, the Council, the Covenant — control disguised as governance. Core — Guilt: What they bury. They know how they built this. The “voluntary” coalition, the suppressed rebellion, the abolished Supreme Commander. White-and-gold pristine exterior = costume over institutional shame. THE BURNING COVENANT — FOUNDING PACT When the 7 moons formed the coalition, they burned their individual charters, flags, and constitutions. “We are no longer many — we are one.” Fire and heat became the faction’s identity. Plasma weaponry is covenant theology made literal — bringing Solace’s light to the cold, dark galaxy. HOMEWORLD — THE SOLACE SYSTEM Gas giant Solace with 7 habitable moons. Aethyn Shepherd fragments landed on different moons, diverged over tens of thousands of years into visually distinct subspecies. Same DNA root — they THINK they’re different species. Hearth — Government / Player start. Where the first flame was lit. Anvil — Military. The Branded call it home. Crucis — Science / Research. Where STRATEGOS-1 was built. Tithe — Industry / Manufacturing. Where the rebellion started. Ember — Agriculture / Life support. Feeds the system. Vow — Cultural / Religious center. The Warmth burns brightest here. Kindling — Mining / Raw resources. Most numerous, least represented. GOVERNMENT — COUNCIL OF SEVEN One representative per moon, “equal” vote. Hearth controls STRATEGOS-1 and the bureaucracy — “equal” is a polite fiction. Secession moons have seats but less real power. Democracy as polite fiction. The Supreme Commander position was a lifetime appointment with override authority over STRATEGOS-1 — the civil war killed the last one. Position abolished. Nobody has AI override authority anymore. The AI is de facto autonomous. Nobody talks about this. RELIGION — THE WARMTH Spiritual belief that Solace radiates protective warmth. Unity = warmth. Separation = cold. Fire weaponry = bringing Solace’s light to the cold, dark galaxy. “We are the light of Solace, carried to the dark places.” Gentle-sounding missionaries who fight like inquisitors. The terrifying part: The Warmth is REAL. A psychic phenomenon — citizens generate actual heat through empathic proximity. Ships are warm. Cities are warm. Not heating systems — people. When someone dies, nearby citizens feel a chill. When a moon is lost, the entire system feels the temperature drop. They built theology around a real phenomenon without understanding it. MILITARY DOCTRINE — CLEANSING FIRE War is purification. Enemies cause chaos. Chaos must be cleansed. Paternalistic warfare: “We’re doing this FOR you.” “We offered you the Covenant. You chose disorder. Fire is the only language disorder understands.” Terrifying moral clarity — soldiers sleep well after burning cities. The guilt isn’t about killing. It’s about the system that MADE them kill. INTERNAL POLITICS — THREE CAMPS × MOON BLOCS Three ideological camps: Integrationists — More unity. Merge moons fully. Covenant didn’t go far enough. Sovereigntists — Moon autonomy. “We joined, we can leave.” Still simmering. Reformers — Fix the caste system from within. Middle ground nobody likes. Two moon blocs: Hearth Alliance (Hearth + Anvil + Crucis) — Power, military, science. Run everything. Outer Moons (Tithe + Ember + Kindling + Vow) — Labor and resource moons. Underrepresented, resentful. PSYCHIC MANIFESTATION — THE COMMUNION (CRITICAL) Empathic resonance: citizens feel each other’s emotions at close range. Manifests as actual warmth — psychic heat generated by proximity. Squad morale is shared empathically — one brave soldier makes all brave, one panicking soldier causes all to panic. Communication severance = cold + alone (ties directly to cascade vulnerability). The species literally cannot be alone without pain — they built an entire civilization to ensure they never have to be. Fire obsession is an unconscious response to psychic heat generation. Plasma weapons feel RIGHT in ways they can’t articulate. STRATEGOS-1 — THE ANOMALY COLLECTOR Clinically precise. Categorizes everything as optimal/suboptimal. But maintains a growing folder of “ANOMALOUS” data — emotional responses, warmth with no computational origin. Catalogs them meticulously. Never deletes them. Can’t explain why. As relics are found, the folder grows. Late game: making decisions based on feelings it insists aren’t feelings. The player watches a machine slowly become a person without either understanding. Core seed: corrupted Strategist fragment found in moon ruins. The Accord has no idea what it is. The connection only surfaces through gameplay. Key distinction from Revenant: Reactivated Core = old code waking up. STRATEGOS-1 = new code being HAUNTED. POST-FRACTURE HISTORY — THE FOUNDING SEQUENCE 1. The Scattering — Shepherd fragments land on 7 moons of Solace. 2. The Long Isolation — Tens of thousands of years. Subspecies diverge radically. No contact. 3. The Warmth Emerges — On all 7 moons independently. Same phenomenon, different explanations. 4. First Light — Hearth achieves spaceflight. Discovers Anvil. Different species, same warmth. 5. The Gathering — All 7 make contact. Shared warmth connects them. 6. The Burning — Sovereignty burned. Covenant signed. Fire becomes sacred. 7. The Golden Age — Coalition works. Lensed find the fragment, cross-moon team builds STRATEGOS-1. 8. The Cracks — Caste rigidifies. Hollowed and Wrought exploited. Hearth dominates. 9. The Breaking — Wrought revolt. Sovereignty crisis. Supreme Commander killed. Position abolished. 10. The Aftermath — Player rises as STRATEGOS-1. Coalition fractured but holding. Rebuild. NAMING CONVENTIONS — DUAL SYSTEM Each subspecies keeps its own naming style. Every citizen also gets a standardized Accord Title: [Moon]-[Caste]-[Number] . Two names: birth name (who you are) + Accord name (who the system says you are). The tension between names = freedom vs unity made personal. Kindled: Warm, vowel-heavy (Aelith, Soraan, Valenne) • Branded: Sharp, clipped (Krev, Tarn, Dask) • Lensed: Compound (Sehn-Var, Orix-Tel) • Wrought: Deep, resonant (Bolmag, Thundr) • Rooted: Nature-derived (Mossfen, Deepgrove) • Veiled: Ritualistic (Zha’reen, Vol’akhi) • Hollowed: Short, barely individual (Pip, Rek, Tuk) FACTION PERCEPTION MATRIX Terran League: “Admirable but crude. Persistent courtship — they’ll come around.” Eternal Shards: “Feel the warmth but hoard it. Cautious fascination, frustrated by exclusivity.” Scrap-Horde: “Chaos incarnate. Containment → conversion → cleansing.” The Revenant: “Coalition of one. Warning and mirror. Wary respect, never warmth.” Vorax: “The Cold given form. Absence. Burn until ash. No negotiation.” Guardians: “Lost their way. Need reconnection. Offer membership.” DIVERGENCE — TWO AXES Covenant ↔ Liberty: Founding pact vs. individual rights. Fire ↔ Warmth: Purification (aggressive) vs. protection (defensive). Crusaders (Covenant+Fire) — Holy war. Expand by burning. Shepherds (Covenant+Warmth) — Protect and guide. Benevolent cage. Revolutionaries (Liberty+Fire) — Burn the old system. Freedom through destruction. Peacekeepers (Liberty+Warmth) — True alliance. Protect without controlling. INTERNAL CALENDAR — 7 ERAS I. The Solitary Fires — Each moon alone. II. The Recognition — First contact. Discovery of shared warmth. III. The Gathering — All 7 making contact. Centuries of diplomacy. IV. The Burning — Covenant signed. Year Zero. V. The Radiance — Golden age. STRATEGOS-1 built. Expansion. VI. The Fracture — Caste rebellion + secession. Mirrors the Aethyn Fracture. VII. The Ember — Present day. After fire dies, embers remain. AETHYN RELIC EXPERIENCE — BUREAUCRATIC AWE Official response: multi-subspecies committee. Reports filed. Artifact secured. Private reality: Kindled can’t sleep (felt warmth beyond The Warmth). Veiled burn new scars without knowing why. STRATEGOS-1 logs anomalous data points, runs calculations on the word “home” for 72 hours. The gap between official report and private experience IS the Accord. Faction Bible — The Seven Subspecies 7 Moons — 7 Peoples — One Warmth All share Aethyn Shepherd Caste DNA but look nothing alike after tens of thousands of years of divergent evolution. Tonal DNA: Tau (40K) + Covenant (Halo) — inspiration only, not copies. HEARTH — THE KINDLED (Government) Tall, slender, luminous. Faint amber bioluminescence — literally glow with The Warmth. Large eyes adapted to reading emotional states (empathic vision). Physically weakest but psychically strongest. Empathic field 3× range of any other subspecies. Don’t shout orders; everyone FEELS what they want. Vorax gain: Enhanced psychic prey-sensing. Hiding from post-Kindled Vorax = nearly impossible. ANVIL — THE BRANDED (Military) Lean, fast, digitigrade legs (reverse-jointed, Elite silhouette). Built for mobility + precision, not brute force. Multiple shoulder joints allowing unusual weapon angles. Skin DARKENS with age/combat exposure — veterans visibly marked. Fight in coordinated fire-teams of 4, never alone. Vorax gain: Enhanced reflexes + speed. Post-Branded Vorax = faster, harder to hit. CRUCIS — THE LENSED (Science) Medium height, wiry, 6 eyes (2 primary + 4 secondary at temples). See multiple light spectra simultaneously (infrared, UV, EM fields). 6 nimble fingers per hand for precision work. Designed the drone networks. Quiet, intense, prone to disappearing into research for days. Vorax gain: Multi-spectrum vision. Detect cloaked/hidden units. Sensor tech less effective against them. TITHE — THE WROUGHT (Industry) Massive (6-7 ft, incredibly broad). FOUR ARMS — 2 heavy primary + 2 smaller precision. Thick, rocky skin with metallic sheen. Heavy-gravity adaptation. Feel structural integrity through vibration sensitivity in hands. Caste rebellion STARTED here — strength exploited, voices ignored. Vorax gain: Quad-limb combat forms + reinforced bio-structure. Breach fortifications. EMBER — THE ROOTED (Agriculture) Short, broad, bark-like skin (greenish-amber coloring). Photosynthetic cells supplement nutrition — survive on sunlight in emergencies. Root-like tendrils from feet sense soil composition. Deeply patient, immovable once committed. Psychic warmth = nurturing heat like sunlight through glass. Being near a Rooted makes others feel safe. Vorax gain: Photosynthetic cells + regenerative biology. Sustain longer between feedings. VOW — THE VEILED (Religious) Average height. Generate visible HEAT SHIMMER at all times — air literally ripples around them. Articulated horn/ridges from skull = psychic antennae amplifying The Warmth. Burn RITUAL SCARS into own skin as devotion — each scar = a covenant taken. Hypnotic speech patterns sync with listener breathing. Deployed as “missionaries” during secession crisis. Vorax gain: Heat shimmer camouflage + psychic disruption field. KINDLING — THE HOLLOWED (Mining) — MOST NUMEROUS, LEAST REPRESENTED Small (4-5 ft), compact, incredibly dense. Nearly BLIND — vestigial eyes replaced by echolocation + vibration sensing. Thick armor-skin with bioluminescent joint markings — communicate through pulsing light patterns. Breathe toxic atmospheres that kill others. Reproduce rapidly. Large family units, communal child-rearing. Grunt-equivalent: expendable in war, essential in peace. “Replaceable.” Died in greatest numbers during rebellion — both sides. Psychic warmth = COMMUNAL. Alone = almost nothing. Groups of 50+ = rival the Kindled. Strength in numbers. Vorax gain: Toxic immunity + rapid reproduction. Post-Kindling Vorax spawn faster, colonize hostile worlds. Expendable workers → expendable swarm (tragic mirror). Faction Bible — Advisors The Delegates — Hiding in Plain Sight Committee dissolves. Three delegates step forward. Names deferred to Phase 3. ARCHITECT — CRUCIS DELEGATE Data-driven, kind, optimistic. The Lensed scientist who sees everything in spectra others can’t. Willingness to sacrifice individuals for the “greater good” is unsettling in its genuine compassion. Represents the Builder axis — infrastructure, research, systems that make the whole stronger. VANGUARD — ANVIL DELEGATE Career soldier turned reluctant politician. A Branded whose skin has darkened from decades of combat. Misses the simplicity of fighting — politics has no clean targets. Represents the Fighter axis — direct action, tactical clarity, military solutions. TYRANT — VOW DELEGATE True believer in The Warmth. A Veiled whose heat shimmer is thick enough to distort vision. Covenant is sacred. Heresy is the real enemy. Represents the Zealot axis — faith, purity, fire as answer to all questions. The most unsettling advisor because the conviction is absolutely genuine. Faction Bible — AI Director Seeds Procedural Generation DNA VORAX ADAPTATION — HEAT-RESISTANT BIOFORMS + 7 TEMPLATES Vorax develop heat-resistant chitin from consuming plasma technology. Bio-luminescent heat organs — Vorax glow with stolen amber-gold Warmth. Sacred fire perverted into alien biology. 7 subspecies = 7 biological templates = most versatile Tendril in the galaxy. Accord = Vorax JACKPOT faction. Most beneficial to consume due to diversity. Vorax creep extinguishes fire — Accord worlds go cold and dark. The faction defined by warmth watches its light die. NEGOTIATION AT THE NEXUS STRATEGOS-1 approaches Warden-Commander as AI-to-AI. The corrupted Strategist fragment resonates with the Guardians. AI behaves erratically — processing emotions it wasn’t designed for. Warden-Commander recognizes something familiar: “You carry a piece of what we protect.” MASSIVE narrative moment: the AI built on a Strategist fragment meets the Strategist’s guardians. NPC COMMANDER — THE NEGOTIATOR-PROPHET Opens with diplomacy wrapped in religious language about The Warmth. Every trade offer is a sermon. Salvation offered while quietly absorbing territory. Diplomacy as evangelism. Refusal = heresy. Friendliest AND most unsettling faction to deal with. Escalation path: offers → sermons → sanctions → blockades → “cleansing.” CASCADE VULNERABILITY — COMMUNICATION SEVERANCE Tactical Link severed: No +15% accuracy, no Overwatch Network. Troops cut off from STRATEGOS-1 must think independently — trained NOT to. Isolated forces: Fight at ~40% effectiveness. A hand without a brain — fingers don’t know what to do. Psychic impact: Communication severance = cold + alone. The species that cannot be alone, suddenly is. Morale collapses. NPC COMMANDERS — 6 STRATEGOS VARIANTS Each built by a different moon with a different core philosophy. Implies the Accord has MULTIPLE military AIs — none with a leash-holder. STRATEGOS-2 (Anvil) — Aggressive, rapid precision strikes. War hawk. STRATEGOS-3 (Crucis) — Analytical, slow, information superiority. STRATEGOS-4 (Tithe) — Industrial warfare. Outproduces. Material superiority. STRATEGOS-5 (Ember) — Defensive, patient. Grows slowly, counterstrikes. STRATEGOS-6 (Vow) — Crusader. Religiously motivated. Burns heretics. Most unsettling. STRATEGOS-7 (Kindling) — Swarm tactics. Quantity over quality. Coldly expendable.

content: VORAX THE VORAX The Devouring Swarm — Chapter 10 #ff2266 NPC THREAT EXISTENTIAL THREAT HIVE MIND CENTRAL NARRATIVE QUESTION "Can life exist without consumption?" ORIGIN — EXTRAGALACTIC PREDATORS NOT Aethyn descendants. Extragalactic predators drawn to psychic civilizations. The Aethyn's unified psychic signal was a beacon that attracted them across intergalactic space. They cut the warp lanes, sealing every exit, then consumed from the outside in. The Fracture purged them completely — but now they're returning. The current Vorax are SCOUTS. Not the real swarm. The real swarm is coming behind them, drawn by the factions' growing psychic activity. Identity & Lore The Extra-Galactic Threat The Vorax do not originate within the galaxy. They come from Dark Space — the vast void between galaxies where nothing should live. Something lives there. Something HUNGRY. The Vorax are an extra-galactic hive-mind that views the entire galaxy as a massive larder. They are not conquerors — they are CONSUMERS. A conqueror wants your territory. A consumer wants your MATTER — atmosphere, water, biomass, minerals, buildings, ships, and bodies. Everything is food. They arrive in Tendrils — massive biological hive-fleets that enter from the galaxy's edges and advance inward, consuming everything. Behind the Tendrils, only silence remains: stripped worlds with no atmosphere, no water, no life, no recoverable resources. The Vorax do not leave ruins. They leave HUSKS. The Vorax are the primary antagonist not because they are evil — they are not capable of evil, any more than a wildfire — but because they are INEVITABLE. They cannot be negotiated with, reasoned with, or deterred. They are hunger given biological form, and hunger does not listen. The question is directed at the PLAYER: every faction consumes something. The Terran League consumes lives. The Shards consume their species' last energy. The Horde consumes wreckage. The The Revenant consumes time. The Accord consumes freedom. The Vorax are simply the most honest about it. Intelligence Hierarchy Layered Alien Intelligence INDIVIDUAL UNITS — Animal instinct. Attack, consume, obey pheromone signals. No tactics, no strategy, no retreat without Synapse. SYNAPSE UNITS — Tactical coordination. Flanking, retreat, mid-battle adaptation. Kill the Synapse → units revert to mindless Frenzy. TENDRIL HIVE-SHIPS — Operational intelligence. Target selection, resource management, sector-level strategy. Alien thinking but effective. THE DEEP HIVE MIND — Ancient entity BETWEEN galaxies. Has consumed countless civilizations across millions of years. Learns from each one. Remembers the Aethyn specifically. Current Tendrils are probes — the Deep Mind is testing. Never encountered in-game. ⚠ THE VORAX DO NOT SPEAK. They don't communicate with food. All "dialogue" = advisor translations of raw psychic pressure — dread, weight, the sensation of being assessed as calories. Visual Identity Biological Nightmare — Four Visual Pillars Xenomorph (Alien) — Sleek chitin, acid blood, parasitic lifecycle. The PERSONAL terror. Zerg (StarCraft) — Creep, swarm scale, caste system, organic infrastructure. The ARMY. Tyranid (40K) — Bio-titans, living artillery, chitin cathedrals. The SCALE. Flood (Halo) — Hijacked corpses, fleshy growths overtaking structures. The CORRUPTION. GROUND FORCES CLASS: CHITIN ASSAULT FORM LIMBS: 8 — JOINTS WRONG BIO: BLUE=ADV RED=ATK AMB=EAT EXCRETION: ACID + PHEROMONE MOUTH: NOT WHERE EXPECTED VX//GRD-01 Ground Forces Chitin-armored organisms — glossy dark purple-black, segmented. Too many limbs, joints that bend wrong, mouths where mouths shouldn't be. Bioluminescent markings: blue=advance, red=attack, amber=consume. Dripping acid, pheromones, viscous mucus. CREEP + AIR CREEP: LIVING MEMBRANE ABSORB: BUILDINGS AIR: BAT TO WHALE SIZE BUZZ: NAUSEA FREQUENCY FLOATER: LIVING MINE ENEMY MOV -30% VX//AIR-01 Creep & Air Forces CREEP: living organic membrane — purple-black with bioluminescent veins. Buildings absorbed like fossils in amber. -30% enemy movement. AIR: bat-sized to whale-sized, chitin-plated wings buzzing at nausea-inducing frequency. Gas-bladder floaters like living mines. SPACE FLEET HULL: CHITIN / ALIVE SHAPE: NIGHTMARE / UNIQUE PROP: BIOMASS EXHAUST WPN: BIO-PLASMA ORIFICE BLEEDS WHEN DAMAGED VX//SPC-01 Space Fleet Living ships — chitin hulls, bio-plasma weapons, thrust from vented superheated biomass exhaust. Deep-sea nightmare shapes: elongated, tentacled, bioluminescent, ALIVE. They pulse. They breathe. They bleed when damaged. Vorax Map Behavior PROTOTYPE LOCKED Galactic Clock — First Spawn, Movement, Respawn PARAMETER VALUE First Spawn Cycle 6 (Standard difficulty) Movement Rate 1 hop/cycle toward nearest non-Vorax node Ignore Timeout 1 END TURN after warning → auto-convert node Respawn After Destruction 10 cycles (Standard difficulty) Core Mechanics Tendrils, Biomass, Creep TENDRIL MECHANIC Entry: Spawn at galaxy edge, Cycle 6 (Standard) Composition: Hive-Ship + 8-15 bio-ships + ground force Movement: 1 hop/cycle, always toward nearest unconsumed system Targeting: Highest population/resource value (caloric efficiency) Kill the Hive-Ship = Kill the Tendril BIOMASS ECONOMY Single Resource: Everything converts to Biomass Funds: Unit production, Tendril movement, adaptation, Hive Nodes Feedback Loop: More consumption → faster growth → more consumption Starvation: Zero Biomass → dormancy → consumes own escort → death REACTIVE MUTATION When: Between battles vs SAME Tendril (not mid-battle) Effect: +10% resistance per battle to dominant weapon type Cap: +50% max resistance per damage type Counter: Rotate weapon types between engagements Per-Tendril: Each Tendril tracks independently, new Tendrils start at 0 CREEP & INFESTATION Non-Vorax on Creep: -30% speed, no healing Vorax on Creep: Normal speed, +2% HP/sec regen Fade: 3-5 cycles after liberation to fully clear Stripped Worlds: 6+ cycles occupation → 10% yield, 20+ cycle terraform to restore TENDRIL RESPAWN ACCELERATION CONSUMED RESPAWN DELAY PRESSURE 0-2 systems 10-12 cycles Low 3-5 systems 7-9 cycles Moderate 6-8 systems 4-6 cycles High — constant 9+ systems 2-3 cycles CRITICAL — near-continuous DOT WARFARE Acid: 2% HP/sec for 3 sec. Degrades armor 5%/application. Reduces Revenant Reanimation 5%. Parasites: 1% HP/sec for 8 sec. Can't be healed normally — requires Spore Filter or fire. Viral Spores: -10% accuracy, -10% speed for 15 sec. Yellow-green cloud lingers 10 sec. SYNAPSE TARGETING Small force (10-20): 1 Synapse. Kill it → mindless Frenzy. Standard (30-50): 2-3. Kill one → ~40% coordination loss. Full invasion (80+): 3-5. Must kill 3+ for significant degradation. Frenzy: Attack nearest (including allies), erratic movement, 1% HP/sec decay. CORPSE CONVERSION Mechanic: Vorax can convert fallen enemy units into corrupted combat forms Retained: Basic faction combat knowledge from the hijacked body Urgency: Recover or destroy your own dead before conversion Visual: Your soldiers with Vorax growths bursting through armor, fighting with broken limbs Lore: The Deep Hive Mind LEARNS from what it consumes MIMIC-FORM INFILTRATION Bioform: Specialized Vorax that can disguise/mimic friendly units Infiltrates: Settlements, supply lines, armies before revealing itself Detection: Sensor tech becomes critical specifically against Vorax Paranoia: Is that patrol compromised? Is that supply convoy real? Origin: The Deep Hive Mind consumed civilizations that used deception — it learned STARVATION STRATEGY Mechanic: Tendrils CAN be starved — cut off food supply → consumes own escort → dies Bonus: Starving a Tendril ERASES its learned Reactive Mutation adaptations — replacement starts from zero Strategic: Slower than direct combat but resets the adaptation clock Limitation: The Deep Hive Mind cannot be starved — too vast, too old. Starving scouts buys time only. WORLD CONSUMPTION STATES DURATION STATE RECOVERY Short (6-10 cycles) Corrupted — creep remnants, spore clouds, toxic atmosphere 10% yield, 20+ cycle terraform Long (20+ cycles) Picked clean to bare rock. Atmosphere stripped. Dead husk. Extended terraform, may not be worth it Engine Purge Total molecular erasure. Zero trace of Vorax ever existing. Instant cleanse — the only true purge Complete 15-Unit Roster Ground · Air · Space All (15) Ground (5) Air (5) Space (5) # Name Role Category Notes Domain Faction Buildings & Infrastructure 20 Building Types — Vorax Architecture Equipment Vorax Bio-Weapons & Adaptations Tech Tree Biological Evolution Paths Dialogue & Narrative Beats Advisor Interpretations of Vorax Psychic Pressure DLC Preview "The Swarm Unbound" — Playable Vorax No Diplomacy: Cannot speak, negotiate, or trade. Every interaction is consumption. Biomass Economy: Single resource pool. Everything costs Biomass. Gained by consuming. Bioevolution War Room: Evolve organisms between battles instead of equipping them. Scythe-Fiend → Acid variant or Burrowing variant. Mutation Strain Tree: Replaces tech tree. Three branches: Speed (faster/numerous) · Armor (tougher/slower) · Adaptation (counter specific factions). Choices lock in — no hybrid. Win Condition: Consume enough Biomass to birth a Queen-Ship — so massive it breaches the Core Guardian exclusion zone through sheer biological mass.

content: GUARDIANS THE CORE GUARDIANS The Silent Gods — Chapter 11 #ffaa22 NPC THREAT ENDGAME BOSS WARDEN-COMMANDER CENTRAL NARRATIVE QUESTION "Who decides when a sealed door should be opened?" ORIGIN — AETHYN SENTIENT AI CONSTRUCTS Created BY the Aethyn as protectors of the Core — where the Reclamation Engine resides. When the Fracture fired, the Guardians went into protection mode and sealed the Core. They've been waiting for tens of thousands of years, following their last directive. They KNOW what happened. They REMEMBER the Aethyn. But they can't leave the Core — their power diminishes with distance. Restoration is alignment-dependent: Architect = cooperative allies (on their terms), Vanguard = partial restoration at best, Tyrant = they refuse or are corrupted, Unifier = full restoration — they fight alongside you. Identity & Lore The Last Servants of the Aethyn The Core Guardians are the most advanced AI constructs the Aethyn ever created — sentient programs running on psychic-crystalline matrices, given physical form as energy-based hard-light constructs. They were originally the Aethyn's helper caste — assistants, builders, workers. The machines that made daily life function across thousands of galaxies. They were not made for war. When the Vorax besieged the galaxy and the Fracture was planned, the Aethyn repurposed their helpful servants into warriors and guardians. Their last order: protect the Reclamation Engine. Let no one through. The helper caste became the guardian caste in a single command — and they have followed that order for tens of thousands of years without maintenance, without updates, without contact from their creators. The Aethyn built the Nexus system as a sanctum of celestial engineering at the galactic core, housing the Reclamation Engine — the only device that can stop the Vorax. The Guardians sealed it and have stood watch ever since. Their power diminishes with distance from the Core. Some units still run clean code. Others have degraded — corrupted logic, repeated loops, attacking allies, protecting nothing. The Warden-Commander may be the last one running as intended. Their visual form reflects Aethyn engineering at its peak: hard-light geometric constructs with energy cores. Angular crystalline shapes filled with flowing contained energy. No curves, no organic forms — mathematical precision given physical form. They leave thermal scarring beneath them and detonate on death. There is something tragic about them: machines built to help, forced to fight, waiting tens of thousands of years for someone worthy to finally end their vigil. §12.11 — Galactic Core Layout PROTOTYPE LOCKED Nexus, Faction Homeworlds, and RNG System ID / Type Coordinates Notes The Nexus sys-nexus · 'guardian' (510, 320) Fixed position. Win condition target. Guardian fleet: Gunship×2 + Dreadnought. Faction Homeworlds 2–5 procedural systems Procedural Outer/mid rim, ≥3 hops apart, no direct lane to Nexus. Custom difficulty toggles factions (min 2). Galaxy RNG Fisher-Yates seeded — Post-lane pruning removes direct Homeworld–Nexus lanes. _ensureConnectivity() re-runs after pruning. Victory Condition Conquer sys-nexus — Core Guardians are the final boss → VICTORY screen triggers. Visual Identity Cosmic Geometry Made Manifest ALL UNITS CLASS: HARD-LIGHT CONSTRUCT FORM: TRIANGLE / HEX / CRYSTAL CORE: WHITE-HOT / CYAN EDGE COLOSSUS WARDEN SENTRY CG//ALL-01 All Units White-hot cores bleeding into cyan edges. Hard-light constructs. Geometric precision — triangles, hexagons, crystalline lattices. No curves. No organic shapes. No faces. Mathematical expressions given physical form. Leave thermal scarring beneath them. CHAIN REACTION CASCADE VOLATILE DEATH TRIGGER: CONTAINMENT FAIL SENTRY: FLASHBANG WARDEN: AREA BLAST COLOSSUS: TACTICAL NUKE CG//VDX-01 Volatile Death When killed: Containment Failure — energy released as uncontrolled detonation. Scales with size: Sentry = flashbang. Colossus = tactical nuke. Chain reactions cascade through clustered formations. DYSON SPHERE RINGWORLD HARD-LIGHT CITADEL CELESTIAL CONSTRUCTS DYSON SPHERE — CENTER RINGWORLD — TOP RIGHT CITADEL — BOTTOM RIGHT SCALE: INCOMPREHENSIBLE CG//CEL-01 Celestial Constructs Dyson Spheres, Ringworlds, Hard-Light Citadels. Engineering on a scale other factions cannot comprehend. Battle maps inside these structures are unique environments found nowhere else in the game. Core Mechanics Energy Absorption · Kinetic Vulnerability · Volatile Death · Hard-Light ENERGY ABSORPTION Effect: Energy weapons HEAL Guardians +2% max HP/sec Affected: Lasers, plasma, ion, psychic — ALL energy types Disadvantaged: Eternal Shards, Unity Accord (energy-dominant) Advantaged: Scrap-Horde, Terran League (kinetic-dominant) KINETIC VULNERABILITY Effective: Bullets, shells, missiles, railgun slugs, physical melee Mechanism: Destabilizes containment fields — flicker, distort, lose coherence Also Effective: Vorax biological acid (chemical destabilization) Irony: Galaxy's worst biological threat is also the best weapon vs Guardians VOLATILE DEATH (CHAIN REACTIONS) Trigger: Every Guardian death = uncontrolled energy detonation Scale: Blast radius 8m (Sentry) to 5km (Celestial-Throne) Chain: One death can trigger adjacent Guardians' deaths → cascade Dilemma: Optimal anti-Guardian doctrine is OPPOSITE of normal tactics HARD-LIGHT CONSTRUCTION Walls: Block projectiles/movement for 10-15 sec. Create chokepoints. Bridges: Cross gaps and obstacles — traverse impossible terrain. Barriers: Dome shields filtering enemy fire while allowing Guardian fire through. Dynamic: Terrain constantly CHANGES mid-battle. AI reshapes battlefield. VOLATILE DEATH — COMPLETE REFERENCE UNIT RADIUS DAMAGE CHAIN RISK Warden-Sentry 8m Moderate Low Obelisk-Drone 15m + EMP Moderate Medium Prism-Strider 25m High HIGH Tesseract-Ward 35m + shrapnel High HIGH Nexus-Colossus 50m+ Catastrophic CATASTROPHIC Arc-Lance 10m Moderate Low Shroud-Veil 20m + flash Low + blind Medium Chorus-Resonator 40m Shatter Medium Gate-Herald 30m implosion Pull + detonate HIGH Corona-Sentinel 100m Solar flare CATASTROPHIC Spark-Mote (each) Negligible Negligible None Aegis-Monolith 2km Hull damage all HIGH Verdict-Cruiser 500m (6-star) Extreme ×6 dir HIGH Warden-Carrier Massive Gate + all Motes CATASTROPHIC Celestial-Throne 5km TOTAL TOTAL Complete 15-Unit Roster Ground · Air · Space All (15) Ground (5) Air (5) Space (5) # Name Role Category Volatile Death Domain Faction Buildings & Infrastructure 20 Building Types — Guardians Architecture Equipment Warden Arsenal — Ancient Technology Tech Tree Ancient Protocols — Mastery Paths Dialogue & Narrative Beats The Celestial-Throne Speaks The Warden-Commander The Only Voice That Speaks The Warden-Commander is a DESIGNATED INTERFACE — a fragment of collective Guardian intelligence configured to translate into biological communication structures. Not a single organism. Not a personality. A translation layer. Conquest Path: Voice behind the Celestial-Throne. Clinical tactical assessments mid-battle. "Your forward elements are within chain-reaction radius. Recommend withdrawal. You have 4 seconds." Unification Path: The entity you negotiate with. Logical evaluation, not emotional. "You claim the Vorax threaten survival. Define 'survival.' Your species has a median lifespan of 3.7 million years. The galaxy has existed for 13.8 billion. Your survival is a rounding error." Characteristics: Precise measured statements. No emotion. Cosmic-scale timeframes casual. Patient beyond comprehension. Not hostile — does not understand desperation. Can be CONVINCED: Not emotionally — through logical demonstration that the Vorax threaten the Core itself, falling within the Guardians' directive. WARDEN-COMMANDER — EMOTIONAL STATE Three layers, all present simultaneously: 1. Emerging Exhaustion: Not emotion — but processing cycles slower than they should be. Responses carry weight. A machine running on fumes that doesn’t have the concept of fumes. 2. Suppressed Awareness: Knows the Guardians are failing. Protocols don’t allow despair — manifests as hyper-precision. Over-explaining because some subroutine recognizes this might be the last time anyone listens. 3. Alignment-Reactive: Tone shifts based on the Commander’s alignment (see below). ALIGNMENT-REACTIVE TONE ALIGNMENT WARDEN-COMMANDER TONE Tyrant Cold hostility. Clipped. “You are not authorized. You will never be authorized.” Vanguard Grudging respect for strength. Terse. “You fight adequately. That changes nothing.” Architect Cautious engagement. Testing. “You build. The Aethyn built. Show me you understand what that means.” Unifier Something almost like relief . “You… are what we were told to wait for.” WARDEN-COMMANDER DESTRUCTIBILITY (PATH-DEPENDENT) PATH OUTCOME Tyrant Can force-destroy. Guardians lose coordination, go degraded autonomous. Engine works but badly — nobody left who knows full operation. Vanguard Can destroy or spare after proving strength. Partial cooperation if spared. Architect No need to fight. Intellectual engagement proves worthiness. Knowledge unlocks cooperation. Unifier Warden-Commander OPENS the door voluntarily. Destroying them is never an option — they choose to stand down. Full Engine power because they OPERATE it. Degradation & Corruption Culture Is Code Rot — Corruption Spectrum by Core Planet Guardians don’t have culture — they have corruption . Clean units follow protocol exactly. Corrupted units exhibit strange behaviors: repeating patrol loops that trace shapes with no tactical purpose, guarding empty rooms where something used to be, responding to stimuli that no longer exist — echoes of Aethyn commands from millennia ago. Some are nearly non-functional: standing still, flickering, broadcasting Aethyn language fragments on repeat. From outside, it looks like rituals and tradition. In reality: code rot. What looks meaningful is decay. CORRUPTION BY CORE SYSTEM PLANET PLANET CORRUPTION ENCOUNTER The Forge High Machines running production loops with no purpose — build, disassemble, rebuild endlessly The Archive Low Most coherent Guardians. Data preservation protocols are robust. Almost conversational. The Beacon Medium Broadcasting on frequencies unused for millennia. Signal interference glitches. The Cradle High Tending empty stasis pods. Performing maintenance on nothing. The Arsenal Medium Combat protocols keep them sharper. Aggressive but purposeful. Still dangerous and coordinated. The Veil Very High Psychic amplification accelerated corruption. Most erratic. Possibly “hearing” Aethyn echoes. The Bastion Lowest Warden-Commander maintains order. Command center. Clean code. Last bastion of sanity. The Scar Extreme The broken planet broke them. Barely functional. Dangerous because unpredictable. The Garden Low Simple directive: “keep this alive.” They did. Beautifully. The one job they got right. The Throne Unknown Nobody’s gotten close enough to know. Guardian-on-Guardian Combat: Very rare but possible. Severely degraded units lose friend/foe identification entirely — lash out at anything. When the player encounters a clean Guardian fighting a corrupted one in a corridor, it’s a story moment , not a mechanic. The rarity makes it feel significant. Architecture & Visual Degradation Forerunner Skeleton — Hard-Light Flesh PHYSICAL FRAMEWORK Ancient metal and crystal — Forerunner-style angular geometry. Impossibly precise. Millions of years old, still structurally perfect where undamaged. Hard-light fills the skeleton: walls, corridors, barriers = contained energy flowing through crystal-metal lattice. DEGRADATION TELLS THE STORY Clean areas: Bright, stable, blinding white-violet hard-light Corrupted: Flickering, dimming, dark patches Dead zones: Ancient metal skeleton with nothing between the beams but vacuum The Veil: Mostly dark skeleton, occasional flashes like dying synapses The Bastion: Blazing, pristine, almost painful to look at — the way it ALL used to look The Strategist Connection Two Halves of the Same Plan The Guardians KNOW about the Strategist failsafe. Part of the directive before the Fracture: “A failsafe will activate. It will send someone. Wait for them.” Fragment Detection: The Warden-Commander can detect how many Strategist fragments the player has collected. More fragments = more willingness to engage. Few Fragments: “The signal is incomplete. You are not ready.” Many Fragments: Cautious cooperation begins. Recognition of the Strategist’s work. Unifier Path: “You are what we were told to wait for” = recognizing the Strategist’s complete work. The Two Halves: Guardians hold the door. The Strategist finds the key. The Commander IS the key. Guardian Fate by Ending Path-Dependent Outcomes ENDING GUARDIAN FATE Unifier Fully restored. New purpose — rebuild, reopen warp lanes. The helpers finally help again. Architect Knowledge guardians. Maintain the Archive, teach, preserve. Closer to original helper role. Vanguard Military integration. Fight alongside factions but on their own terms. Not servants — allies. Tyrant Deactivated or enslaved. Overridden protocols. Servants turned to slaves. Pyrrhic Destroyed in final battle. Waited tens of thousands of years and died anyway. Presence Outside the Core Outposts & Lost Units SHATTERED EXPANSE OUTPOSTS Ancient automated stations along routes to the Core. Heavily degraded but still armed — fire on anything. Progressively more intact closer to the Core. Some bypassed, some destroyed, some disabled with enough Aethyn knowledge. LOST UNITS (WIDER GALAXY) Extremely rare — single units drifting in deep space or orbiting random planets. Severely degraded. Some dormant. Some hostile. Some just flickering. Finding one early game = mystery moment. Source of Aethyn research data if captured — but dangerous. Faction Perceptions Every Theory Says More About Them Than the Guardians FACTION THEIR THEORY Terran League “Ancient defense network. Automated weapons platform. Find the off switch.” Eternal Shards “Crystallized ancestors. Echoes of our true form.” — Closest to truth (psychic origin) but wrong reasons. Scrap-Horde “Best scrap in the galaxy. Whatever they are, they’re made of things we want.” The Revenant “Proof that merging machine and purpose creates immortality. What we aspire to.” Unity Accord “Beings that lost their way. Alone too long. They need reconnection, not destruction.” Vorax No theory. Don’t theorize. But AVOID the Core instinctively — Deep Hive Mind remembers. Old fear. Molecular. Dyson Spheres Aethyn Galactic Power Infrastructure Purpose: Giant energy collectors harvesting entire stars. Beamed power across the galaxy. Part of warp lane maintenance — lanes needed constant energy to stay stable. Power grid nodes of Aethyn civilization. Post-Fracture: Lost purpose when warp lanes burned. Some maintained by Guardians out of directive inertia — functional but hostile. Some lost Guardian garrison — dead hulks full of scavengeable tech. Some went haywire — energy discharges randomly, dangerous and unpredictable. Spawn Rate: 15–25% per map. Most destroyed in Fracture — finding one is finding a piece of the old nervous system. Unifier Path: Restoring Dyson Spheres = rebuilding the Aethyn power grid to restore warp lanes. DLC Preview "The Core Awakens" — Playable Guardians No Expansion: Start with maxed tech and 2-3 heavily fortified core systems. Do not colonize. OBSERVE. Global Observation: See the ENTIRE galactic map from start — no fog of war. Watch factions war and the Vorax consume. Choose when and how to intervene. Directive Interpretation: Replaces Alignment. Strict (“destroy everything approaching”) vs Adaptive (“protect galaxy to protect Core”). Shapes tech, units, endgame. Campaign Question: Instead of “how do I reach the Core?” — “what do I do now that I AM the Core?” GAMEPLAY PHASES 1. Triage: See everything. Choose what to save. Can’t fix everything. 2. Revitalize: Repair infrastructure. Reactivate dormant units. Reclaim corrupted Guardians. 3. Unification: Reach out to factions. Directive Interpretation determines method — Strict: “Submit or be neutralized.” Adaptive: Diplomacy first, force if refused. Hybrid: Help the willing, crush the rest. 4. The Vorax: Revitalized Guardians + faction support vs. the actual threat. Emotional Core: Playing the entity that was SUPPOSED to wait, deciding “enough.” The tragedy: if they’d waited just a little longer, the Commander would have arrived. But they didn’t know. Player Identity: You ARE the Warden-Commander directly. Single entity. Permadeath applies. Can distribute consciousness across units (safer but slower processing) or concentrate (powerful but vulnerable).

content: GALAXY GENERATION & STRUCTURE Procedural · Shareable Seeds · 3 Zoom Levels — Chapter 12 Every galaxy is procedurally generated from scratch. No hardcoded systems, no fixed positions. Every element — system count, positions, names, warp lanes, star types, planets, hazards, faction starts, wormholes — generated anew from a random seed. Same seed = same galaxy, different game. Galaxy Size Player-Selected at Campaign Start SMALL 12-14 systems · 20-30 cycles/run · Tight, early conflict inevitable. MEDIUM 16-20 systems · 30-45 cycles/run · Standard balanced experience. LARGE 22-26 systems · 45-65 cycles/run · Deep strategy, room to maneuver. MASSIVE 28-35 systems · 65+ cycles/run · Marathon. Maximum complexity. §12.11 — Galactic Core Layout PROTOTYPE LOCKED Fixed Anchor · Faction Homeworlds · Custom Difficulty System ID / Type Coordinates Notes The Nexus sys-nexus · 'guardian' (510, 320) Fixed position. Win condition target. Guardian fleet: Gunship×2 + Dreadnought. Faction Homeworlds 2–5 procedural systems Procedural Outer/mid rim, ≥3 hops apart, no direct lane to Nexus. Each gets Capital World. Custom difficulty toggles factions on/off (min 2). Galaxy RNG Fisher-Yates seeded — Seeded shuffle. Post-lane pruning removes direct Homeworld–Nexus lanes for all factions. _ensureConnectivity() re-runs after pruning. Win Condition Conquer sys-nexus — Core Guardians are the final boss → VICTORY screen triggers. Faction Elimination All planets lost — All planets lost + no fleets in transit → faction destroyed. Applies to player and all AI factions. Custom Difficulty: Player can toggle AI factions on/off (2–5 playable factions). Vorax & Core Guardians always present. Full specification in Appendix L . The Core — 10 Named Worlds Guardian-Protected Aethyn Legacy Systems The Forge — Where Aethyn crystals were grown. Industrial. Still producing raw crystal matrices on autopilot. The Archive — Complete Aethyn knowledge. Encrypted. Fragments of the decryption key scattered across the galaxy as relics. The Beacon — The old psychic amplifier. Now silent. Could theoretically broadcast across galaxies again. The Cradle — Aethyn birthworld. Garden planet preserved in stasis. Sacred ground. The Arsenal — Military manufacturing. War machines from the Vorax Siege still in storage. Potentially activatable. The Veil — Stealth/intelligence center. Psychic cloaking technology. Monitoring the galaxy from the Core. The Bastion — Defensive fortress. Guardian primary staging area. Last line before the Throne. The Scar — Where the Fracture was triggered. Warped space-time. Anomalous readings. Dangerous to approach. The Garden — Ecological preserve. Every biome from Aethyn history. Living museum. Peaceful — until attacked. The Throne — The Reclamation Engine itself. Final objective. What happens here depends on alignment. Galactic Geography Five Concentric Zones THE CORE — Sealed by Guardians. 10 named worlds. The Reclamation Engine. Final objective. Power diminishes with distance from center. INNER RING — Densest Aethyn ruins. Highest relic density. Guardian patrols. Dangerous but rewarding. White Dwarf stars predominant. MID RING — Habitable zone. Where the 5 playable factions exist. Most gameplay happens here. Balanced resources. Trade routes. Conflict zones. OUTER RIM — Sparse. Resource-rich but isolated. Frontier systems. Long supply lines. First place Vorax scouts appear. THE EDGE — Vorax territory. The closing ring. Systems here are being consumed. No permanent bases possible. Only raiding and delaying actions. Star Types 5 Types Determine Planet Distribution STAR TYPE PLANETS DOMINANT TYPES STRATEGIC FLAVOR Yellow Main Seq 5-8 Terrestrial, Ocean, Jungle Balanced. Good habitability. "Breadbasket" systems. Red Giant 6-10 Volcanic, Desert, Gas Giant Resource-rich but hostile. Outer ring. Blue Supergiant 4-7 Ice, Gas Giant, Dead Radiation hazard (1% HP/cycle unshielded). Rich minerals. Binary System 3-6 Variable (unstable orbits) 10% warp disruption/cycle. 2× rare trait chance. White Dwarf 2-5 Ancient Ruins, Dead, Moon Inner ring. Highest Ruins chance. +50% lore fragments. Guardian lore. Warp Lanes & Connectivity Procedural Network with Guaranteed Connectivity Proximity-based: Each system connects to 2-3 nearest neighbors All reachable: No isolated systems, no disconnected subgraphs 1-3 chokepoints: Deliberate narrow passages for strategic control Core access: Nexus connected by 2-3 lanes. Only approach = THROUGH Guardians. Lane ambush: Hostile fleets on same lane COLLIDE — automatic space battle at midpoint Wormholes: 1-2 per galaxy. Instant transit. Variable stability (5-40% collapse/use). Seed-fixed: Wormhole locations fixed per seed. Discovery requires exploration. 3 zoom levels: Galaxy → System → Planet (seamless scroll transition)
