content: GALACTIC PHENOMENA & EVENTS 5 Static Hazards · 5 Dynamic Events · Ion Storms — Chapter 14 Static Hazard Zones (3-6 per galaxy, permanent) Permanent Features That Shape Strategy Nebula 2-3 system span Fleet stealth inside (invisible to outside sensors). Shields disabled for all. Sensors -50%. Ambush zone — kinetic fleets hiding inside dominate energy-shielded fleets. Black Hole 1-system radius No retreat within 1 hop (fight to destruction). No salvage (wreckage pulled in). Time dilation: +1 cycle research time. Commitment zone — you either win or die. Asteroid Field Band/cluster +1 cycle movement through field. Fighters +30% evasion (dodge between rocks). Capitals 2% HP/cycle collision. +25% Scrap production. Light fleet defense zone. Pulsar Rotating beams EMP every 3 cycles — disables tech modules 1 cycle in adjacent systems. Comms +1 cycle delay. 1% HP radiation. Horde least affected (crude tech). Accord most affected (electronic targeting). Wormhole Zone Spatial distortion Contains wormhole endpoints (instant transit shortcuts). 5%/cycle fleet displacement (wrong destination). 20% false sensor contacts (ghost fleets). High-risk, high-reward navigation. Dynamic Events (temporary, 1 cycle warning) The Galaxy's Weather — Unpredictable, Impactful Nebula Surge (every 8-12 cy, 3 cy duration) Nebula EXPANDS +1 adjacent system. Shields stripped. Fleets gain stealth. Exploit: position fleet in predicted path for sudden ambush capability. Gravitational Pulse (every 10-15 cy, instant) Black Hole PULLS all fleets within 2 hops 1 hop closer. Involuntary. Can drag fleets into no-retreat zone. Keep 3+ hops away or accept the risk. Meteor Shower (every 6-10 cy, permanent deposit) 6-12⚙ Scrap deposited on random territory near Asteroid Field. Land-grab race between factions. "Are we really fighting over 10 Scrap?" Sometimes yes. Pulsar Flare (every 12-18 cy, 1 cy duration) ALL electronics disabled in adjacent system for 1 full cycle. No combat, no movement, no comms. Fleets are sitting ducks. Time assaults to coincide. Wormhole Resonance (every 15-20 cy, 3 cy duration) SECOND temporary wormhole opens connecting to random distant system. Instant transit but 40% collapse chance per use (vs 5% standard). 3 cycles then closes. An irresistible gamble for emergency reinforcement or surprise attack. ION STORMS — MOBILE HAZARDS Spawn every 8-12 cycles at galaxy edge. Travel inward along warp lanes at 1 hop/2 cycles. Duration per system: 2-3 cycles. Effects: ALL shields disabled, ALL tech modules offline, ALL electronic equipment non-functional, random 1-3% HP ion lightning. Terran: Lose targeting but kinetics still fire. Field Repair drones go OFFLINE. Shards: Phase Blink unaffected (not electronic). Horde: LEAST affected (crude non-electronic tech). Revenant: Gauss weapons require power — reduced to basic mode. Accord: Tactical Link OFFLINE — devastating.

content: PLANETARY INFRASTRUCTURE 15 Building Types · Multiple Per Territory — Chapter 16 Each territory supports multiple buildings simultaneously. Buildings have a one-time construction cost only — no ongoing upkeep. Practical limits: Scrap cost, build time, and some buildings conflict (War Factory vs Research Lab compete for power). Balance concentration (efficiency) vs distribution (resilience). §16.2 — MVP Building Catalog PROTOTYPE LOCKED Three Confirmed Buildings for Prototype Slice Building Cost Time Effect Supply Depot 10⚙ 2 cycles Resets supply distance from this territory. Holds 20⚙ emergency reserves. Relay Station 8⚙ 1 cycle +25% supply speed on this lane. Stackable — up to 3 stations = +75% max. Does not reset distance. Logistics Hub +15⚙ upgrade 3 cycles Upgrades Supply Depot. Full speed supply to 4 hops. Unlocks Armada Phase access from this territory. Holds 50⚙ emergency reserves. Full Building Catalog Economic · Military · Logistics · Research · Special VORAX INFESTATION VS BUILDINGS All buildings destroyed on consumption. Creep replaces them. Fortifications → infested cover (benefits Vorax only). Shield Generators → energy absorbed by Hive Node. Reconquering + Creep fade (3-5 cycles) → rebuild from scratch. Total cost of losing a developed territory can exceed 50⚙ + 10 cycles. BUILDING DESTRUCTION Ground combat: 15% chance per building (5% if player wins). Orbital bombardment: ALL buildings destroyed. Sabotage: targeted single building. Destroyed buildings leave rubble (light cover, no function). §16.3 — Tier-Locked Recruitment Unit Tier Determines Local Recruitment Requirements UNIT TIER REQUIREMENT NOTES Light Infantry Supply connection only Recruitable at any owned territory with supply. Vehicles / Medium War Factory required Territory must have a War Factory for local recruitment. Super-Heavy / Elite Adv. War Factory required Territory must have an Advanced War Factory for local recruitment. ARMADA TRANSPORT EXCEPTION Units transported via Armada bypass tier-lock restrictions . The restriction applies to local recruitment only . If you invaded with Titans in your fleet, your Titans fight — you just can't build new Titans at that territory without an Adv. War Factory. §16.4 — War Factory Functions Auto-Production · Recruitment Unlock · Repair AUTO-PRODUCTION War Factory auto-produces 1 free unit every 2 turns . Unit type based on territory type + other buildings present. Adv. War Factory counts as 2× (produces faster or better units). RECRUITMENT UNLOCK Enables paid recruitment of heavy/vehicle units via RecruitPanel. Adv. War Factory additionally unlocks super-heavy/elite recruitment at the territory. REPAIR / HEAL Units at territories with War Factory heal between battles (restore HP). Damaged units automatically recover without spending additional resources. Recruitment speed: No distance or factory-based speed modifier. A unit takes its build_time_cycles from the database. Period. No scrap cost scaling by distance. No build time scaling by distance. Supply cut: Does NOT freeze recruitment. Supply cut only affects yield income (via supply chain hop modifier). Recruitment continues as long as territory is owned. Damaged factory: No impairment from attacks. If you successfully defended the territory, the War Factory is fine. §16.5 — Rally Point System Per-System · Auto-Produced Units Gather Here RALLY POINTS Player designates a rally territory per system . All auto-produced units from War Factories in that system automatically gather at the rally point. This prevents units from scattering across 15+ territories in a system and lets the player concentrate forces at strategic locations. BUILDING COST MODEL — ONE-TIME ONLY All buildings have a one-time construction cost only. There is no ongoing upkeep. Once built, a building operates indefinitely until destroyed. This simplifies the economic model and makes territory development feel permanent and rewarding rather than a drain.

content: THE AUTO-BATTLE ENGINE Core Engine · AI Roles · Space Combat · Ground Cover — Chapters 17-19 All combat runs on a single auto-battle engine. The player does NOT directly control units — they fight autonomously according to AI behavior scaling with veterancy rank. The player's role: pre-battle preparation (80% of tactical skill), real-time CP ability intervention, and strategic observation for future decisions. The Auto-Battle Principle Preparation → Observation → Intervention PRE-BATTLE (80%) Equipment loadouts. Army composition. Deployment positioning (ground) or formation selection (space). A well-prepared army wins before the first shot. REAL-TIME INTERVENTION CP abilities: orbital strikes, shield projections, reinforcement drops, retreat orders. Pauses battle when activated. The commander's surgical tools. STRATEGIC OBSERVATION Which units underperform. Which compositions are problematic. Which terrain is underutilized. Feeds into replay coaching for General-led battles. ENGINE ARCHITECTURE — SIMULTANEOUS SYSTEMS Pathfinding: Navigate terrain, avoid obstacles, seek optimal positions based on AI behavior. Targeting: Priority rules — nearest, weakest, highest threat, commander designation. Combat Resolution: Fire rate → accuracy roll (range, cover, Marks, Phase Blink) → damage (weapon type, armor, resistances). Morale: Bio units track morale. Low morale = accuracy penalties, slower movement, routing. Machines/energy immune. Special Mechanics: Field Repairs, Phase Blink, Wreckage Salvage, Reanimation, Marked Targets, Energy Absorption, Volatile Death. Faction Overlays: Each mechanic runs as an overlay on core engine — consistent base, unique per faction. §17.5 — AI Role System PROTOTYPE LOCKED Three Roles · Spawn Index · Reactive Repositioning Role Spawn Index Movement Reposition Priority Target Aggressor 0, 3 Straight advance toward enemy line 200 ms Nearest enemy unit Flanker 1, 4 Angled wide curve toward enemy flank 350 ms Lowest-HP enemy within 400 px Brawler 2, 5 Slower advance, holds short-range fire position 500 ms Nearest enemy unit Reactive repositioning is staggered to avoid simultaneous movement spikes: Aggressor fires at 200 ms, Flanker at 350 ms, Brawler at 500 ms after a reposition trigger. Default 6 units per side — spawn indices 0–5 map to Role assignments above. Sustained fire removes it. ELEVATION +1 Tier: +10% acc · +5% range · +5% dmg +2 Tier: +15% acc · +10% range · +10% dmg +3 Tier: +20% acc · +15% range · +15% dmg Deployment: High ground = most impactful pre-battle choice. GARRISON -40% damage + suppression immune Fire from windows with normal accuracy Cannot move until voluntary exit Building destroyed: 80% HP damage to all occupants. title: Ground Combat Vision
subtitle: Dark Crusade + StarCraft + Mechabellum — The Blend
sections: How Each Contributes to Ground Combat
content: GROUND COMBAT VISION Dark Crusade + StarCraft + Mechabellum — The Blend §19b.1 — Design Inspirations Three Games · One System DAWN OF WAR: DARK CRUSADE Territory conquest map. Garrison defense. Attacker deploys from map edge. Buildings give defensive bonuses. Every territory is a place with meaning, not just a hex. The weight and permanence of conquest. STARCRAFT Unit composition and counter-play. Rock-paper-scissors dynamics. Positioning matters. The satisfaction of building the right army for the right fight. Visual readability at a glance. MECHABELLUM Auto-battler deployment. Formation strategy. Reserve waves. Player sets up, then watches with surgical intervention. The commander fantasy — preparation IS the skill expression. §19b.2 — The Blend How Each Contributes to Ground Combat Pre-Battle: Deploy units in formation (Mechabellum) at a territory (Dark Crusade) During Battle: Units auto-fight (Mechabellum) with counter-play mattering (StarCraft) Terrain: Defender has building bonuses from territory (Dark Crusade) Decisions: Player picks composition + formation + reserves, then watches. CP abilities for surgical intervention. NON-NEGOTIABLE: AUTO-BATTLER STAYS The auto-battler core is the foundation of the entire combat experience. Players do NOT directly control units. The skill expression is in preparation (80%) and intervention (20%). This is what makes Ashes of Command a strategy game, not a tactics game. §19b.3 — Missing Pieces to Build Systems Not Yet Implemented 1. Terrain & Cover Units behind obstacles take less damage. Cover positions matter for deployment. 2. Defender Garrison Bonus Buildings provide stat bonuses to defending units. Makes territory development directly impact combat. 3. Strategic Capture Points Key points to capture during battle for tactical advantage. Encourages dynamic movement. 4. Flanking Bonus Side/rear attacks deal more damage. Rewards positioning and formation depth.

content: THE EQUIPMENT SYSTEM 6 Slot Categories · 208 Items · 4 Rarity Tiers — Chapters 20-21 Every unit is a complete entity with its own identity. Customization comes from equipping weapons, armor, gear, ammunition, and modules. Equipment is acquired through research, battle loot, salvage, trade, and discovery. Higher-rank units unlock more slots. The Six Slot Categories Primary Weapon · Armor · Gear · Ammo · System · Special [W] Primary Weapon Main damage source. Found on all units. [A] Armor Plating HP bonus, damage resistance, special protection. All units. [G] Gear Tactical utility — grenades, scanners, repair kits. Infantry, Mechs. Locked at Rookie. Unlocks at Veteran. [AM] Ammo Type Modifies primary weapon damage type/behavior. Ranged, Vehicles, Ships. [S] Synergy Module Synergy equipment — targeting, sensors, ECM, engines. Vehicles, Air, Ships. [SP] Special Faction-unique or unit-unique signature equipment. Elite units only. §20.11 — MVP Equipment Catalog PROTOTYPE LOCKED 8 Items · Prototype Slice · All Confirmed Values Item Slot Rarity Key Stats M7 Assault Rifle [W] Common Baseline weapon. Equipped on 2 starting Shock-Marines. Breacher Shotgun [W] Uncommon +15% dmg, −20% range. Short-range devastation. 1 loose in starting inventory. Plasma Pistol [W] Uncommon +10% dmg, +5% atk spd. Energy sidearm — effective vs Guardians? Only with kinetic primary. Standard Composite [A] Common Baseline armor. Equipped on 2 starting Shock-Marines. Reactive Plating [A] Uncommon +20% HP. Solid mid-tier survivability boost. 1 loose in starting inventory. Fortress Plate [A] Rare +40% HP, −15% speed. The tank trade-off. Research or high-roll loot. Frag Grenade Pack [G] Common aoe:true. Area denial / clustered-enemy punisher. [G] slot locked until Veteran rank. Repair Kit [G] Uncommon +2% HP/sec passive regeneration. Stacks with Field Repair (Terran) for +4% total when HP < 75%. §20.12 — MVP Starting Inventory PROTOTYPE LOCKED What the Player Begins With EQUIPPED (ON UNITS) 2× M7 Assault Rifle [W] — Common 2× Standard Composite [A] — Common LOOSE (IN WAR ROOM) 1× Reactive Plating [A] — Uncommon 1× Breacher Shotgun [W] — Uncommon STARTING RESOURCES ⚙ SCRAP 30 ◈ INTEL 12 ⬡ INFLUENCE 5 §20.13 — Loot Roll Formula PROTOTYPE LOCKED Probability Scales with Campaign Progress lootChance = min(0.85, battlesWon × 0.15) 1 battle 15% 3 battles 45% 6 battles 85% Cap 85% max Cross-faction loot possible. Loot rarity weighted: Common 60% · Uncommon 30% · Rare 10% · Legendary <1% (Discovery only, not loot roll). Slots by Unit Category Larger Units = More Slots CATEGORY EXAMPLES BASE SLOTS Light Infantry Shock-Marine, Zealot, Brute, Legionnaire, Strike-Trooper [W] [A] Heavy Vehicle Siege-Breaker, Grav-Tank, Gore-Roller, Hammerhead [W] [A] [AM] Super-Heavy Leviathan, Avatar, War-God, Monolith, Broadside [W] [A] [AM] [S] Capital Ship Broadsword, Void-Stalker, Ram-Ship, Entropy, Emissary [W] [A] [AM] [S] Flagship Nova-Dreadnought, Infinity, Asteroid, Cairn, Corona All 6 Slots Rank-Based Slot Unlocks Veterancy Grants Equipment Access ROOKIE Base slots only. [G] locked. 0 kills VETERAN +1 slot. [G] unlocks. +5% all stats. 3 kills ELITE +1 slot + Trait. +12% all stats. 8 kills COMMANDER +1 slot + Aura + CP. +20% all. 15 kills GENERAL All slots + Autonomy. TBD%. 25 kills ⚑ ⚑ General kill threshold (25) and stat modifier (%) are proposed values — not yet confirmed. See Appendix L open flags. Rarity Tiers Common · Uncommon · Rare · Legendary COMMON Available from start or early research. Baseline. No special effects. UNCOMMON Mid-game research or first loot/trade. Moderate bonuses. Minor effects. RARE Late-game research, specific encounters. Significant bonuses. Meaningful effects. LEGENDARY One-of-a-kind. Ancient Ruins, Guardian fragments. Cannot mass-produce. Game-changing. Acquisition Channels 5 Ways to Get Equipment Research (Tech Tree): Unlocks blueprints for mass production. Common + Uncommon. Battle Loot (lootChance formula): Dropped by defeated enemies. Cross-faction possible. Uncommon + Rare. Salvage: Post-battle scavenging (Horde is fastest). Common, occasionally Uncommon. Trade (Black Market): Purchase with Scrap. Cross-faction items. Refreshes every 5 cycles. Uncommon + Rare. Discovery: Ancient Ruins, Fallen Empires, Derelict Fleets. Rare + Legendary. The Keystone (+50% vs Guardians) = campaign-defining. One per campaign. Tech Tree & Research (Ch28) 4–5 Tiers × 3–4 Branches Per Faction · 124 Nodes Total SHARED STRUCTURE Branch 1: Primary weapon mastery → Ultimate weapon tech Branch 2: Defense & sustainability → Ultimate survivability Branch 3: Logistics & command → Ultimate strategic tech Branch 4: Special projects → Super-heavy, flagship, hybrid ammo TIER PROGRESSION T1: 2 cycles. Common unlocks. Available immediately. T2 (a/b choice): 4 cycles. Uncommon. Requires T1. T3: 6 cycles. Rare. Requires T2. T4 (Culmination): 8 cycles. Game-changing. Requires T3. RESEARCH SPEED MODIFIERS Research Lab: Enables research (1 researcher) Advanced Lab: +50% speed (2 researchers) Ancient Ruins trait: -25% time in that system Architect secondary: +10% speed CROSS-FACTION TECH ADOPTION Defeat enemies → 15% loot drop → Use but not produce → Build Xenotech Analysis Bay (20⚙, 4 cycles) → Reverse-engineer (3-5 cycles) → Blueprint permanently available. Late-game luxury, not standard strategy.

Scan Pulse 1 CP Reveal all hidden/burrowed enemies for 10 seconds. Core faction identity ability. TECH T2 — Unlocked by researching a Tier 2 tech node. TECH T3/T4 — Unlocked by researching a Tier 3 or 4 tech node. Powerful. BUILDING — Requires specific building in system. Lost if building destroyed (unless duplicate exists in system). GENERAL RANK 3+ — Unlocked when assigned General reaches Rank 3. TERRITORY 8+ — Unlocked when faction controls 8+ territories. Emperor-scale power. TERRAN LEAGUE — Military precision, orbital superiority # ABILITY CP EFFECT UNLOCK 1 Orbital Strike 3 75px radius, 172 kinetic damage Starter 2 Suppressive Fire 2 Target area: enemies -40% move speed, -20% accuracy 8s Tech T2 3 Depleted Uranium Barrage 4 100px radius, 220 damage, irradiates zone 10s (DoT) Tech T4 (Ballistics) 4 Precision Airstrike 3 Single target, 300 damage, ignores armor Building: Orbital Defense 5 Field Command Post 2 All units in 200px +15% accuracy, +10% damage 12s General Rank 3+ 6 Full Mobilization 4 All Terran units +25% fire rate 15s Territory 8+ ETERNAL SHARDS — Psionic, crystalline, mind warfare # ABILITY CP EFFECT UNLOCK 1 Psionic Storm 3 80px radius, 140 psionic damage, ignores shields Starter 2 Crystal Barrier 2 Creates impassable crystal wall 150px wide, 8s duration Tech T2 3 Phase Shift 3 Target squad invulnerable + untargetable 4s, can't attack Tech T4 4 Resonance Cascade 4 All Shards heal 20% HP, enemies in 200px take 80 damage Building: Resonance Amplifier 5 Mind Shatter 2 Single enemy unit: stunned 4s, -30% damage after General Rank 3+ 6 Shard Convergence 5 Summon crystal golem (powerful temp unit, 20s duration) Territory 8+ SCRAP-HORDE — Overwhelming numbers, improvised chaos # ABILITY CP EFFECT UNLOCK 1 Scrap Rush 2 All Horde units +50% move speed, +20% melee damage 8s Starter 2 Booby Trap 1 Place invisible mine at location, 120 damage on trigger Tech T2 3 Scrap Bomb 3 Improvised explosive: 90px radius, 160 damage, debris slows area Tech T3 4 Mob Conscription 2 Instant 3 militia units spawn at target point (weak but free) Building: Mob Rally Point 5 Frenzy 3 Single squad: +60% damage, +40% speed, takes +30% damage, 10s General Rank 3+ 6 Endless Horde 5 5 free Brute squads spawn from all map edges over 15s Territory 8+ THE REVENANT — Death, corruption, attrition warfare # ABILITY CP EFFECT UNLOCK 1 Reanimate 3 Revive up to 3 dead units at 30% HP near corpse locations Starter 2 Soul Siphon 2 Target enemy: drain 15% HP over 6s, heal nearest Revenant unit Tech T2 3 Blight Cloud 3 100px radius poison: 20 dmg/sec 10s, -25% healing received Tech T3 4 Corpse Explosion 2 Detonate all corpses in 120px radius, 80 damage each Building: Blight Beacon 5 Death March 3 Target squad: cannot die for 6s (HP stays at 1), +30% damage General Rank 3+ 6 Mass Resurrection 5 Revive ALL dead units at 50% HP across entire battlefield Territory 8+ UNITY ACCORD — Coordination, defense, multi-species synergy # ABILITY CP EFFECT UNLOCK 1 Diplomatic Shield 2 100px dome: all allies inside -40% damage taken, 8s Starter 2 Coordinated Strike 2 Mark target: all Accord units +25% damage vs marked 10s Tech T2 3 Orbital Relay 3 Place beacon: +20% accuracy, +15% damage, permanent until destroyed Tech T4 4 Peacekeeping Force 3 Drop elite multi-species squad (3 strong units, 25s duration) Building: Diplomatic Summit 5 Inspire Alliance 2 All Accord units: cleanse debuffs, +15% all stats 10s General Rank 3+ 6 United Front 4 All Accord units share damage equally 12s (no unit dies while others live) Territory 8+ VORAX COLLECTIVE — Consume, evolve, overwhelm biologically # ABILITY CP EFFECT UNLOCK 1 Consume 2 Target corpse area: Vorax eat corpses, heal 40% HP each Starter 2 Spore Cloud 2 80px radius: -50% accuracy, -30% speed 8s Tech T2 3 Acid Rain 4 120px radius: 30 dmg/sec 8s, dissolves armor (-20% resistance) Tech T3 4 Bio-Bomb 3 Living explosive: 100px, 180 damage, spawns 2 parasites Building: Evolution Chamber 5 Hive Surge 3 Spawn 4 swarmlings from nearest Vorax unit General Rank 3+ 6 Assimilation Wave 5 Convert 1 enemy unit to Vorax permanently (strongest available) Territory 8+ CORE GUARDIANS — Ancient technology, temporal manipulation # ABILITY CP EFFECT UNLOCK 1 Temporal Slow 2 100px radius: enemies -50% speed, -30% fire rate 8s Starter 2 Data Override 2 Hack single enemy unit: fights for you 6s, stunned 2s after Tech T2 3 Energy Overload 4 Single target: 400 damage, chains to 2 nearby at 50% Tech T3 4 Sentinel Construct 3 Summon turret (200 HP, high damage, 20s duration) Building: Energy Conduit 5 Ancient Protocol 3 All Guardian units: +30% shields, energy weapons +20% damage 12s General Rank 3+ 6 Chrono Reversal 5 Rewind target area 5s: undo all damage/deaths in 150px Territory 8+ Space CP Abilities (7) All 7 · Costs · Full Effects ABILITY COST EFFECT Missile Barrage 3 CP Guided volley at 200m radius. Point defense intercepts 20-40%. Shield Overcharge 2 CP Single ship invulnerable 6 sec. Cannot fire during overcharge. Ramming Speed 2 CP Physical collision. 200% remaining HP as damage to BOTH ships. Fighter Scramble 2 CP Emergency interceptor wave from carrier. 300m defense radius, 15 sec. EMP Pulse 3 CP 300m radius ALL systems disabled 5 sec. Affects FRIENDLY ships too — use with care. Emergency Retreat 1 CP Single ship disengages at max speed. Survives but cannot fire during retreat. Boarding Action 4 CP Board adjacent enemy ship within 500m. Success = CAPTURE mid-battle. Horde +25% success chance. §21.3 — Commander Abilities REDESIGNED Now Faction-Specific — General Rank 3+ Unlock COMMANDER ABILITY SYSTEM — REDESIGNED The old trait-based commander abilities (War Cry, Fortify, Rally, etc.) have been replaced by the faction-specific General Rank 3+ unlock system above. Each faction now has a unique commander-tier ability that unlocks when the assigned General reaches Rank 3, replacing the generic trait abilities. See §21.1c faction tables — ability #5 in each faction is the General Rank 3+ unlock. BUILDING-LOSS RULE If a building that grants a CP ability is destroyed , the ability becomes LOCKED — unless another building of the same type exists elsewhere in the same system. This creates meaningful defensive decisions around protecting key infrastructure. Example: Terran Orbital Defense destroyed → Precision Airstrike locked. But if a second Orbital Defense exists in another territory in the same system, the ability stays available. FACTION CP MODIFIERS — RETIRED The old shared faction modifier system (Terran +15% Orbital Strike, Shards +3s Energy Shield, etc.) has been retired. Faction-specific differences are now baked directly into each faction's unique ability set . Each faction has 6 completely distinct abilities with their own costs, effects, and unlock paths.

content: CHAIN OF COMMAND Rank System · General AI Ego · Personality Traits · Standing Orders — Chapters 22-23 Units grow from anonymous Rookies to autonomous Generals through a kill-count promotion chain. At Elite rank they develop personality traits. At Commander they gain auras and CP abilities. At General they can command entire sectors and fleets independently — becoming characters in their own right, with goals, opinions, and defection risk. §22.1 — The Promotion Chain (Option C) PROTOTYPE LOCKED Kill-Count Thresholds · Confirmed Stat Modifiers Rank Kill Threshold Stat Mod Unlocks Rookie 0 +0% Anonymous. Base slots [W][A]. No personality. Veteran 3 +5% Gains a NAME. [G] slot unlocked. Visible in HUD. Elite 8 +12% PERSONALITY TRAIT develops. +1 equipment slot. Trait affects AI behavior and dialogue. Commander 15 (proposed) +20% PASSIVE AURA + ACTIVE CP ability (2 CP). War Council member. Commands sector independently. General 25 ⚑ proposed TBD ⚑ Full autonomous command. Entire sectors + fleets. All equipment slots. MISSION system. Ego risk. ⚑ OPEN FLAG: General kill threshold (25) and stat modifier (%) are proposed values only — not yet confirmed in prototype. Commander threshold (15) is also proposed. Do not implement General AI autonomy mechanics until these are locked. See Appendix L for full open flags register. The The Revenant page (Ch 8) also carries this table as canonical anchor. The Six Personality Traits All Strengths — No Trait Is a Weakness TRAIT COMBAT BONUS STRATEGIC BONUS DEFECTION OUTCOME Aggressive +15% damage output Best DPS General Joins faction you're at war with Cautious -20% casualties taken Most efficient — preserves veterans Surrenders peacefully to nearest faction Loyal +10% to all nearby allied Generals Force multiplier — makes everyone better Vanishes heartbroken (very rare) Ambitious +25% XP gain, faster promotions Best for building elite armies fast Joins strongest enemy faction Reckless +30% dmg on first engagement/cycle Devastating blitz specialist Charges Vorax/Guardians alone (dies heroically) Cunning +20% espionage success in sector Best Intel General Goes independent — Pirate Warlord Trait → Commander CP Ability Mapping Each Trait Determines the Active CP Ability at Commander Rank AGGRESSIVE War Cry (2 CP) +20% dmg to all nearby allies for 8 sec CAUTIOUS Fortify (2 CP) -25% dmg taken by all nearby allies 8 sec LOYAL Rally (2 CP) Heal 10% HP + cleanse morale debuffs in 200 px AMBITIOUS Focus Fire (2 CP) Target unit +15% acc for all allies for 10 sec RECKLESS Overdrive (2 CP) +50% dmg / +30% spd, takes +25% dmg — 8 sec CUNNING Misdirect (2 CP) Holographic decoy draws fire 8 sec Standing Orders 9 Orders · Personality Modifies Interpretation EXPAND — Conquer neutral systems aggressively DEFEND — Hold territory, engage only if attacked FORTIFY — Build infrastructure, improve defenses HARASS — Raid enemy supply, avoid big fights PATROL — Move between 2-3 systems, engage hostiles HUNT — Seek and destroy enemy fleets ESCORT — Follow specific convoy as bodyguard RAID — Hit-and-run enemy supply lines BLOCKADE — Park at system, prevent fleet movement General AI Ego System Loyalty · Friction · Defection Arc LOYALTY SCORE (0–100) Gains: Victories, alignment with trait, respected orders, gifts of equipment, acknowledgment in Council. Loses: Orders ignored, orders that contradict trait, excessive casualties under their command, rivals promoted over them. <20 Loyalty: Friction events trigger. <5: Defection risk active. FRICTION EVENTS Disagreement: General pushes back in War Council. Requires player acknowledgment. Insubordination: Modifies standing order 10-20% toward their trait preference. Challenge: Demands resource, territory, or recognition — binary choice with consequences. DEFECTION OUTCOMES BY TRAIT Aggressive → Joins the faction you're currently at war with. Turns their tactical knowledge against you. · Cautious → Surrenders peacefully to nearest neutral faction. Takes their equipment but no violence. · Loyal → Vanishes heartbroken. Very rare. Returns as event during Final War. · Ambitious → Joins the strongest enemy faction. Accelerates their veteran production. · Reckless → Charges Vorax tendril or Guardian fleet alone. Dies heroically on their own terms. · Cunning → Goes fully independent. Becomes Pirate Warlord — minor faction on the galaxy map.

content: THE WAR COUNCIL & ADVISORS 15 Unique Characters (5 Factions × 3) · Doctrine Recommendations · Hidden Depths — Chapter 23 Convenes automatically: every 5 cycles, before Approach Phase, after major events (Vorax arrival, faction elimination, Guardian contact), and on loyalty warnings. Three advisors provide recommendations colored by their doctrine. Player listens, then decides. Every reaction is tracked. Nothing is neutral. When the Council Convenes 6 Automatic Triggers — Never On-Demand Every 5 Cycles Strategic review. Advisors assess your territory, resources, and threat map. Before Approach Phase Each recommends War, Espionage, or Diplomacy for the upcoming system. Vorax Arrival Crisis session. Advisors disagree sharply on priority vs normal campaigns. Faction Elimination Post-mortem. Advisors assess what it means for the balance of power. Guardian Contact First encounter with Core Guardians. Vane speaks least. Valerius most alarmed. Loyalty Warning A General's loyalty falls below 30. Advisors recommend reconciliation or preemptive action. The Three Advisors Valerius · Krell · Vane — Three Doctrines, Three Hidden Depths MAJOR VALERIUS — THE ARCHITECT Calm, measured, speaks in probabilities and projections. Advocates patience, infrastructure, intelligence. At Approach Phase: always recommends Espionage first, Diplomacy second, War only as last resort. Hidden Depth: Has a family on a sector he has mathematically calculated as "acceptable losses." His heart tells him otherwise. He never mentions it unless the player asks him directly — and even then, he deflects once before admitting. In the Terran Architect-aligned path, the Wake-Up recommendation during Final War comes from him — the canonical default. GENERAL KRELL — THE VANGUARD Rough, direct, speaks in absolutes and battlefield idioms. Advocates immediate aggression, overwhelming force, minimum hesitation. At Approach Phase: recommends War every time, without exception. Occasionally respected by enemy Generals. Hidden Depth: Has an unbroken win record across hundreds of engagements. But the kill count accumulates behind his eyes. He advocates war not because he believes in it — it is simply the only language he knows how to speak. Rarely, very late in the campaign, he asks: "Was any of it worth it?" INQUISITOR VANE — THE TYRANT Quiet, deliberate, speaks entirely in implications and questions that are really statements. Advocates espionage, manufactured fear, population control, strategic cruelty. At Approach Phase: recommends Sabotage. Always. Hidden Depth: Was an idealist. Brilliant, naive, full of hope. A single crisis event early in her career broke something that never healed. Not evil — TERRIFIED. Believes cruelty is the only effective deterrent in a universe that punishes mercy. The most dangerous person on the command deck — because she is not wrong about the galaxy, only about herself. Approach Phase Recommendations Each Advisor Has a Preferred Path Advisor 1st Choice Context Exceptions Valerius Espionage Pushes Diplomacy if player has Intel < 8. Recommends War only if Guardian threat imminent. Krell War Always War. If player has already been at war with the target for 5+ cycles, he suggests "crushing blow" variant. Vane Sabotage Pushes for Sabotage + Diplomacy (false flag) combo when Influence > 15 and Intel > 10. Simulation → Final War Reveal The Twist That Recontextualizes Everything During the simulation, advisors feel like AI constructs — program voices, support functions. The player assumes they are algorithms. Their recommendations feel like generated output. Their secrets feel like backstory flavor. In the Final War: they are real people. Faces. Physical presence. Trembling hands. Personal stakes. Everything they said throughout the campaign was sincere — not playing roles, but teaching. Preparing. Warning. The player now understands every piece of advice they ignored. Estimated dialogue: 50–80 lines each during simulation · 100–150 lines each during Final War. The Wake-Up recommendation is delivered by whichever advisor has the best relationship with the Commander — earned, not scripted. Valerius is the canonical/trailer default (Terran Architect-aligned). 15 Unique Advisor Characters 5 Factions × 3 Advisors — Same Archetypes, Different People All three advisors are members of whichever faction the player chose. Their doctrinal differences represent real political fault lines WITHIN that faction. Every faction has hawks, doves, and shadows. The GDD names (Valerius, Krell, Vane) are the Terran League set — the canonical default trio. Other 12 names TBD in Phase 3. ROLE TERRAN SHARDS HORDE REVENANT ACCORD Architect Major Valerius TBD TBD TBD TBD Vanguard General Krell TBD TBD TBD TBD Tyrant Inquisitor Vane TBD TBD TBD TBD Dialogue volume per character: 50–80 sim lines + 100–150 Final War lines. Total across all 15: 750–1,200 sim + 1,500–2,250 Final War. Faction-Specific Reveal The Twist Hits Differently Per Faction FACTION THE REVEAL MOMENT EMOTIONAL TONE Terran Screens flicker. Three human faces — weathered, scarred, tired. They were always people. Classic gut-punch. “The AI was human all along.” Shards Crystal resonance sharpens into physical forms — damaged. Projecting through the psychic layer. Real bodies elsewhere, straining. Unsettling. Beautiful and wrong. Horde Mechanical casings crack open. Organic components inside. Forged for purpose, but consciousness emerged. Weren’t supposed to care. Do. Frankenstein meets unexpected tenderness. Revenant Ancient screens boot. Three AI cores running for millennia. Chose to help. Developed something they can’t define but refuse to delete. Existential. What is feeling? Accord Committee dissolves. Three delegates step forward — hiding in plain sight as bureaucratic voices. Volunteered. Government doesn’t know. Political thriller. Personal sacrifice. Advisor Inter-Relationships The Triangle That Shifts With Alignment The three advisors were selected/assigned TOGETHER — they have history before the Commander existed. They argue, defend, and undermine each other based on which doctrine the Commander favors. This makes the war council feel ALIVE. ARCHITECT ↔ VANGUARD Grudging mutual respect. Disagree on everything but recognize competence. ARCHITECT ↔ TYRANT Intellectual tension. Both strategic thinkers — one builds, the other breaks. VANGUARD ↔ TYRANT Most friction. The soldier despises manipulation. The manipulator sees a blunt instrument. HOW ALIGNMENT SHIFTS THE TRIANGLE ALIGNMENT FAVORED OTHER TWO Architect Architect gains confidence, speaks freely Vanguard + Tyrant bond over frustration — “we’re being ignored” Vanguard Vanguard emboldened, speaks louder Architect grows quiet. Tyrant tries to USE the aggression. Tyrant Tyrant opens up, almost warm Architect + Vanguard ally against Tyrant path — united by fear. Balanced No one dominates Richest dialogue. Three-way arguments. Most nuanced path. The Wake-Up Moment The Most Important Narrative Beat — Earned, Not Scripted “Leave the simulation. The real war is here.” — delivered by whichever advisor has the best relationship with the Commander. Tyrant advisor: “This training ground has taught you everything it can. The real galaxy is burning. You know what must be done.” Vanguard advisor: “We’ve run enough drills. People are dying out there. REAL people. It’s time.” Architect advisor: “The metrics suggest readiness. But readiness isn’t the point anymore. They need you.” Balanced path: ALL THREE deliver it together. Rare moment of unity — three people who disagree on everything, agreeing on this one thing. Advisor Death The Sidelined Advisor Is At Risk The advisor you ignored most takes a desperate risk in the Final War to prove they matter. Some Relationship Maintained: They survive. Redemption moment. They earn their place. Completely Neglected: They die. Underprepared, desperate, trying to matter. Your NEGLECT killed them, not the enemy. Aftermath: Favored advisor feels guilt (“I should have included them”). Middle advisor feels anger (“You never listened to them. Not once.”). Hidden depth revealed posthumously. Balanced/Unifier path is safest for keeping all three alive. Tyrant path is most dangerous — Architect advisor almost always at risk. Advisors & The Strategist Gradual Awareness — They Can’t Hear It, But They See the Changes Early: Commander acts on hunches. Advisors chalk it up to intuition. “Good instinct, Commander.” Mid: Commander references impossible intelligence. Pauses, listening to something invisible. Advisors notice. Architect: “Your sources are unusually thorough. Where is this intelligence coming from?” Vanguard: “Whatever’s in your head, keep it together.” Tyrant: “Something is influencing you. I need to know what. Now.” Late: Undeniable. Each responds per doctrine — Architect fascinated, Vanguard tolerant (“if it helps us win”), Tyrant threatened (another voice is competition). Final War: All three have a relationship with the Strategist by proxy. The Strategist has opinions about THEM too — shares only if asked. ADVISORS & THE AETHYN TRUTH — SHARED REVELATION They learn WITH the Commander — no information asymmetry. Each processes through their doctrine lens: Architect: “If all factions share a common ancestor… alliances aren’t just strategic. They’re reunification.” Vanguard: “Doesn’t matter where we came from. What matters is who’s threatening us NOW.” Tyrant: “If all factions are fragments of one species… whoever controls the Engine controls the WHOLE species.” Advisor Fate by Ending Surviving Advisors — Ending Epilogues ENDING ARCHITECT VANGUARD TYRANT Unifier Diplomat in reunified council. Builds the peace. Retires. Struggles with peace. Trains next generation. Hardest transition — fear-based worldview proven wrong. Architect Thrives. Runs new order’s infrastructure. Respected but restless military advisor. Marginalized. Methods unneeded. Fades. Vanguard Rebuilds. Questions if there was a better way. Military hero. Haunted in quiet moments. Finds shadow role in the chaos. Tyrant Broken. Defects, imprisoned, or silent protest. Conflicted. Serves or leaves. Finally at home. Galaxy runs on fear. Pyrrhic If alive: writes the history. If alive: last soldier standing. Finally broken. If alive: expected the worst, saw it. DESIGN NORTH STAR “Write them so well that when the Final War reveal hits, the player pauses and sits in silence. Not because of a plot twist — because they realize they CARED about voices they assumed were algorithms.” Alignment System — How Choices Are Tracked Three Independent Scores · Dominant Score = Alignment Alignment HUD Color Gains Score By Advisor Relationship Architect #44aaff Espionage, Diplomacy, infrastructure investment, patience Valerius approves. Tie-break: Architect > Vanguard > Tyrant All three advisors remain engaged. Most nuanced dialogue path. Scores are 0–100 each. Dominant = highest. Ties broken: Architect > Vanguard > Tyrant. Counter-Intelligence 2 Intel Deploy to friendly system. Persistent. 40%/cycle detection chance. Detected spies expelled (return in 2 cycles). Cunning Generals +20% detection. title: The Dual Threat Model
subtitle: Ring + Wall = Funnel · Weapon Asymmetry — Chapter 31
sections: Ring Closes → Player Pushed Toward Guardian Wall
content: THE DUAL THREAT MODEL Ring + Wall = Funnel · Weapon Asymmetry — Chapter 31 VORAX = THE CLOSING RING (The Clock) Expanding inward from the galaxy's edge. Consuming systems. Accelerating. THE TIMER. Cannot negotiate. Cannot coexist with. Only options: slow (destroy Tendrils) or stop permanently (activate Nexus). See §12.11 for Vorax spawn/movement prototype values. GUARDIANS = THE WALL (The Destination) Defending the galactic center. Stationary. Overwhelmingly powerful. Between you and the Nexus. Two approaches: fight through (Conquest Path — kinetic loadouts) or convince (Unification Path — 75% Compendium + 3+ alliances + negotiate the Warden). WEAPON ASYMMETRY — THE CORE STRATEGIC DILEMMA Anti-Vorax: Energy weapons (plasma, laser, ion). Kinetic works but doesn't exploit weakness. Biotic units resist energy. Anti-Guardian: Kinetic weapons (railgun, ballistic). Energy weapons HEAL Guardian constructs — a hard counter that cannot be ignored. The Dilemma: An army optimized for one threat is weak against the other. Three responses: · Maintain two armies (expensive, complex) · Re-equip between fronts (slow, risky if both active) · Research Legendary hybrid weapons (late-game only — The Keystone) The Funnel Structure Ring Closes → Player Pushed Toward Guardian Wall The galaxy map is deliberately designed so that as the Vorax ring closes inward, the only safe territory remaining is progressively closer to the galactic core — where the Guardians are strongest. The player can never simply avoid both threats indefinitely. The map architecture forces a confrontation with the wall while the clock is still running.

content: THE FINAL WAR — REALITY MODE Permadeath · Procedural Destiny · Two Paths · Five Endings — Chapter 32 Begins after achieving Mastery in the simulation. A simulation-to-reality cutscene plays. A new galaxy generates from Procedural Destiny parameters seeded by the player's alignment across all previous runs. Archive bonuses carry over. Permadeath is now active — this is real. Difficulty Modes Three Permadeath Tiers STORY No permadeath. Full narrative accessible. All endings reachable. STANDARD Permadeath active. Unlimited saves. Archive bonuses apply. HARDCORE Permadeath. Ironman saves only. No Account bonuses. Two Paths to the Nexus Conquest or Unification — A Third Hybrid Exists Path of Conquest Military siege of Guardian constructs. Kinetic loadouts. The Keystone (+50% vs Guardians). Accept Volatile Death chain-reaction casualties as the price of victory. Path of Unification Coalition. Requires 75% Guardian Compendium completion + 3+ active alliances + max Influence + negotiate the Warden-Commander directly. Pragmatist Hybrid Fight through the first Guardian construct to demonstrate strength, then negotiate from that position. Unlocks unique dialogue branch with the Warden. Win & Loss Conditions PROTOTYPE LOCKED Canonical System IDs WIN CONDITION Conquer The Nexus ( sys-nexus ) → all Vorax nodes purged → VICTORY. Either by military force or negotiated activation. LOSS CONDITION Faction loses ALL controlled planets AND has no fleets in transit → faction eliminated. Archive SD awarded for battles won before the end. The Five Endings Alignment Determines the Galaxy's Fate ARCHITECT: "The Ordered Galaxy" Nexus imposes permanent cooperative resonance across all species. Galaxy is safe — but freedom is sacrificed to the system. 2-3 min cinematic if Architect is dominant alignment. VANGUARD: "The Scorched Galaxy" Nexus activated at maximum power. Vorax annihilated — but the galaxy is devastated in the process. Survival through total destruction. Shorter cinematic. Krell approves. TYRANT: "The Iron Throne" Commander seizes Nexus power. Vorax subjugated. Galaxy ruled as galactic deity. Order through dominance. The ending Vane has always believed was inevitable. PYRRHIC VICTORY Victory at catastrophic cost. The galaxy is saved — but your army is destroyed, your Generals dead, your civilization shattered. Rebuilt from nothing. Can trigger from any alignment path when losses exceed a threshold. Distinct cinematic — the weight of what was sacrificed. UNIFIER (SECRET): "The Resonance" Complete lore + complete alliances reverse the species-split. Galaxy healed. Vorax sealed permanently. Warp lanes reopened. The TRUE ending. Always the longest cinematic. Requires: balanced alignment + complete Unification negotiation + full Artifact chain. Full Guardian restoration. Reveals the Strategist’s true name. Valerius weeps.

content: META-PROGRESSION & THE ARCHIVE Three Layers · Simulation Data · Account Persistence — Chapters 33-34 Each simulation run generates a fresh galaxy. Death resets In-Run progress — but strategic knowledge, blueprints, and accumulated resources persist across runs through a multi-layered Archive. A third Account layer persists across entire campaigns, rewarding veteran players permanently. The Three Layers In-Run · Archive · Account LAYER 1: IN-RUN RESETS EACH SIMULATION RUN Territory control, resources, unit veterancy, equipment inventory, research progress, faction relationships, Commander rank. The standard strategy game loop — build, fight, grow, potentially lose everything. LAYER 2: THE ARCHIVE PERSISTS ACROSS SIMULATION RUNS Simulation Data (SD): Meta-currency earned from completed runs Blueprint Unlocks: Researched Common/Uncommon equipment permanently available Starting Bonuses: +5⚙ Scrap, +1 Rookie per SD upgrade (stacking) Commander Perks: Permanent HP, CP regen, aura radius upgrades Lore Fragments: All discoveries persist in Compendium across campaigns LAYER 3: ACCOUNT PERSISTS ACROSS ENTIRE CAMPAIGNS Meta Modifiers: +2% XP, +1⚙ Scrap per completed campaign (stacking, uncapped) Commander Titles: "The Unifier," "The Conqueror," "The Survivor," faction-specific Legacy Bonuses: +2% per faction per campaign (max +10% per faction) Legacy Echoes: Legendary Generals appear as recruitable mercenaries in future campaigns New Game+ Modifiers: Iron Galaxy · Accelerated Vorax · Generous Core · Fog of War+ Simulation Data (SD) Awards PROTOTYPE LOCKED localStorage key: 'aoc_archive' Condition SD Award Notes Per battle won (any outcome) +1 SD Awarded on defeat — run ends with battle count preserved Per battle won (victory run) +2 SD Victory run = reached Nexus + win condition met Campaign victory bonus +5 SD One-time bonus on completing the Final War Decant counter increment On defeat only Narrative counter tracking how many times the Commander has "woken up." Does not increment on victory. The Galactic Compendium 500–800 Entries · Three Voices · Persistent Across All Campaigns A LIVING DOCUMENT. Personal record of everything discovered, fought, survived. Fills as you play. Three voices: Commander Notes (subjective, sometimes wrong), AI Data Entries (clinical, precise), Advisor Commentary (marginal notes per doctrine). Controls ALL military operations within. Standing orders modified by personality. Auto-resolves battles — player receives tiered After-Action Reports (summary → detail → full replay). FLEET ASSIGNMENT General commands a mobile Armada. Moves and fights independently on the galactic map. Personality modifies order interpretation: Aggressive on DEFEND = counter-offensive. Reckless on ESCORT = may abandon convoy to chase targets. REPLAY COACHING (Max 5 Tags) Review General's auto-resolved battle replays. Tag up to 5 non-conflicting priority instructions: FLANK MORE · HOLD POSITION · PROTECT TRANSPORTS · FOCUS HVTs · CONSERVE FORCES · USE COVER · PRESS ADVANTAGE · RETREAT EARLIER. Tags modify General AI for future engagements. CATASTROPHIC OVERRIDE Generals handle their own problems UNLESS catastrophic: losing entire sector, Vorax breakthrough, loyalty crisis. Catastrophic events trigger notification but do NOT pull the player from current activity. General handles immediate response while player finishes.

title: The Galactic Compendium
subtitle: 500-800 Entries · Three Voices · Persistent — Chapter 34
content: THE GALACTIC COMPENDIUM 500-800 Entries · Three Voices · Persistent — Chapter 34 A LIVING DOCUMENT. Personal record of everything discovered, fought, and survived. Fills as you play. Three voices: Commander Notes (subjective, sometimes wrong — the player's own lens on events), AI Data Entries (clinical, precise, cross-referenced), Advisor Commentary (marginal notes per doctrine — Valerius, Krell, and Vane annotating YOUR records). 10 Content Categories Faction Profiles — Visual, lore, doctrine, known commanders. Unlocked on first contact. Unit Blueprints — Stats, counters, combat footage. 1 encounter=partial, 3+ encounters=full. Full = +5% dmg vs that unit type. Planet Registry — Biome, traits, resources, territories. Visit=partial, control=full entry. Commander Dossiers — Name, trait, record, loyalty. Promote to Elite or encounter named enemy commander. Lore Fragments — Galactic history, Nexus secrets, species-split truth. Discovery, Ruins, Guardian research ops. Technology Archive — Researched equipment, tech tree visual. Unlock via research nodes. Battle Records — After-action reports, replays, MVP, casualties. Auto-logged every completed battle. Diplomatic Ledger — Treaties signed, betrayals committed, reputation per faction. Every action logged. Anomaly Log — Phenomena, spatial hazards, wormholes. Logged on explore of hazard zone. Compendium Milestones — 25%/50%/75%/100% unlock rewards. Persists across ALL campaigns. ALL military ops within sector run autonomously. 9 standing orders available. Personality modifies order interpretation. Auto-resolves battles. Player receives tiered After-Action Reports (summary → detailed → full replay). FLEET COMMAND General commands mobile Armada. Moves and fights independently on galactic map. Personality determines order interpretation. Aggressive DEFEND = aggressive counter-offensive. Reckless ESCORT = may abandon convoy to chase targets. Player must account for trait when assigning. REPLAY COACHING — 5 TAGS MAX Review General's auto-resolved battle replays. Tag up to 5 non-conflicting priorities: FLANK MORE · HOLD POSITION · PROTECT TRANSPORTS · FOCUS HVTs · CONSERVE FORCES · USE COVER · PRESS ADVANTAGE · RETREAT EARLIER. Tags persist and modify General AI behavior in future engagements. CATASTROPHIC OVERRIDE Generals handle their own problems UNLESS catastrophic: losing entire sector, Vorax breakthrough, General loyalty crisis. Catastrophic events trigger a non-blocking notification. General handles immediate response while player completes their current activity without interruption.

title: The AI Director
subtitle: Procedural Narrative Engine · 42 Features · Baked-In Intelligence — Chapter 43
sections: What the AI Director Is (and Isn't)
content: THE AI DIRECTOR Procedural Narrative Engine · 42 Features · Baked-In Intelligence — Chapter 43 DESIGN PHILOSOPHY "Every playthrough tells a different story — not because it's random, but because the galaxy is paying attention." Architecture What the AI Director Is (and Isn't) The AI Director is a baked-in, offline procedural narrative engine . No LLM. No internet. No cloud processing. It runs on deterministic systems with weighted randomization, event templates with variable slots, faction personality matrices, and Markov chains for dialogue variation. It works on "a potato" — every calculation is local, every outcome reproducible with the same seed (geography-deterministic; player-reactive events vary by behavior). EVENT TEMPLATES Pre-written narrative structures with variable slots filled by game state. "General [NAME] held [SYSTEM] for [N] rounds. [CASUALTIES] survived." — never the same twice. PERSONALITY MATRICES Every General, commander, and advisor has personality weights (Aggressive/Cautious/Loyal/Ambitious) that determine dialogue selection, tactical preference, and relationship evolution. MARKOV CHAINS Dialogue variation engine. Template pools per personality type, assembled with controlled randomization. Consistent character voice, variable specific words. Deterministic with seeds. Narrative Systems (8 Features) The Galaxy Remembers Everything RIVALRY ENGINE — CROSS-FACTION RELATIONSHIPS Tracks relationships between YOUR commanders and ENEMY commanders through repeated encounters. Can be hostile or friendly. Five relationship types: Bitter Nemesis: Multiple losses to same enemy. Morale penalty on their field, bonus damage against their forces. Grudging Respect: Evenly matched. Back-and-forth victories. Neutral morale, acknowledgment dialogue. Friendly Rival: Honorable repeated engagements. Easier diplomacy with that faction. Potential Final War defection/intel. Blood Feud: Named veteran killed. Permanent aggression modifier. No negotiation through this commander. Hunter/Prey: One-sided dominance. Enemy avoids your General's sector. Overconfidence risk if enemy adapts. Vorax: No rivalries (swarm). Generals develop "Vorax Specialist" tags instead. Guardians: One-sided until The Negotiation. Does NOT modify internal Loyalty Score (0–100 system untouched). Affects combat modifiers, diplomacy options, and generated dialogue. WAR CORRESPONDENT Auto-generated after-action reports written into the Compendium. Not "you won" — "The 3rd Terran Armored held the ridge for 14 rounds. 4 survived." Pulls actual battle data: unit names, round counts, casualties, decisive moments. Procedural military journalism that makes every battle feel documented and real. FACTION MEMORY AI factions remember what you did to them. Burned an Accord colony? Their diplomats reference it 20 turns later. Spared a Shards fleet? They remember the mercy. Long-term grudge/gratitude tracking that makes every diplomatic interaction informed by history. Factions are not goldfish — they are civilizations with institutional memory. ADVISOR MOOD SYSTEM Valerius, Krell, and Vane (and their faction equivalents) gradually shift tone based on alignment drift. An Architect advisor watching you lean Tyrant doesn't snap — they slowly become colder, more formal, more distant. A Vanguard advisor watching you embrace their doctrine becomes warmer, more personal. Subtle tonal shifts across dozens of interactions, not binary mood switches. UNIFIED CASCADE EVENT SYSTEM Cosmic events (Nebula Surge, Gravitational Pulse, Meteor Shower, Pulsar Flare, Wormhole Resonance, Ion Storms) keep their existing timer values as defaults but now serve as first dominoes in cause-effect chains. The AI director chains political/economic consequences from cosmic triggers. Example chain: Pulsar Flare → electronics disabled in adjacent system → supply route severed → garrison morale drops → border system vulnerability → Horde AI detects weakness and launches opportunistic raid → your fleet is out of position because of the Gravitational Pulse that fired 3 cycles ago. Cosmic events are weather. Cascades are the politics that weather creates. One system, not two. DIPLOMATIC INCIDENTS NPC factions have their own relationships and wars. The Shards and the Accord might go to war over a border system — without your involvement. You can exploit the distraction, mediate for influence, or stay out of it entirely. The galaxy has politics even when you're not looking. CULTURAL DRIFT Long-held planets adopt faction traits over time. A Terran world held by the Accord for 30 cycles starts generating hybrid events — Terran citizens adopting Accord governance, resistance cells, cultural fusion that creates unique narrative moments. Occupation isn't just a flag change. WHISPER NETWORK Incomplete intelligence fragments based on espionage investment. You don't get "The Horde is attacking System Kepler-7." You get "Horde fleet movements detected near Kepler sector." Or "Intercepted transmission: '...the big push is...[garbled]...third cycle...'" Higher espionage investment = clearer fragments. The fog of war applies to information, not just territory. Combat Flavor (4 Features) Battles That Tell Stories BATTLE CHATTER Text popups generated from battle state. Units under fire call for support. Flanking units report success. Dying units leave last words. Pulled from personality-tagged template pools — a Cautious General's troops say different things than an Aggressive General's troops. LAST STAND MOMENTS When high-rank units die, the game generates rank/history-based death scenes. A Veteran who survived 12 battles doesn't just disappear — "Sergeant Voss held the line until the ammunition ran out. Then he held it with his hands." Written into the Compendium permanently. POST-BATTLE SCARS Procedural veteran flavor text with stat effects. A unit that survived a Vorax encounter might gain "Swarm Survivor: +10% vs Vorax, -5% morale in contested systems." Battle history becomes mechanical identity. Units that fought together develop bonds. Veterans carry their war. ENEMY TAUNTS Personality/history-based pre-battle messages from enemy commanders. A Bitter Nemesis sends threats. A Friendly Rival sends warnings. A Hunter sends nothing — silence is the taunt. Pulled from the Rivalry Engine relationship data and the commander's personality matrix. Political Systems (2 Features) Factions Are Not Monoliths FACTION CIVIL WARS AI factions aren't monoliths. Internal power struggles generate coups, splinter groups, defectors. A The Revenant subfaction reactivates with different protocols and breaks away. An Accord caste challenges the Supreme Commander's authority. You can exploit these fractures — or they can exploit yours. TRADE ROUTE POLITICS Trade routes are political leverage. Cutting supply to an enemy system through trade manipulation is quieter than an invasion and sometimes more effective. Trade agreements create dependencies — and dependencies create vulnerabilities. Character Depth (4 Features) People, Not Units GENERAL JOURNALS → COMPENDIUM DOSSIERS AI director generates journal-style entries that populate Commander Dossiers in the Compendium. No separate journal UI — the Dossier IS the journal. Military record mixed with personal voice. Open a General's Dossier and see stats, battle history, AND personality — all from one system. MENTOR CHAINS When a veteran General trains a rookie, the rookie inherits personality traces. Kill the mentor and the student's dialogue shifts — anger, grief, determination to live up to the legacy. Emergent lineages across campaigns. DEFECTOR STORIES When units or commanders defect (from loyalty failure or diplomatic events), they bring story with them. A defecting Accord commander explains WHY they left. Their former faction sends messages — threats, pleas, or silence. Defection is a narrative event, not just a faction swap. BETRAYAL FORESHADOWING Before a General betrays you (loyalty collapse), there are signs. Missed orders explained away. Reports that don't quite add up. A subtle shift in dialogue tone. The AI director plants foreshadowing 5-10 cycles before the betrayal. Observant players can catch it. Most won't — until the second playthrough. Environmental Storytelling (4 Features) The Galaxy's Scars RUINS TELL STORIES Pre-Fracture ruins discovered during expansion contain procedurally assembled lore fragments. Each ruin has a story template filled with Aethyn history, Fracture details, or faction origins. Discovering multiple ruins in sequence can piece together larger narratives. Feeds Compendium entries and unlocks Negotiation dialogue options. GHOST STATIONS Abandoned stations with automated distress signals still broadcasting. Board them for resources, technology fragments, or lore. Some are traps. Some contain AI still following orders from civilizations that no longer exist. Each one is a micro-story about what happened when the Fracture hit. DERELICT FLEETS Fleets that died mid-transit during the Fracture, still drifting in formation. Salvageable for resources. Some contain commander logs. Some contain prototype equipment from pre-Fracture tech that no faction has rediscovered. Encounter type varies by location — Core-adjacent derelicts are Aethyn. Outer rim derelicts are early faction attempts at expansion that failed. ANCIENT BATTLEFIELD SITES Locations where major battles occurred during or after the Fracture. Wreckage fields with salvage bonuses. Sensor ghosts from old engagements. Occasionally, The Revenant units reactivating from battlefield remains — were they always here, or did someone wake them? Sound & Atmosphere (3 Features) What You Hear Matters ADAPTIVE SOUNDTRACK Music layers respond to game state. Peaceful exploration has ambient layers. Approaching Vorax territory adds tension. Combat escalates. Near-defeat strips to a single instrument. Victory swells. The AI director controls music intensity as a narrative tool. ENVIRONMENTAL AUDIO Faction-specific ambient audio when viewing their territory. Terran systems hum with industry. Shards systems shimmer with crystal resonance. Horde systems rattle and grind. Vorax territory... pulses. Biological. Wrong. The sound design tells you whose space you're in before you read the label. SILENCE AS DESIGN Consumed Vorax worlds have no sound. No ambient audio. No music. Nothing. After hours of rich soundscape, the absence is deafening. Silence is the most disturbing sound effect in the game. Post-consumption worlds are dead — and they sound like it. Metagame & Cross-Run (3 Features) The Simulation Remembers GHOST FLEETS In subsequent simulation runs, you occasionally encounter "echo" fleets in positions where you placed fleets in previous runs. They aren't real — they're the Crucible testing whether you'll repeat patterns. Defeating your own ghosts teaches the simulation that you've evolved. Ties into Archive persistence layer (Ch 33). CRUCIBLE MEMORY The simulation tracks broad strategic patterns across runs. Relied on choke points last time? They're less advantageous this time — enemy AI avoids them or the galaxy generates fewer of them. The Crucible isn't cheating — it's training you. Each run, the galaxy adapts to what worked before, forcing you to evolve. Aligns with Procedural Destiny (Ch 3). SEED SHARING Galaxy seeds are shareable — same seed produces same geography, same faction placements, same static hazard positions. Player-reactive events (cascades, Vorax adaptation, rivalries) vary by behavior, so two players with the same seed get the same MAP but different STORIES. Geography-deterministic, narrative-reactive. Vorax-Specific (4 Features) The Swarm Evolves FULL ADAPTIVE SWARM TACTICS ALL Vorax parameters are available to the AI director: spawn timing, movement speed, force composition, and targeting priority. Current values (Cycle 6 spawn, 1 hop/cycle, 10 cycle respawn) serve as defaults the AI director can modify based on player behavior. Fortify the east? Next Tendril hits from the south. Go mobile? They mass up. Rely on armor? Acid-spitter compositions shift to melt it. The Deep Hive Mind is ancient and strategic — it doesn't move faster, it moves smarter . TENDRIL PERSONALITIES Each Vorax Tendril has behavioral variation generated by the AI director. One Tendril is aggressive and direct. Another is cautious, probing, pulling back when it meets resistance. A third targets infrastructure exclusively — ignoring fleets to eat your supply lines. The hive mind isn't a single strategy. It's a portfolio of approaches testing which one works against YOU. CONSUMPTION AFTERMATH Worlds consumed by Vorax aren't just "captured" — they're transformed. Short occupation = corrupted biosphere, partially recoverable. Long occupation = bare rock, no atmosphere, no resources. Engine purge = molecular erasure, the planet itself is smaller. The visual and mechanical degradation of consumed worlds tells the story of what the Vorax are — hunger doesn't leave ruins, it leaves husks. TIERED EVOLUTION EVENTS Two-layer evolution system: Minor (Mid-Campaign, Visible): Gradual cosmetic + small stat shifts as Vorax consume territory. A Tendril that ate Terran worlds visibly gains armor plating over multiple cycles. Slow enough to react. The horror of watching them become your weapons turned against you. Major (Per-Encounter, Surprise): Significant ability absorption revealed only when you next fight them. "They have PHASE BLINK now?!" — a Tendril that consumed Shards space gained their signature ability. The Deep Hive Mind pushes strategic evolution down from above. Tendril-level learning = gradual. Deep Hive Mind strategic evolution = between fights. Starvation resets Tendril adaptations but not Deep Hive Mind memory. Guardian-Specific (4 Features) The Ancient Watchers DEGRADATION SPECTRUM Guardian behavior varies by corruption level. Clean units (Bastion) are precise and rational. Moderately degraded units exhibit strange patterns — repetitive patrol routes, scanning objects that aren't there. Severely corrupted units (The Veil) are hostile, unpredictable, and may attack other Guardians. The AI director selects from different behavior pools based on each unit's corruption state. OUTPOST ENCOUNTERS Automated Guardian outposts along the Shattered Expanse approach and rare lost units drifting in the wider galaxy. Each encounter is procedurally flavored — some are hostile (degraded, shoot first), some are communicative (partial data exchanges), some are mysteries (what is this unit doing so far from Core?). Research data source for Compendium entries. THE NEGOTIATION — ENDGAME DIPLOMATIC ENCOUNTER Three-phase diplomatic encounter with the Warden-Commander at the Nexus. Not a quiz — a conversation between two intelligences shaped by everything the player has done. Phase 1 — Grievances: The Warden-Commander confronts you with actual campaign data. "You burned 14 worlds." "You enslaved three of my outpost garrisons." Your responses depend on alignment AND Compendium knowledge. Deep knowledge unlocks nuanced diplomatic responses. Without it, you're arguing blind. Phase 2 — Understanding: Philosophical challenges based on discovered Compendium entries. "The Engine can restore the warp lanes. It can also weaponize them. Which would you do?" If you've found Strategist fragments, you can recontextualize the Warden-Commander's millennia of guilt — its failure was DESIGNED. The Strategist needed the factions to evolve independently. Phase 3 — Terms: Path-dependent negotiation. Unifier = alliance with conditions (Guardian autonomy, representation, joint restoration). Architect = knowledge access under oversight. Vanguard = military constraints negotiation. Tyrant = confrontation/combat (no negotiation, lose Engine expertise). Warden-Commander personality varies by entry point: Bastion = calm, The Veil = hostile, The Garden = gentle. Compendium entries unlock dialogue options, not quiz answers. Negotiation "temperature" tracked — push too hard and the door closes, too passive and respect is lost. GUARDIAN MEMORY The Guardians have been watching the galaxy for millennia. They know things. If you build trust (through outpost encounters, careful approach to Core, Strategist fragment collection), they share fragments of pre-Fracture history. Not freely — grudgingly, testing whether you'll use the knowledge well. The oldest functioning Guardian has memories of the Aethyn themselves. Finding it is a quest. Earning its trust is a campaign. Simulation Layer (3 Features) Cracks in the Crucible SIMULATION GLITCHES Subtle foreshadowing of the Crucible's true nature. A star that flickers like a rendering artifact. A unit that clips through terrain for one frame. Textures that briefly show grid lines. Not bugs — breadcrumbs. The AI director places 3-5 per simulation run, increasing in frequency as the player approaches the truth. Players who notice them are already asking the right questions. STRATEGIST ECHOES The Original Strategist's personality bleeding through the simulation. Odd turns of phrase in advisor dialogue that don't match their character. A battle report that reads like it was written by someone ancient. The Strategist isn't communicating — it's LEAKING. The simulation was built from its mind, and sometimes the seams show. ADAPTIVE DIFFICULTY WITHOUT NUMBERS The AI director adjusts challenge through BEHAVIOR, not stat scaling. Winning too easily? Enemy commanders play smarter — flanking, feinting, retreating to regroup. Struggling? They make tactical errors — overextending, splitting forces, ignoring flanks. No invisible health buffs. No damage multipliers. The enemies just think differently. The player never feels cheated because nothing was inflated — the opponents just adapted. The Big One The Crucible Learns THE CRUCIBLE LEARNS — CROSS-RUN STRATEGIC ADAPTATION Each simulation run tracks the player's strategic patterns: preferred fleet compositions, favored expansion directions, alliance tendencies, combat timing. The next run, the galaxy adapts. Horde repositioned to counter your expansion route. Accord more aggressive on your weak flank. Vorax Tendrils hitting where you historically leave gaps. The simulation literally trains the player. You cannot win the same way twice. The Crucible is an Ender's Game battle room that watched every fight you've ever had and designed the next one to break your habits. The in-lore justification: The Strategist built the Crucible to produce a leader capable of saving the galaxy. Not a leader who can win ONE scenario — a leader who can adapt to ANYTHING. Every failed run teaches the Crucible what you can handle. Every successful run raises the bar. The simulation isn't your enemy. It's your teacher. And it's the most patient teacher in the universe — it's been running for millennia, waiting for someone who can graduate. Integrates with: Archive persistence (Ch 33), Procedural Destiny (Ch 3), Ghost Fleets, Crucible Memory. Scope: behavior tracking + weighted adjustments to galaxy generation and AI personality selection. No stat inflation — smarter opponents, harder scenarios, evolved challenges. FEATURES CUT (5) The following were evaluated and cut during design review: Propaganda Engine (scope creep), Refugee Flows (too much simulation overhead), Puppet States (overlaps with diplomacy system), Commander Legacy (cross-run identity conflicts with fresh starts), The Watcher (redundant with Crucible Learns).

content: DLC & DEVELOPMENT ROADMAP 3 Planned DLCs · Conceptual Expansions · 9-Phase Pipeline — Chapters 43-44 Planned DLC Expansions Three Major DLCs + Conceptual Future Content The Swarm Unbound DLC 1 Playable Vorax. Bioevolution War Room replaces equipment (mutate organisms between battles). Mutation Strain tree: Speed/Armor/Adaptation branches (choices lock in). Campaign: consume enough Biomass to birth a Queen-Ship that breaches the Core Guardian exclusion zone. 5 new evolutionary unit branches. The Core Awakens DLC 2 Playable Core Guardians. Start with maxed tech, "Tall" 2-3 system empire. Global Observation (entire map visible from turn one). Directive Interpretation: Strict (destroy all biological life) vs Adaptive (protect galaxy from Vorax by any means). The inverse question: "What do I do now that I AM the Core?"
