L.3.1 Physical Dimensions (UNIT_SCALE_TABLE) UNIT HEIGHT (m) COL RADIUS COL HEIGHT HP BAR Y Shock-Marine 1.8 0.30 1.80 2.50 Hellfire-Trooper 1.9 0.35 1.90 2.70 Aegis-Walker 4.0 0.50 3.80 5.00 Siege-Breaker Tank 1.5 1.20 1.50 2.20 Titan-Class Leviathan 8.0 1.50 7.00 9.20 Stat-to-meters: game_units / 50.0. HP bar width scales proportionally with collision radius. L.4 Dynamic BattleManager Architecture (Godot 4.4) Config-driven battle orchestrator. Accepts army composition via start_battle(config: Dictionary) . Handles variable army sizes, dynamic formation placement, reserve wave reinforcements, and last-stand mechanics. KEY FILES BattleManager.gd — Orchestrator (spawn, reserves, victory) BattleUnit.gd — Per-unit AI (target, move, attack, death) BattleHUD.gd — HUD (counts, timer, speed controls) BattleCamera.gd — Auto-track camera system BattleArena.tscn — Scene root (ground, zones, lighting) FORMATION CONSTANTS UNIT_SPACING 2.5m — between units in row ROW_GAP 3.5m — between front/back rows TEAM_OFFSET_X 8.0m — distance from center MAX_PER_ROW 5 — wrap threshold L.4.1 Category Sort Weights CATEGORY WEIGHT POSITION Light Infantry 0 Front line Heavy Infantry 1 Front-mid Light Mech 2 Mid line Light Vehicle 3 Mid-rear Heavy Vehicle 4 Rear line Super-Heavy 5 Back rank L.4.2 Reserve Wave System RESERVE DEPLOYMENT Reserves defined per-team in battle config Each wave: unit_id, count, deploy_delay (seconds) Spawns at team back edge (TEAM_OFFSET_X + 6.0m) 1.0s spawn protection (immune + fade-in tween) HUD notification with 2s display + 1s fade LAST-STAND MECHANIC If all field units die but reserves remain: → Force-deploy ALL pending reserves immediately Prevents instant-loss when reinforcements are queued Victory deferred until force-deploy resolves Outcomes: PLAYER WINS / ENEMY WINS / DRAW L.5 Battle Pipeline — 11-Step Combat Formula # OPERATION FORMULA 1 Accuracy Roll rand() < attacker.accuracy → hit or miss 2 Base Damage attacker.attack_damage 3 Critical Hit rand() < crit_chance → dmg × crit_multiplier 4 Evasion Roll rand() < target.evasion → attack dodged 5 Resistance dmg × (1.0 − target.resistances[dmg_type]) 6 Armor Reduction dmg = max(1, dmg − target.armor) 7 Damage Floor max(1, final_dmg) — always at least 1 8 Shield Absorb shield absorbs first; overflow → HP 9 HP Deduction target.current_hp −= remaining_dmg 10 Death Check hp ≤ 0 → emit unit_died, disable, death FX 11 Floating Number DamageNumber3D at target pos (crit = yellow) L.5.1 DEFAULT_TEST_CONFIG (Terran Mirror Match) INITIAL WAVE (×2 TEAMS) 3× Shock-Marine (Light Infantry) 2× Hellfire-Trooper (Heavy Infantry) 1× Aegis-Walker (Light Mech) 1× Siege-Breaker Tank (Heavy Vehicle) 1× Titan-Class Leviathan (Super-Heavy) = 9 units per side initial RESERVES (×2 TEAMS) 2× Shock-Marine @ 30s deploy delay 1× Hellfire-Trooper @ 45s deploy delay = 3 reserves per side Grand total: 11 per team · 22 battlefield L.6 Runtime Bugs Found & Fixed During Godot Testing # BUG / RESOLUTION 1 modulate not declared on CharacterBody3D Symptom: Runtime error — modulate property not found on BattleUnit (CharacterBody3D). Cause: modulate is a CanvasItem property (2D only). BattleUnit extends CharacterBody3D (3D). Fix: Replaced with GeometryInstance3D.transparency. Added recursive _set_mesh_transparency(node, value). Spawn protection tweens 0.7 → 0.0. File: Scripts/Systems/BattleUnit.gd 2 Invalid access to is_alive on previously freed instance Symptom: Crash after first unit death — accessing .is_alive on a freed BattleUnit reference. Cause: Death tween calls queue_free(), but player_units / enemy_units arrays still hold stale references. Fix: Added is_instance_valid(u) guards at all 9 access points across BattleManager.gd (6) and BattleUnit.gd (3). Files: Scripts/Managers/BattleManager.gd, Scripts/Systems/BattleUnit.gd 3 filter() lambda type-cast crash on freed objects Symptom: Victory filters return empty arrays — instant DRAW after first death. Cause: Typed lambda func(u: BattleUnit) — GDScript casts argument before lambda body runs. Freed object fails cast before is_instance_valid() can help. Fix: Removed type hints: func(u) instead of func(u: BattleUnit). Changed result arrays to untyped Array. File: Scripts/Managers/BattleManager.gd (lines 366, 369) 4 Integer division warning in timer display Symptom: Godot console warning on int / int truncation. Cause: int(battle_time) / 60 performs integer division, flagged by GDScript 4.x. Fix: Changed to int(battle_time / 60.0) — float division first, then explicit truncation. File: UI/Screens/BattleHUD.gd (line 51) L.7 Godot 4.4 Engine Gotchas (Cumulative) # GOTCHA DETAIL / WORKAROUND 1 class_name on Autoload scripts Causes naming conflict — don't use on GameDatabase.gd 2 const Dict with Color() constructors May not compile — use var instead of const 3 Array[TypedClass] can't hold freed refs Typed arrays reject freed objects. Use untyped Array when elements may be freed mid-frame. 4 Typed lambda params crash on freed refs func(u: BattleUnit) casts before body runs. Freed object fails cast. Use func(u) untyped. 5 modulate is CanvasItem only (2D) Use GeometryInstance3D.transparency for 3D fade effects. 6 queue_free() doesn't null array refs Always guard with is_instance_valid() at every access point, or remove from arrays on death. 7 .glb models require editor import Automatic on project open — wait for import to complete before running scenes. 8 exponentialRampToValueAtTime(0) Illegal in Web Audio — use 0.0001 instead of 0. 9 e.target.closest() on text nodes Can fail — guard with inline fallback: (e.target.closest ? e.target : {closest:()=>null}) 10 HP bars: QuadMesh billboard Unshaded, no_depth_test, billboard via camera basis copy, left-anchor drain, alpha 0.7. L.8 Balance Observations from First Playtest (Terran Mirror Match) TEST CONFIG: 9 initial + 3 reserves per side · Terran mirror · DEFAULT_TEST_CONFIG OBSERVATION DETAIL RECOMMENDED ACTION Hellfire-Trooper effective range is ~3m Range 150 / 50 = 3m in-game. Classified as ranged Heavy Infantry but fights in melee. Increase range to 250-300, OR add close-range damage bonus. Titan-Class Leviathan dominates end-game 1000 HP makes Titan-vs-Titan last excessively long as final survivors. Reduce HP to 750-800, OR increase anti-heavy damage. Reserve wave timing needs tuning 30s / 45s delays — infantry often dies before reserves arrive. Waves feel disconnected. Test 20s / 35s, or trigger on unit-count threshold. Mirror match tends toward DRAW Expected — identical armies, low variance. Confirms RNG spread is tight. Not a bug. Resolves with asymmetric factions. Full balance pass deferred Observations only — no stat changes made yet. Dedicated balance session needed before non-mirror testing. L.9 Procedural Galaxy Generation: Implementation Specification L.9.1 Galaxy Size Parameters SIZE SYSTEMS NEUTRAL CYCLES/RUN NOTES Small 12 – 14 6 – 8 20 – 30 Tight, early conflict inevitable Medium 16 – 20 10 – 14 30 – 45 Standard balanced experience Large 22 – 26 16 – 20 45 – 65 Deep strategy, room to maneuver Massive 28 – 35 22 – 29 65+ Marathon, maximum complexity Nexus always placed + 2–5 faction homeworlds based on custom difficulty settings. System count stays the same regardless of faction count — fewer factions = more neutral systems to contest. L.9.2 Fixed Anchor & Faction Homeworlds ANCHOR ID POSITION RULES The Nexus sys-nexus (510, 320) fixed Win condition target. Guardian fleet: Gunship×2 + Dreadnought. Connected by 2–3 lanes. FACTION HOMEWORLDS (Procedurally Placed) FACTION HOMEWORLD PLACEMENT RULE TOGGLEABLE Terran League Procedural name Outer rim, spread from other homeworlds NO Eternal Shards Procedural name Outer/mid rim, spread from other homeworlds YES Scrap-Horde Procedural name Outer rim, spread from other homeworlds YES The Revenant Procedural name Mid/inner rim, spread from other homeworlds YES Unity Accord Procedural name Outer/mid rim, spread from other homeworlds YES Custom Difficulty: Player can toggle AI factions on/off before galaxy generation. Minimum 2 factions (player + 1 AI). Fewer factions = more neutral systems. Vorax (NPC) and Core Guardians (NPC) always present regardless of toggle. Homeworld Rules: Each homeworld is a normal procedural system — no fixed coordinates. Must be ≥3 lane-hops apart from each other. No homeworld can have a direct lane to the Nexus. Each homeworld gets a guaranteed Capital World planet. Faction Elimination: A faction is destroyed when it loses ALL controlled planets AND has no fleets in transit. If a fleet is still moving, the faction survives. L.9.3 Generation Pipeline (13 Steps) # STEP SPECIFICATION 1 Seed Initialization Accept seed (user-provided or time-based). Init seeded PRNG (mulberry32). All randomness flows through this. 2 System Count Pick random count within size range (S:12–14, M:16–20, L:22–26, X:28–35). Fewer active factions = more neutral systems (count stays the same). 3 Place Nexus + Homeworlds Nexus at (510, 320). Place N faction homeworlds (N = active factions, 2–5) via spaced radial placement: ~360°/N apart ±jitter, radius 0.65–0.90 × galaxy_radius. Each gets a guaranteed Capital World. Enforce ≥3 lane-hop separation. 4 Generate Systems Place remaining (total − 1 − N) systems via jittered radial placement on 1024×640 space. Min distance 80px. Inner ring (0.2–0.4r): White Dwarf/Blue bias. Mid (0.4–0.7r): Yellow/Binary. Outer (0.7–1.0r): Red Giant/any. 5 Assign Star Types 5 types: Yellow Main Seq, Red Giant, Blue Supergiant, Binary, White Dwarf. Distribution biased by ring. Fisher-Yates shuffle of type pool. 6 Generate Planets Count by star type (Yellow:5-8, Red:6-10, Blue:4-7, Binary:3-6, WD:2-5). Planet type weighted by star. Each gets: type, territory count, base yield, traits. 7 Generate Warp Lanes For each system: connect to 2 nearest, 50% chance for 3rd. Dedupe edges. Ensure 1–3 chokepoints (prune longest edges). 7b Roll Orbital Features For each planet, filter ORBITAL_FEATURES by valid planet types, weighted pick. Keys: asteroid_belt, ring_system, debris_field, none. 8 Post-Lane Pruning Remove direct Homeworld↔Nexus lane for ALL factions. BFS flood fill. If disconnected subgraph found, add minimum bridge lane. Verify all homeworlds ≥3 lane-hops apart (re-add/remove lanes if needed). 9 Place Wormholes 1–2 pairs connecting non-adjacent systems (>60% galaxy diameter apart). Collapse chance: 5–40% per use, fixed per seed. 10 Faction Placement N homeworlds already placed in Step 3. Nexus = Guardians. Vorax at 1–2 edge systems (not homeworlds). Remaining systems = neutral/unoccupied. Elimination: all planets lost + no fleets in transit = destroyed. 11 Assign Planet Traits Roll from Ch13 tables: Resource (Rich Veins 20% Volcanic, Flux 15% Ocean), Combat (Fortified 15% Capital, Low-G 100% Moon), Strategic (Precursor 30% Ruins, Guardian Echo 15% Ruins/WD). Max 1 per category per planet. 12 Territory Adjacency Per planet: build connected territory graph (DoW Soulstorm style). Ring backbone 1→2→…→N→1 guarantees all reachable. Add 1–3 seeded cross-links for strategic shortcuts. Max 5 connections per territory. Store as adjacency dict { 1: [2, N], … }. 12b Roll System Phenomena Per-system weighted pick. nebula, ion_storm, gravity_well, radiation_field, solar_flare_zone, none. Homeworlds always none. Nexus always gravity_well. 13 Output Return { seed, size, systems[], lanes[], wormholes[], homeworlds{} }. Each system: { id, name, position, star_type, planets[], faction, is_homeworld, lanes[] }. Each planet: { type, territory_count, territory_adjacency{}, base_yield, traits[] }. L.9.4 Seeded RNG Requirement Same seed MUST produce identical galaxy across all platforms. Implementation: mulberry32 seeded PRNG. All random calls routed through it. Fisher-Yates shuffle for all randomized array selections. Godot's built-in randi() not used — not guaranteed deterministic cross-platform. L.9.5 Visual Style Reference (3 Zoom Levels) Helldivers 2 + Dawn of War: Soulstorm BLENDED — the galaxy map is NOT split into separate styles per zoom level. Instead, Zoom Level 1 (Galaxy View) blends BOTH: the HD2 radial layout with faction territory fog AND the DoW Soulstorm visual richness with detailed planet spheres, central stars, orbital rings, and fleet icons. Planets look like real celestial bodies inside faction-colored territory zones connected by supply lines. What NOT to do: No 3D rotatable globe — all views top-down 2D. No photorealistic rendering — stylized sci-fi with glows and clean shapes. No cluttered UI — information density managed through zoom levels. LEVEL VIEW INTERACTION REFERENCE 1 — Galaxy Full radial map, detailed planet spheres, faction territory fog, warp lane supply lines Click system → zoom to system view. Scroll wheel zooms. HD2 + DoW Soulstorm blended 2 — System Central star with glow, orbiting planets as spheres, orbital paths, fleet icons Click planet → zoom to territory view. Warp lane exits at edges. DoW Soulstorm system map 3 — Territory Planet surface divided into numbered regions, faction-colored control, attack arrows Click territory → deployment screen → ground battle. DoW Soulstorm territory map FACTION COLORS ■ Terran #00b4ff ■ Shards #00ffcc ■ Horde #ff8800 ■ Revenant #44ff66 ■ Accord #ffaa22 ■ Vorax #ff2266 ■ Guardians #ffaa22 L.9.6 Territory Count Reference (from Ch13) PLANET TYPE TERRITORIES NOTES Capital World 18 – 22 Multi-session campaign per planet Terrestrial 10 – 14 Standard balanced Volcanic 8 – 12 Hostile, resource-rich Gas Giant 6 – 8 Orbital platforms only Ice World 8 – 10 Slow movement Desert 8 – 12 Open terrain, long sightlines Ocean / Archipelago 6 – 10 Island hopping Jungle 10 – 14 Dense vegetation, infantry advantage Ancient Ruins 8 – 12 Highest lore fragment chance Moon 3 – 5 Quick skirmish, low gravity guaranteed Orbital Station 1 – 3 Tiny, strategic chokepoint Dead World 4 – 8 Stripped, minimal yield L.10 — Living Universe: Orbital Mechanics & Galactic Phenomena The galaxy is not a static game board. Every celestial body moves. Storms drift. Vorax tendrils creep. Players who study the rhythms gain a strategic edge. GAMEPLAY WEIGHT: MODERATE — meaningful timing for big campaigns, not punishing for casual play. L.10.1 — ORBITAL CYCLE SYSTEM ORBITAL SLOT SPEED (°/CY) FULL ORBIT NOTES Slot 1 (innermost) 30° 12 cycles Fast, near the star Slot 2 20° 18 cycles Slot 3 14° ~26 cycles Slot 4 10° 36 cycles Slot 5 7° ~51 cycles Slot 6 5° 72 cycles Slot 7+ (outermost) 3° 120 cycles Slow, far from star System drift: 0.5°/cycle (720 cycles for full galactic orbit). Warp lane distances recalculate ±5% per cycle. L.10.2 — TRAVEL TIME ORBITAL IMPACT INTRA-SYSTEM Base 1 cycle. −0.5 if planets within 60° (same side), +0.5 if >180° apart (opposite sides). Net: 0.5–1.5 cycles. INTER-SYSTEM current_distance / base_distance modifier ( 0.95–1.05 ). Displayed as Optimal / Standard / Extended. LAUNCH WINDOWS Open when two systems at closest approach. 15% travel discount , lasts 2–3 cycles, occurs every 30–50 cycles per lane. Shown as green pulse on warp lane. L.10.3 — BATTLE CONDITION ORBITAL MODIFIERS ORBITAL POSITION EFFECT MODIFIER Perihelion (closest) Intense heat/radiation +10% energy dmg, −5% bio HP regen Aphelion (farthest) Cold/dark +10% stealth, −5% move speed Mid-orbit Standard No modifier L.10.4 — CELESTIAL HAZARDS PULSAR BEAMS Rotating energy beams sweep nearby systems every 8–12 cycles . Fleets caught lose ALL shields for 2 cycles. Systems near pulsars +30% scrap yield . Visible as rotating lighthouse beam. NEUTRON STAR GRAVITY WELLS Fleet sublight speed HALVED . +50% scrap yield. Retreat from combat +2 cycles. Natural chokepoints. SOLAR FLARES Every 10–20 cycles by star type (Red:10, Yellow:15, Blue:20). Orbital structures disabled 1–2 cycles, unshielded fleets 5% hull dmg/cycle , ground battles +10% energy weapon damage. Predictable countdown. L.10.5 — MOVING GALACTIC HAZARDS PHENOMENON COUNT SPEED DURATION GALAXY EFFECT SYSTEM / BATTLE Asteroid Fields 2–4 ~2–3 u/cy Persistent Fleet: 10% hull dmg, +1 cy travel −10% building HP, +20% scrap Ion Storms 1–3 ~3–5 u/cy 15–30 cy Lane disrupted 1–3 cycles Tech/equipment disabled, −20% sensors Nebula Clouds 2–3 ~1 u/cy Persistent Sensor range ZERO (fog of war) +10% stealth in ground battles Void Rifts 0–1 Expand 1u/5cy Until sealed Systems PHASED OUT (not destroyed) Buildings 50% HP, pop −30% on return Comets 1–2 ~10+ u/cy 15–20 cy Cosmetic trail +5% research speed 2 cycles L.10.6 — COSMIC OPPORTUNITIES ROGUE PLANETS 1–2 per galaxy, drift ~1–2 u/cycle. Temporarily capturable for 5–8 cycles when near a system. Random type/traits. First to claim gets a bonus planet. DEBRIS FIELDS Dynamic — generated after major space battles. Persist 10–15 cycles . Salvageable for scrap + rare equipment. Ambush cover (first-strike bonus). Diminishing returns. ANTIMATTER FOUNTAINS 1–2 per galaxy, stationary. Fleet parked 2 cycles = full shield + HP recharge . Strategically valuable control points. L.10.7 — STAR SYSTEM PHENOMENA TIDAL LOCKING Inner planets around red giants/white dwarfs. Day side: +15% energy dmg, −10% bio regen . Night side: +15% kinetic dmg, −10% movement. Twilight zone: no modifiers. Visible per territory. PLANETARY RING MINING Gas giants with rings: +3 scrap/cycle for 20–30 cycles, then depleted. Rings thin visually when depleted. BINARY STAR ECLIPSES Every 15–25 cycles, lasting 2–3 cycles. All planets get night-side conditions ( −10% ranged accuracy, +15% stealth ). Predictable cycle in system info. L.10.8 — FACTION-SPECIFIC PHENOMENA FACTION PHENOMENON EFFECT VISUAL Eternal Shards Resonance Nodes (2–3) +10% ability effectiveness within 1 system Teal crystalline asteroids pulsing The Revenant Tomb Beacons (3–5) Free unit every 5 cycles when Revenant controls system Faint green pulses Scrap-Horde Scrap Trails (dynamic) Enemy +10% travel time on Horde lanes, Horde −10% Visible debris on lanes Vorax Spore Clouds (drift) Starts corruption if reaches undefended system Faint red haze drifting Vorax Tendril Spread (lanes) 1 lane/3 cycles, cuttable, isolated Vorax weaken Red creep + pulse on lanes L.10.9 — MYSTERY PHENOMENA DIMENSIONAL ECHOES 1–2 per campaign, random. Ghost fleet appears 2–3 cycles. Study with science fleet: +15% research 3 cycles, 20% precursor blueprint chance . Visual: ghostly translucent ships mid-battle. L.11 — Ground Battle Visual Direction (StarCraft 2 + Dawn of War 2 Target) L.11.1 — VISUAL REFERENCES STARCRAFT 2 Isometric camera, detailed terrain tiles, buildings with faction identity, selection circles, HP bars, minimap, resource HUD, production queue, unit info panel. DAWN OF WAR 2 Isometric camera, destructible cover, squad-based units taking cover, heavy terrain interaction, directional combat with flanking. L.11.2 — CAMERA • Isometric ~45° top-down, fixed angle (no rotation) • Zoom levels: close for detail, far for battlefield overview • Auto-follow action + manual pan + minimap (bottom-left) L.11.3 — TERRAIN REQUIREMENTS FEATURE DESCRIPTION Terrain tiles Biome-appropriate ground textures (11 palettes) Elevation 3 tiers (from GDD Ch19) — raised platforms, hills, ridges Cover objects 5 destructible types (from GDD Ch19) — walls, sandbags, rocks, wrecks, barricades LOS blockers Buildings, terrain features blocking ranged attacks Environmental hazards Lava pools, ice patches, Vorax creep Map borders Natural boundaries (cliffs, water, void) L.11.4 — MAP SIZES TERRITORY SIZE GRID EXAMPLES Small (Moon, Station) 32×32 tiles Quick skirmish Medium (most types) 48×48 tiles Standard battles Large (Capital, Jungle) 64×64 tiles Extended campaigns L.11.5 — HUD LAYOUT (REF CH40) • Top bar: Timer, unit counts, battle phase • Bottom-left: Minimap • Bottom-right: Unit info panel, commander abilities • Top-right: Speed controls, reserve status • Center: Event notifications — reinforcements, kill feed, rank-up L.11.6 — VFX CHECKLIST VFX TYPE DESCRIPTION Projectiles Visible travel from source to target Muzzle flash Weapon-specific flash at firing point Impact effects Sparks/dust/splatter by armor type Explosions AoE blast with shockwave Shield shimmer Shards faction — energy shield ripple on hit Phase blink Shards teleport — shimmer-in/out effect Creep spread Vorax organic corruption expanding on terrain Volatile death Guardian chain explosion — cascading AoE L.11.7 — CURRENT STATE VS TARGET CURRENT STATE (PROTOTYPE) TARGET (SC2 / DOW2 QUALITY) Flat arena, no terrain Terrain tiles, elevation, cover 5 Terran ground models 100+ models (all factions) Basic HP bars Full HUD: minimap, unit panel, abilities Damage numbers only Projectiles, VFX, animations, sound Movement + attack + reserves Pathfinding, cover, flanking, abilities No minimap or speed controls Full battle HUD with all controls L.12 — End-of-Campaign Summary / Final Cutscene (Planned Feature) “The simulation was watching the entire time. After the final battle, the player’s entire career plays out before them.” L.12.1 — SUMMARY COMPONENTS COMPONENT DESCRIPTION Career Stats Battles won/lost, units killed, factions eliminated, cycles survived, planets conquered, resources gathered Pivotal Battle Replays System auto-tags top 3–5 moments (closest victories, biggest losses, first faction kill, final Nexus assault). Replays as cinematic clips. Alignment Timeline Visual timeline showing how decisions shaped the galaxy (diplomatic vs aggressive, alliances/betrayals) Generals Remembered Named commanders who survived honored; fallen commanders memorialized with kill counts and death circumstances Galaxy State Timelapse Territory map animates cycle-by-cycle from start to end, showing faction expansion/contraction L.12.2 — TRIGGER RULES VICTORY (NEXUS CONQUERED) Full summary — all 5 components, cinematic presentation, part of ending cutscene. DEFEAT (FACTION ELIMINATED) Abbreviated — career stats + generals remembered + galaxy timelapse (showing territory shrinking). No battle replays. Tone shifts from triumphant to somber. L.12.3 — PREREQUISITES Requires battle event logging system running throughout the campaign. Tag pivotal moments as they happen. Track territory ownership per cycle, commander lifetime stats, alignment decision log. L.13 — DeploymentScene Architecture (Godot 4.4 Rewrite) “Pre-battle force allocation — choose your initial wave and reserves before the fight begins.” L.13.1 — SCENE TRANSITION PATTERN Static var pending_battle_config — The DeploymentScene writes a static pending_battle_config dictionary before transitioning to BattleManager. BattleManager reads this config in _ready() and clears it after consumption. This avoids scene-to-scene parameter passing through globals. Flow: MapScene → DeploymentScene (player allocates forces) → BattleManager (reads pending_battle_config) → Battle begins. L.13.2 — THREE-COLUMN LAYOUT AVAILABLE INITIAL WAVE RESERVES All player units eligible for this battle Units deployed at battle start Units held back, deployed later via reserve mechanic L.13.3 — CLICK FLOW ACTION RESULT Click unit in Available Moves to Initial Wave Click unit in Initial Wave Moves to Reserves Click unit in Reserves Returns to Available L.13.4 — ENEMY TEAM GENERATION Auto-generated enemy team mirrors the player’s composition. The enemy faction’s unit pool is filtered to match the player’s unit count and approximate tier distribution. This ensures balanced encounters without manual enemy roster design. L.13.5 — CONFIG OUTPUT FORMAT Output dictionary is compatible with BattleManager.start_battle() . Contains: • player_initial: Array[UnitData] • player_reserves: Array[UnitData] • enemy_units: Array[UnitData] • battle_type: String • system_id: int L.14 — Galaxy Map Visual Layer (Godot 4.4 Rewrite) “Animated parallax starfield with nebula clouds — cosmetic layer that never touches galaxy generation.” L.14.1 — 3-LAYER PARALLAX STARFIELD LAYER COUNT BEHAVIOUR Far (back) 200 Smallest dots, slowest parallax scroll, subtle twinkle Mid 80 Medium dots, moderate parallax, moderate twinkle Near (front) 30 Largest dots, fastest parallax, strongest twinkle L.14.2 — STAR TWINKLING Sine wave modulation per star. Each star has a random phase offset and speed. Alpha oscillates between base_alpha × 0.5 and base_alpha × 1.0. Applied per-frame in _process(delta) . L.14.3 — NEBULA CLOUD PATCHES 2–4 soft nebula patches placed randomly across the map. Each patch is rendered as concentric circles with low alpha (0.01–0.03). Colors drawn from faction palette. Purely cosmetic — no gameplay effect. L.14.4 — SEPARATE RNG Visual layer uses a dedicated RNG with seed 42 . This is completely separate from the galaxy generation RNG. Ensures that adding/removing visual elements never changes the procedurally generated galaxy layout. L.14.5 — 11-LAYER DRAW PIPELINE (CC-011 OVERHAUL) # LAYER DESCRIPTION 1 Background Stars 3-layer parallax starfield with twinkle 2 Nebula Clouds Soft faction-colored patches with low alpha 3 Galactic Core Glow Concentric warm gradient centered on Nexus 4 Faction Influence Zones Soft colored halos around faction-owned systems 5 Warp Lanes Base lane lines connecting systems 6 Lane Pulse Animation Energy dots traveling along warp lanes 7 Wormhole Portals Spinning purple spirals at wormhole endpoints 8 System Nodes Star-type colored fill, faction ring, planet-count sizing 9 Homeworld Beacons Pulsing rings + compass tick marks 10 System Labels System name text below each node 11 Selection Highlight Click feedback ring on selected system L.14.6 — GALACTIC CORE GLOW Concentric warm gradient centered on the Nexus system (512, 320). Multiple circles radiate outward with decreasing alpha, blending from warm gold/amber at the center to transparent at the edges. Creates a visual “galactic core” focal point that draws the eye to the Nexus objective. L.14.7 — SPIRAL ARM STAR CLUSTERING Logarithmic spiral density applied to background star placement. Stars cluster along spiral arm paths radiating from the galactic center, creating a more natural galaxy appearance. Uses the visual RNG (seed 42) — does not affect game system placement. L.14.8 — STAR-TYPE NODE COLORS Each system node’s fill color reflects its star type (yellow for yellow_main_seq, red for red_giant, blue for blue_supergiant, white for white_dwarf, dual-tone for binary). An outer ring color indicates faction ownership . Unowned systems show a neutral grey ring. L.14.9 — LANE PULSE ANIMATION Energy dots travel along warp lanes at a steady pace. Multiple dots per lane, evenly spaced, animated each frame via _process(delta) . Dot positions wrap at lane endpoints. Gives the galaxy a living, connected feel without impacting gameplay logic. L.14.10 — WORMHOLE PORTALS Spinning purple spirals rendered at each wormhole endpoint. Drawn as arcs that rotate over time, creating a swirling portal effect. Both ends of a wormhole pair display matching portals. Wormhole connections are drawn as dashed lines between paired systems. L.14.11 — HOMEWORLD BEACONS Pulsing rings + compass tick marks surround each faction’s homeworld system. The ring expands and contracts on a sine cycle, drawing attention to the faction’s capital. Four compass ticks (N/S/E/W) extend outward from the ring. Colored in the faction’s primary color. L.14.12 — SYSTEM SIZE SCALING System node radius scales by planet count . Systems with more planets render as larger circles on the galaxy map, providing an at-a-glance indication of system value. Homeworlds receive an additional size bonus to ensure they are always prominent. L.14.13 — FACTION INFLUENCE ZONES Soft colored halos drawn around faction-owned systems. Each faction’s influence zone uses that faction’s primary color at very low alpha, creating a territorial “fog” that visually groups owned systems. Overlapping zones blend naturally. Drawn on layer 4, below lanes and nodes. L.15 — Turn Phase Pipeline Architecture (Godot 4.4 Rewrite) “Full 6-phase turn cycle with adaptive phase skipping and conflict-driven resolution.” L.15.1 — 6-PHASE CYCLE # PHASE AUTO-DELAY DESCRIPTION 1 MAP — Player input phase: issue fleet orders, manage units, review territory 2 ARMADA 0.5s Execute fleet moves, run conflict detection (multi-faction fleet presence) 3 APPROACH skippable Pre-battle fleet positioning (skipped if no conflicts) 4 SPACE 0.3s Space combat resolution (skipped if no conflicts) 5 GROUND 0.3s Ground combat resolution (skipped if no conflicts) 6 RESOLUTION — Collect resources via supply chain, apply combat results, advance turn counter L.15.2 — ADAPTIVE PHASE SKIPPING The pipeline skips APPROACH, SPACE, and GROUND phases when no conflicts are detected during the ARMADA phase. Conflict detection checks for multi-faction fleet presence at any system. If no conflicts exist, the pipeline jumps directly from ARMADA to RESOLUTION, keeping peaceful turns fast. L.15.3 — MVP CONFLICT RESOLUTION In the MVP implementation, conflict resolution is simplified: the attacker captures one territory per conflict . Full space and ground battle scenes will be wired in later phases. This allows the turn pipeline to function end-to-end while battle mechanics are developed independently. L.15.4 — RESOURCE COLLECTION (RESOLUTION PHASE) During RESOLUTION, each faction collects yields from all owned territories . Yields are modified by the supply chain hop distance from homeworld (see §15.9). Credits, scrap, influence, and intel are accumulated per-territory and added to faction totals. The turn counter then advances and the pipeline returns to MAP. L.15.5 — END TURN BUTTON STATES PHASE BUTTON TEXT BEHAVIOUR MAP End Turn Clickable — commits player orders and starts phase execution ARMADA Executing… Disabled — fleet moves processing SPACE / GROUND Engage Disabled — combat resolution in progress RESOLUTION Resolving… Disabled — resource collection and turn wrap-up L.16 — Input Controls (Godot 4.4 Rewrite) “Unified input scheme across all three map views — galaxy, system, and territory.” L.16.1 — CAMERA CONTROLS INPUT ACTION WASD / Arrow Keys Camera pan on all map views Mouse Wheel Zoom in / out Right Click Drag Pan camera (all 3 map views) Middle Click Drag Pan camera (all 3 map views) L.16.2 — SELECTION & NAVIGATION INPUT ACTION Left Click Select system / planet / territory Double Click Drill into system (galaxy view) or territory (system view) Right Click (move mode) Cancel fleet / unit move command Escape Back to previous view / cancel move mode
